Asteroid - small, rocky objects orbiting the Sun

question_text: The _______ radiation belts are giant swaths of magnetically trapped highly energetic charged particles that surround Earth. [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]
solution:
(A) Van Allen
Van Allen belts are two radiation belts of charged particles located in Earth's magnetic field.
Location - Inner belt - 3,000 km above Earth; outer belt - 15,000-20,000 km above Earth.
These belts are dangerous for humans and spacecrafts and the energetic electrons in them are called "killer" electrons as they can damage spacecrafts.
Aurora - It is a display of light in the sky at the poles.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements about some planets in the solar system:
1. Mercury has no atmosphere.
2. Venus has two moons.
3. There is no land on Jupiter.
4. Rings of Saturn are composed of lumps of ice and dust.
Which of the statements given above are correct? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(D) 1, 3 and 4
Mercury doesn't have any atmosphere because of its small size, weak gravity and its proximity to the Sun. Venus doesn't have any moon.
Jupiter being a jovian planet is made up of gases, hence lacking any solid surface.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In Sun-earth system, the Sun, the Earth and Lagrange point L4 form: [CDS II 2024]
solution:
(B) An equilateral triangle
Lagrange point - a point in space where the gravitational pull of two big masses equals the centripetal force of a small mass. Total 5 points - L1, L2, L3, L4, L5
L1, L2, L3 - Unstable
L4, L5 - Stable, located at same distance from both Sun and Earth.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In Sun-Earth system, the distance between Lagrange points L2 and L3 is about: [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(D) 32 crore kilometres
Do you know that the James Webb space telescope is placed in the L2 lagrange point?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the solstices, an event that occurs when the Sun appears to reach most northerly or southerly, is correct? [CAPF 2024]

options.a: The winter solstice takes place on June 21 in both the northern and southern hemispheres.
options.b: The winter solstice takes place on December 21 in both the northern and southern hemispheres.
options.c: The summer solstice occurs in northern hemisphere on June 21 and on December 21 in southern hemisphere.
options.d: The summer solstice occurs in northern hemisphere on December 21 and on June 21 in southern hemisphere.

solution:
(C) The summer solstice occurs in northern hemisphere on June 21 and on December 21 in southern hemisphere.
Summer solstice (21 June) - rays of Sun fall vertically on Tropic of Cancer (23.5° N) - summer in NH, winter in SH
Winter solstice (22 Dec) - rays of Sun fall vertically on Tropic of Capricorn (23.5° S) - winter in NH, summer in SH

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements with reference to the Nebular Hypothesis for the origin of the Solar System is correct? [CAPF 2024]

options.a: The initiation of the nebular hypothesis can be considered as a diffuse, roughly spherical, slowly rotating expanding nebula.
options.b: As a result of rotation and expansion, a disk is formed with matter concentrated in the centre.
options.c: Expansion continues with the formation of the proto-Sun and the rings of material are left behind.
options.d: The material in the rings get condensed and form the planets that revolve in orbits around the Sun.

solution: (D) The material in the rings get condensed and form the planets that revolve in orbits around the Sun.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following astronomers proved that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(A) Copernicus
Copernicus gave Heliocentric model of universe meaning that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun.
Do you know that before Copernicus, Ptolemy gave Geocentric model which said that Earth is at the centre of the universe and Sun, moon revolve around it?
Laws of Kepler - (1) planets move in elliptical orbits with the Sun as a focus, (2) a planet covers the same area of space in the same amount of time, and (3) a planet's orbital period is proportional to the size of its semi-major axis.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of arrangement of the given planets in descending order of their density (in gm/cm³)? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(C) Earth > Venus > Jupiter > Saturn
Planets as per their density (lowest to highest) - Saturn < Uranus < Jupiter < Neptune < Mars < Venus < Mercury < Earth.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following are called 'terrestrial planets'? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(C) Earth, Mercury, Mars, Venus
Terrestrial planets - Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars
Jovian planets - Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Geoid is the _______ of the Earth. [SSC CHSL 01/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Shape
The shape of Earth is Geoid/Oblate spheroid (flat from top and bottom and bulges at centre)
Reason for geoid shape - more centrifugal force at equator due to rotation due to rotation.
2 types of forces act on a revolving body - Centripetal force (towards the centre) and Centrifugal force (away from centre)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following statements is INCORRECT regarding the Coriolis force? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]

options.a: It deflects the wind to the right in the north and left in the south.
options.b: It is absent at the equator.
options.c: Deflection is less when the wind is high.
options.d: It is directly proportional to the angle of altitude.

solution:
(C) Deflection is less when the wind is high
Coriolis force is the apparent force experienced due to rotation of Earth. It deflects the wind to the right in the NH and to the left in the SH. It is max at poles and absent at equator. It is directly proportional to the speed of wind.
Geostrophic winds - winds which blow parallel to isobars due to balance of Coriolis force and pressure gradient

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which is the Standard Meridian of India? [SSC CHSL 05/07/2024]
solution:
(B) 82°30'E
Meridian (longitude) is the imaginary vertical lines running from North to South. It is the angular distance of a place from prime meridian. Distance between meridians - max at equator (111.32 km) and least at poles. Total meridians = 360.
Important meridians - Prime meridian/GMT (0°) - passes through Greenwich, London; International Date Line (180°)
GMT passes through 8 countries - UK, France, Spain, Algeria, Mali, Burkina Faso, Togo, Ghana {TRICK - BSF GAMe in TOGO Kingdom}.
Moving from W to E on IDL - day gained and date lost.
Do you know that all longitudes are great circles (largest circle which can be drawn on a sphere dividing it into two equal halves) but only 1 latitude (equator) is a great circle?
Tropic of Cancer and Indian Standard Meridian intersect each other at Koriya district in Chhattisgarh.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statement/statements is/are true regarding the light year? [SSC CHSL 27/06/2024]
1. Light year is a unit of distance.
2. Light year is a unit of time.
3. A light year is the distance that light travels in one Earth year.
4. Light year is the measurement of light intensity.
solution:
(A) Only 1 and 3 are correct
Light year (9.46 x 10^12 km) is the distance travelled by light in 1 Earth year. Hence, it is a unit of distance.
1 Parsec = 3.26 light years.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Perihelion is when Earth is _______. [SSC PHASE XII 24/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Nearest to the Sun
Perihelion (3 January) - Earth is nearest to the Sun (147.5 mn km)
Aphelion (4 July) - Earth is farthest from the Sun (152.5 mn km)
Distance between Sun and Earth (avg) - 150 mn km
Perigee - moon closest to Earth Apogee - moon farthest from Earth

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: What makes countries near the equator hotter than those which are are away from the equator? [SSC JE 06/02/2024]

options.a: The equator is near the earth's core.
options.b: The equator is a hot line.
options.c: Areas near the equator have more volcanoes.
options.d: Areas near the equator receive direct and more sunlight.

solution:
(D) Areas near the equator receive direct and more sunlight.
Equator (0° latitude) divides the Earth in two equal halves - NH and SH.
Latitudes - imaginary horizontal lines running East to West. It is the angular distance of a place from equator.
1°. latitude = 111 km. Total latitudes = 181. Distance between each latitude is same.
Important latitudes - 0° - Equator, 23.5° N - Tropic of Cancer, 23.5° S - Tropic of Capricorn, 66.5° N - Arctic circle, 66.5° S - Antarctic circle
Do you know that the direct sunlight only falls between 23.5° N and 23.5° S. Beyond these, only slant rays fall?
The temperature in subtropical regions is more than that at equator due to factors like less cloudiness in subtropics and absence of large water bodies.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: What is the radius of the Earth? [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
solution:
(B) 6371 km
Equatorial radius = 6378 km | Equatorial circumference = 40,075 km
Polar radius = 6357 km | Polar circumference = 40,007 km
Mean radius = 6371 km | Mean circumference = 40,040 km
Equatorial speed > Polar speed, Equatorial radii > Polar radii, Equatorial circumference > Polar circumference

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which among the following is not a type of solar eclipse? [SSC CHSL 15/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Penumbral
Solar Eclipse - moon is between Sun and Earth and blocks the sunlight. Occurs during New moon (amavasya).
It is of 3 types - total, annular, partial
Lunar Eclipse - earth is between Sun and moon and blocks the sunlight. Occurs during Full moon (purnima)
Blue moon is when there are 2 purnima in one month
Lunar eclipse + perigee = bigger moon (super moon)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding the moon is correct? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
I. The size of the illuminated part of the moon visible from the Earth increases each day after the new moon day.
II. After the full moon day, the sunlit part of the moon visible from the Earth decreases in size every day.
solution:
(A) Both I and II
Full moon (purnima) - moon appears as a full circle
New moon (amavasya) - moon is invisible
After purnima, illuminated part of moon decreases (waning phase) each day and in nearly 15 days it becomes invisible thereby causing amavasya.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: During the phenomenon of aphelion, the approximate distance between the Earth and the Sun is: [SSC CHSL 12/10/2020]
solution:
(B) 152 mn km
Perihelion (3 January) - Earth is nearest to the Sun (147.5 mn km)
Aphelion (4 July) - Earth is farthest from the Sun (152.5 mn km)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: How many degrees does the Earth rotate about its own axis in one hour? [SSC CGL 06/03/2020]
solution:
(D) 15
Each hour, Earth rotates about 15° and hence in 24 hours it completes one rotation (24 x 15° = 360°).
Moving 15° East from prime meridian - time increases by 1 hour
Moving 15° West from prime meridian - time decreases by 1 hour
Rotation causes day and night while revolution and tilt cause change in seasons.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following sets of statements are correct about the Inter-Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ)? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
I. The ITCZ is a broad trough of low pressure in equatorial latitudes.
II. This is where the northeast and the southeast trade winds converge.
III. The ITCZ is the equatorial trough normally positioned about 5°N of the equator and remains there throughout the year.
IV. The ITCZ moves over to the plane of Ganga in Summer.
solution:
(D) I, II and IV
ITCZ (doldrum) is a low pressure belt between 10° N and 10° S. It is a zone of convergence of airmass. It moves north and south with the apparent shift of Sun.
Cell - representation of general circulation of atmosphere. 3 cells - Hadley cell, Ferrel cell, Polar cell

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: During October-November, the apparent movement of the sun is towards the _______. [SSC MTS 18/05/2023]
solution:
(D) South
During October-November, the apparent movement of the Sun is towards the South because during this time, Earth is tilted away from the Sun in NH and towards the Sun in SH.
Earth is tilted at 23.5° on its axis and 66.5° on its orbital plane.
Summer solstice - 21 June, Winter solstice - 22 December

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: On which of the following two dates does the equinox fall? [SSC CHSL 15/03/2023]
solution:
(C) 21 March and 23 September
Equinox - sunlight fall vertically on equator, so equal day and night. Vernal/Spring equinox - 21 march, Autumnal/fall equinox - 23 september. Recall about summer and winter solstice.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: What is the zone between the Arctic Circle and North Pole called? [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Frigid zone
Frigid zone - 66.5° N - 90° N and 66.5° S - 90° S
Torrid zone - 23.5° N - 23.5° S
Temperate zone - 23.5° N - 66.5° N and 23.5° S - 66.5° S
Recall all the important latitudes.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: 1° latitude is equal to approximately _______ km. [SSC CHSL 16/10/2020]
solution:
(A) 111 km
1° latitude = 111 km

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Lines joining locations at equal travel time from a common center are called: [SSC CHSL 21/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Isochrones
Isochrones - line joining locations at equal travel time
Isohalines - line joining places of same salinity
Isobar - line joining places of same atmospheric pressure
Isobronts - line joining places where thunderstorms are in a specific phase at a specific time
Isohyets - line joining places with same amount of rainfall
Isonephs - line joining places with same amount of cloud cover
Isobaths - line joining places with same depth below water

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: All parallel circles from the Equator to the poles are known as _______. [SSC CHSL 26/10/2020]
solution:
(A) Parallels of Latitude
See solution 6

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which country has the most time zones in the world? [SSC MTS 13/10/2021]
solution:
(A) France
France has 13 time zones due to its overseas territories. Russia - 11 USA - 6 UK - 1
India has only 1 time zone i.e. 82°30'E

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following pressure belts is known as the horse latitude? [SSC CHSL 13/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Subtropical High Pressure
Pressure belts are of 4 types - Equatorial low pressure belt, Subtropical high pressure belt (30°N and 30°S), Subpolar low pressure belt (60°N and 60°S) and Polar high pressure belt (80-90°N and 80-90°S)
Do you know that major hot deserts in the NH are located between 20°-30°N because of subtropical high pressure (the subsiding air is warm and dry)?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: On which day of the year is the Earth farthest from the sun, at a position called aphelion? [SSC CHSL 19/10/2020]
solution:
(D) 4 July
Perihelion (3 January) - Earth is nearest to the Sun (147.5 mn km)
Aphelion (4 July) - Earth is farthest from the Sun (152.5 mn km)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: When does the entire earth experience equal days and nights? [SSC CGL 04/03/2020]
solution:
(D) Day of equinox
Equinox - sunlight fall vertically on equator, so equal day and night. Vernal/Spring equinox - 21 march, Autumnal/fall equinox - 23 september

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct? [CDS I 2024]
1. Angular velocity for all locations on the Earth's surface is the same while linear velocity varies.
2. Linear velocity is maximum at the equator and minimum at the poles.
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Angular velocity - how fast an object moves relative to a point (in Earth's case relative to its axis). It is same (24 hours) for all locations on Earth.
Linear velocity - rate at which an object moves along its path. It is max at equator and min at poles (due to bulge at the equator)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In order to find out the absolute location of a place on the map, which of the following will be required? [CAPF 2021]

options.c: Both latitude and longitude of the place
options.d: Neither latitude nor longitude of the place

solution:
(C) Both latitude and longitude of the place
We can easily find the absolute location of a place by the intersection point of latitude and longitude. So, both are required.
Recall all info about latitude and longitude.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements about maps are correct? [CAPF 2022]
1. Maps that maintain the true shapes of areas are known as conformal maps.
2. Maps are used to show spatial relationships.
3. Maps cannot show route from one place to another.
solution:
(C) 1 and 2 only
Conformal maps - these maps keep the shape same but may change the size. (Imagine drawing a small square on a balloon. If you blow up the balloon, the square gets bigger, but its corners still make 90° angles.) Maps can show route from one place to another. Such maps are called road maps.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements about 'great circle' is not correct? [CAPF 2022]

options.a: Every great circle divides the Earth into equal halves.
options.b: Every great circle is a circumference of the Earth.
options.c: Great circles mark the longest travel routes between locations on the Earth's surface.
options.d: Great circle is the largest circle that can be drawn around the Earth through two particular.

solution:
(C) Great circles mark the longest travel routes between locations on the Earth's surface.
Great circle is the largest circle which can be drawn on. It divides the Earth in two equal halves. It marks the shortest distance between two points along the sphere.
Do you know that equator is the only latitude which is a great circle whereas all longitudes are great circles?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements is correct about Spring tides? [CAPF 2023]

options.a: High tide is higher than average high tide and Low tide is higher than average low tide.
options.b: High tide is lower than average high tide and Low tide is higher than average low tide.
options.c: High tide is higher than average high tide and Low tide is lower than average low tide.
options.d: High tide is lower than average high tide and Low tide is lower than average low tide.

solution:
(C) High tide is higher than average high tide and Low tide is lower than average low tide.
Spring tide - creates the greatest difference in water levels between high and low tides.
Neap tide - creates the smallest difference in water levels between high and low tides. Occurs approx 2 times a month (during the first and third quarter moon phase)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following correctly explains the change in seasons on Earth? [CAPF 2023]

options.b: Rotation of the Earth on its own axis
options.c: Revolution of the Moon around the Sun
options.d: Interaction of the Earth with other planets

solution:
(A) Tilt of the Earth's axis
Change in season on Earth is caused by tilt of Earth's axis and revolution around the Sun while day and night are caused due to rotation of Earth on its axis.
Recall the axial and orbital tilt of Earth.
Which planet is called the rolling planet due to its tilt of 98°?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements regarding the rotation of the Earth:
1. Since the Earth rotates by 15° each hour, time zones normally differ by one hour.
2. At the International Date Line, the calendar day changes - advancing a day for eastward travel, dropping back a day for westward travel.
3. Daylight saving time advances the clock by one hour.
4. The Moon rotates and revolves about the Earth in the same direction that the Earth revolves around the Sun. [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(C) 1, 3 and 4
Earth rotates 360° in 24 hours. So, 1 hour = 15°. Hence, time zones normally differ by one hour.
At the IDL, day is dropped back for eastward travel and advanced for westward travel.
Daylight saving time - it is the practice of forwarding clock by 1 hour in summers and back again by 1 hour in fall to make the better use of daylight.
The moon also rotates and revolves around the Earth in the same direction that the Earth revolves around the Sun (Earth rotates from W to E)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: If it is 31st December, 2023 in New Zealand, at the same time what will be the date in the Hawaiian Islands and Alaska? [CAPF 2024]

options.c: 31 December, 2023 in Alaska and 01 January, 2024 Hawaiian Islands
options.d: 31 December, 2023 in Hawaiian Islands and 01 January, 2024 in Alaska

solution:
(B) Both have 30 December, 2023
New Zealand is located just west of IDL while Hawaiian Islands and Alaska are located to the east of IDL. On moving from west to east on IDL, day is dropped back.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Tropic of Capricorn passes through which one of the following countries? [CISF AC 2022]
solution:
(B) Botswana
Tropic of Capricorn (23.5° S) passes through 10 countries.
South America: Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Paraguay
Africa: Namibia, Botswana, South Africa, Mozambique, Madagascar
Australia: Western Australia, Northern Territory, Queensland
Do you know that Africa is the only continent through which 23.5° N, 0°, 23.5° S all three pass?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: 50° South Latitude passes through [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(C) Argentina
50° S latitude passes through Argentina.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements with respect to 'tides':
1. The tidal bulges on wide continental shelves have greater height.
2. When tides are channeled between islands, they are called tidal currents.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
The tidal bulge on wide continent shelves have greater height because water depth decreases abruptly. Hence, height increases to conserve the energy.
When the tide is channeled between islands or into bays and estuaries they are called tidal currents.
Tides are the periodic rise and fall of sea level, once or twice a day, mainly due to the attraction of the Sun and the moon.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: If it is 12:00 Noon in India, on which meridian will it be 7:00 am of the same day? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(A) 7.5° E Longitude
Since 15° = 1 hour, so 5 hours (12-7 = 5 hours) = 75°.
IST is 82.5° E so to go back 5 hours = 82.5° E - 75° = 7.5° E

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is the longest Latitude? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(C) 0° Latitude
Equator (0°) is the longest latitude while poles (90°) are the smallest latitudes.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is the longest parallel of latitude? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(D) Equator
Equator (0°) is the longest latitude while poles (90°) are the smallest latitudes.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following countries has the maximum time difference from Greenwich Mean Time (GMT)? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(D) Bhutan
Time difference from GMT increases as we move away from it. Bhutan is farthest from it among the options.
Moving West from GMT - time decreases
Moving East from GMT - time increases

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Inhabitants are unaware of the speed of rotation of the planet Earth because:
1. The angular velocity is constant for each place on the Earth's surface.
2. The atmosphere rotates with the Earth.
3. There are no nearby objects, either stationary or moving at a rate different from that of the Earth.
Which of the above is/are the correct explanation(s)? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Angular velocity is same for all the places on Earth.
All objects on Earth and atmosphere moves with Earth, so we are unaware of Earth's rotation.
1 rotation = 23 hours 56 mins 4 sec; Rotational speed is max at equator and min at poles.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements with respect to Global Positioning System (GPS) is not correct? [NDA I 2021]

options.a: It is based on network of satellites orbiting above the Earth.
options.b: It is based on the system of triangulation.
options.c: GPS receivers provide location in terms of latitude, longitude and altitude.
options.d: It provides information exclusively for military operations.

solution:
(D) It provides information exclusively for military operations.
GPS is a system of interconnected satellites used to provide location. It was developed for military operations but now is freely available for civilian use.
A system of triangulation is a surveying method where a network of interconnected triangles is established to determine the location of points by measuring angles between them.
NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation) - it is Indian navigation system similar to GPS.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: While watching the news, you hear about a place, of which you had never heard of before. You want to know more about the place and you want to locate it on the map. Which of the following is/are required for you to be able to locate the place on the map? [NDA I 2021]

options.c: Both longitude and latitude of the place
options.d: Latitude, longitude and altitude of the place

solution:
(C) Both longitude and latitude of the place
We can easily find the absolute location of a place by the intersection point of latitude and longitude. So, both are required.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
In a group discussion on shape and size of the Earth, three students stated the following points:
1. Student 1: The shape of the Earth is basically an oblate spheroid.
2. Student 2: The polar diameter of the Earth is more than the equatorial diameter.
3. Student 3: Bulge along the equatorial region is due to the revolution of the Earth.
Who among the above students is/are correct? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(A) Student 1 only
See solution of questions 1 and 7 (level 1)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The periodic rise and fall of ocean water in response to gravitational forces is called [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(C) Tides
See solution 11
Tides based on frequency - Semi diurnal tide - 2 high and 2 low tides each day; Diurnal tide - 1 high and 1 low tide each day; Mixed tide.
Tides based on height - Spring tide - greatest difference between high and low tide; Neap tide - smallest difference between high and low tide.
Tsunamis are giant waves caused by earthquakes or volcanic eruptions under the sea.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The Prime Meridian does not pass through which one of the following African countries? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(A) Morocco
See solution of question 3 (level 1)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following cities of the world would represent the greatest linear velocity of rotation of the Earth? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(A) Kampala, Uganda
Linear velocity is higher near equator due to larger radius at equator. Kampala is located near equator so it would represent the greatest linear velocity among the given options.
Recall about angular velocity.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: What will happen if you are flying east across the International Date Line? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(C) You will gain 24 hours
When we fly east across the International Date Line, we gain 24 hours.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: If it is 12 noon in New Delhi, what will be the time in London, UK? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(A) 6:30 A.M.
Difference between New Delhi and London is 82.5° which translates to 5.5 hours. Since London is to the west of New Delhi so time there will be behind. So 12 - 5.5 = 6:30 A.M.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following positions of Sun, Earth and Moon is/are suitable for Spring Tide?
1. SYZYGY Conjunction
2. SYZYGY Opposition
3. Quadrature [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(C) 1 and 2
Spring tide occurs when Sun, Earth and Moon are in straight line (SYZYGY condition).
Syzygy conjunction - Moon between Sun and Earth
Syzygy opposition - Earth between Sun and moon
Quadrature means when Sun, Earth and moon are at 90°. It is associated with Neap tides.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Distance between the longitudes becomes zero on the North Pole and South Pole.
2. Distance between the longitudes is maximum on the Equator.
3. Number of longitudes is more than the number of latitudes. [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
See solution of questions 3 and 6 (level 1)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
A geographical grid has:
1. Lines that run east to west around the globe to mark latitude.
2. Lines that run north to south from north pole to south pole to indicate longitude. [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Geographical grid is formed when latitudes and longitudes intersect each other forming a grid. It is used to locate a place on the map.
Recall about latitudes and longitudes.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements regarding the International Date Line:
1. It is roughly 180° meridian, which deviates slightly East and West to avoid land areas surrounded by the Atlantic Ocean.
2. The date to the East of this line is (24 hours) earlier than to the West. [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(B) 2 only
See solution 3
Statement 1 is partially correct. IDL moves East or West to avoid land areas surrounded by Pacific ocean and not the Atlantic ocean.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding neap tides is/are correct?
1. It occurs every 14-15 days, which coincides with the first and third quarter of the Moon.
2. This tide has a small tidal range because the gravitational forces of the Moon and the Sun are in quadrature. [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
See solution of questions 5 and 18 (level 2)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
In which among the following states in India, the Sun rays are never perpendicular?
1. Bihar
2. Chhattisgarh
3. Manipur
4. Rajasthan [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(B) 1 and 3
Sun rays are perpendicular between 23.5°N (Tropic of Cancer) and 23.5°S (Tropic of Capricorn). Bihar and Manipur lie above Tropic of Cancer, so they always experience slant rays.
Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 Indian states - Gujarat, Rajasthan, MP, Chattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura and Mizoram.
Do you know that Mahi is the only Indian river to cross Tropic of Cancer twice?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements with respect to tides:
1. The tidal bulges on wide continental shelves have greater height.
2. When tides are channeled between islands, they are called tidal currents. [CBI 2023]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
See solution of question 11 (level 2)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following deserts is not located around 20°-30° latitude North or South of the Equator? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(D) Gobi
Gobi desert is not a tropical desert. It is located on the border of China and Mongolia.
Locations of other deserts - Atacama desert - Northern Chile, Kalahari - South Africa, Great Victoria - Australia
3 major deserts of Australia - Sandy, Gibson, Victoria (TRICK - 3GB)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements about North-facing slopes and South-facing slopes in the Northern Hemi-sphere is not correct? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.a: North-facing slopes are snow covered while South-facing slopes are bare.
options.b: North-facing slopes get less intense radiation as compared to South-facing slopes.
options.c: Snow on North-facing slopes is found at a lower altitude than South-facing slopes.
options.d: Tree line characteristics on North facing slopes and South-facing slopes are similar.

solution:
(D) Tree line characteristics on North facing slopes and South-facing slopes are similar.
In NH, the northern slope of mountains receive less sunlight as compared to the southern slope, hence northern slope is snow covered. North facing sloped have lower tree line as compared to south facing slopes (tree line is the line on a mountain above which the trees don't grow).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: You are located at 40° N latitude and 90° W longitude. You travel due North 40°, then due East 60°. What are your new coordinates? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(C) 80°N and 30°W
Current location - 90°W. When we move 60° east then we are moving towards the prime meridian so our current position will be 90°W - 60°E = 30°W

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
The Prime Meridian (zero degree longitude) does not pass through which of the following country/countries?
1. England
2. France
3. Portugal
4. Scotland [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(C) 3 and 4
The prime meridian passes through UK, France, Spain, Algeria, Mali, Burkina Faso, Togo, Ghana.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In the northern hemisphere, the longest day of the year normally occurs in the: [IAS 2022]

options.a: First half of the month of June.
options.b: Second half of the month of June.
options.c: First half of the month of July.
options.d: Second half of the month of July.

solution:
(B) Second half of the month of June
In the NH, the longest day is on 21 June (summer solstice) while in the SH, the longest day is on 22 December (winter solstice). On equinox, day and night are equal.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
On June 21 every year, which of the following latitude(s) experience(s) a sunlight of more than 12 hours?
1. Equator
2. Tropic of Cancer
3. Tropic of Capricorn
4. Arctic Circle [IAS 2024]
solution:
(D) 2 and 4
On 21 June (summer solstice), Sun is vertically over Tropic of Cancer (23.5°N). Hence NH experiences the longest day which lasts over 12 hours. During this time, North Pole experiences daylight of 24 hours, a phenomena known as 'Midnight sun'.
Equator experiences the daylight of nearly 12 hours regardless of the position of the Sun.
Sun is vertically over Tropic of Capricorn (23.5°S) on 22 December (winter solstice).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: If it is 10:00 a.m. at Greenwich Mean Time (GMT), then what will be the time at 90° E longitude? [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(D) 4:00 p.m.
Since 15° = 1 hour so 90° = 6 hours. Adding 6 hours to 10:00 a.m. we get 4:00 p.m.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following lines of Longitude and Latitude:
1. Prime Meridian
2. Tropic of Cancer
3. Equator
Which of the above lines is/are a Great Circle? [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(C) 1 and 3 only
All meridians of longitude (including the Prime Meridian) and the Equator are Great Circles because they divide the Earth into two equal halves. Tropic of Cancer is a small circle.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following latitudes will experience a minimum angle of the Sun's rays when it is Summer Solstice in the Northern Hemisphere? [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(D) Tropic of Capricorn
During Summer Solstice in the Northern Hemisphere (June 21), the Sun's rays are least direct (minimum angle) at the Tropic of Capricorn among the given options because it is located farthest south (23.5° S) from the Tropic of Cancer (23.5° N) where the Sun is directly overhead.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following layers of the atmosphere means "region of mixing"? [SSC MTS 15/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Troposphere
Troposphere (height - poles 8km, equator 18km, average 13km) is the lowest layer of atmosphere. It is called zone of mixing. Temperature decreases with height in this layer which is called as Lapse Rate (1°C for every 165m or 6.5°C for every 1 km). Tropopause separates troposphere from stratosphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: All the changes in climate and weather take place in _______. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Troposphere
All the changes in climate and weather take place in troposphere.
Layers of atmosphere :- Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere, Thermosphere, Exosphere (TRICK - Thanks So Much The Ex)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: What is the height of the troposphere near the poles and the equator, respectively? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(A) 8 km and 18 km
The height of troposphere is more at equator because of the heated air that rises to greater heights.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct regarding the stratosphere? [SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]

options.b: It extends up to a height of 50 km.
options.c: It contains a layer of ozone gas.
options.d: This layer is almost free from clouds.

solution:
(A) It lies above the mesosphere
Stratosphere (12-50km) - Second layer of atmosphere. It lies below mesosphere and above troposphere. It contains ozone layer (30-35km). Temperature increases with altitude in this layer. This layer is almost free from clouds therefore ideal for flying aeroplanes.
Stratopause - divides stratosphere and mesosphere
Do you know that 16 September is observed as Ozone day (16 September 1987 - Montreal protocol; to phase out CFCs (Chlorofluorocarbons). Kigali amendment made to phase out HFCs (Hydrofluorocarbons).
Do you know that Ozone layer thickness is measured in Dobson unit?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In which layer of atmosphere do meteorites burn up on entering from space? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Mesosphere
Mesosphere (50-80km) - Coldest layer of atmosphere. Meteorites burn in this layer. Temperature decreases with altitude (-100°C at the height of 80km).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Above which layer of the atmosphere does the Exosphere lie? [SSC CHSL 19/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Thermosphere
Exosphere (700-1000km) - Uppermost layer of atmosphere. High Earth orbit and mid earth orbit satellites are present in this layer.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: How much percentage of oxygen is present in the atmosphere? [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]
solution:
(D) 21%
Composition of gases in Earth's atmosphere :- N_2 - 78%, O_2 - 21%, Ar - 0.9%, CO_2 - 0.036%
Evolution of atmosphere -
Loss of primordial atmosphere
Hot interior of Earth through volcanism
Modification by the living world

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which layer of atmosphere helps in radio transmission? [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Thermosphere
Thermosphere (80-700km) - Hottest layer, temperature rises with altitude. International Space Station is present in this layer. Karman line is located in this layer. Only layer having charged particles in it, which aids in communication.
Ionosphere is a part of thermosphere which reflects and modifies radio waves thereby helping in radio transmission.
Karman line (100km) - boundary between the Earth's atmosphere and outer space.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Atmospheric temperature does NOT depend on _______. [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Salinity of oceans
Atmospheric temperature depends on insolation, latitude, altitude, distance from sea etc.
Salinity of oceans doesn't have any effect on atmospheric temperature.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: At what height, do the Jet Streams blow in India during winter months? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(D) 9-13 km
Jet streams are fast-moving, narrow bands of air that flow from west to east around the Earth. During winter months, they blow at the height of 9-13 km in India.
Westerly jet stream brings Mediterranean disturbances in India.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following statements about the ionosphere is NOT correct? [SSC CPO 12/12/2019]

options.a: Radio wave transmitted from the earth are reflected back to the earth by this layer.
options.c: It is ionized by solar and cosmic radiation.
options.d: It is located immediately above the stratopause.

solution:
(D) It is located immediately above the stratopause.
Ionosphere is a part of thermosphere which reflects and modifies radio waves thereby helping in radio transmission. Ionosphere is located immediately above the mesosphere and not the stratosphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which cycle shows the movement of water? [SSC CHSL 02/06/2022]
solution:
(D) Hydrological cycle
The water cycle is the motion of the water from the ground to the atmosphere and back again. The following processes are involved in the water cycle : evaporation, transpiration, condensation, precipitation, runoff

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: _______ is a naturally occurring phenomenon that is responsible for the heating of the Earth's surface and atmosphere. [SSC CPO 25/11/2020]
solution:
(C) Greenhouse effect
The greenhouse effect is a natural process where certain gases in the Earth's atmosphere trap heat, preventing it from escaping into space and warming the planet, making it habitable.
Greenhouse gases :- water vapour, CO_2, CH_4, SO_2, O_3 (NO_2 isn't a greenhouse gas)
Atmosphere heats up due to - Conduction - transfer of heat through contact, Convection - vertical transfer of heat, Advection - horizontal transfer of heat (loo is a result of advection)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Cirrus and cumulus are types of _______. [SSC CHSL 19/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Clouds
Low level clouds - Nimbus - rain bearing, black/dark grey coloured, opaque to sunlight Stratus - layered clouds
High level clouds - Cumulus - flat base, wool shaped, doesn't rain, 4000-7000 m
Cirrus - feathery appearance, doesn't rain
Do you know that Cumulonimbus clouds have extreme vertical development and are associated with cyclones?
Do you know that the mackerel sky is formed by Cirrocumulus clouds which resemble the scales of mackerel fish?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which environmental phenomenon has been linked to synthetic chemicals like chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs)? [SSC CHSL 19/10/2020]
solution:
(C) Ozone depletion
Ozone depletion means the thinning of ozone layer. It is mainly caused by chemicals like Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs). CFCs are being replaced by hydrochlorofluorocarbons (HCFCs) and hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: What is mainly responsible for the increase in the greenhouse effect? [SSC CHSL 10/01/2024]
solution:
(D) Increasing CO_2 concentration
See solution of question 13 (level 1)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: _______ is defined as the temperature to which the air would have to cool (at constant pressure and constant water vapour content) in order to reach saturation. [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]
solution:
(A) Dew point temperature
Dew point is the temperature at which the air becomes saturated (can hold no more water).
Condensation takes place when the water vapour in air cools down to its dew point or when the air becomes saturated with moisture and can't hold no more amount of it.
Different forms of condensation - Dew - moisture that forms as a result of condensation
Fog - no solid surface needed, water vapour (big particles)
Mist - condenses around hygroscopic particles (small particles)
Frost - deposition of white crystals

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following steps is NOT involved in a Water Cycle? [SSC CHSL 17/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Hydration
The water cycle is the motion of the water from the ground to the atmosphere and back again. The following processes are involved in the water cycle : evaporation, transpiration, condensation, precipitation, runoff.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In the context of mapping climate data, what term refers to a line drawn through all points on a map that contain the same amount of cloud? [SSC CHSL 10/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Isonephs
Isoneph - line connecting places having the same amount of cloud cover.
Isohel - line connecting places having same duration of sunshine.
Isocheim - line connecting places having same average temperature in winter.
Isochalaz - line connecting places with the same frequency of hailstorms.
Isohaline - line connecting places with the same salinity.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: One term for the continuous movement of water through the lithosphere, atmosphere, and hydrosphere is: [SSC CPO 26/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Hydrological cycle
Hydrological cycle/Water cycle is the continuous movement of water through the lithosphere, atmosphere and hydrosphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The zone separating the troposphere from the stratosphere is called the _______. [SSC CHSL 01/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Tropopause
Tropopause is the zone separating troposphere from the stratosphere. The temperature in tropopause remains constant.
Stratopause is the zone separating stratosphere from the mesosphere.
Recall about Earth's inner structure.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following claims about chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) is accurate? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]

options.b: CFCs are used as repairers of the troposphere.
options.c: CFCs are used as fuel for air crafts.
options.d: CFCs are used as repairers of the ozone layer.

solution:
(A) CFCs are used as refrigerants
CFCs (Chlorofluorocarbons) are man-made compounds containing chlorine, fluorine and carbon. They are used as refrigerants, solvents, and aerosol propellants. They are responsible for ozone layer depletion.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following greenhouse gases is most abundant in the atmosphere? [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Water vapour
Water vapour is the most abundant greenhouse gas in the atmosphere but in terms of human emissions, CO_2 is the most important.
Conference of Parties (COP) is the supreme decision making body of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). Latest COP - COP 29 (Baku, Azerbaijan).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Orographic rainfall occurs when _______. [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]

options.a: The air masses collide at Intertropical Conversion Zone
options.c: Hot air rises up in convectional currents
options.d: Saturated air mass comes across a mountain

solution:
(D) Saturated airmass comes across a mountain
Rainfall is of 3 types :
Convectional - occurs when surface of Earth is heated by Sun
Orographic - occurs when saturated airmass comes across a mountain
Cyclonic - occurs due to cyclone
In western ghats, orographic rainfall takes place as the moist air is obstructed and forced to rise along the Ghats.
Cloud seeding - It is an artificial rain caused by spreading Silver Iodide in the clouds.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: International Day for the preservation of the Ozone layer is observed on [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(C) September 16
16 September is observed as Ozone day (16 September 1987 - Montreal protocol; to phase out CFCs (Chlorofluorocarbons). Kigali amendment made to phase out HFCs (Hydrofluorocarbons).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Ozone layer, which absorbs the ultraviolet radiation, is found in which one of the following layers of the atmosphere? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(D) Stratosphere
Ozone layer (30-35 km) is found in stratosphere. It absorbs the harmful UV radiations coming from Sun.
Tropospheric ozone is bad for our environment.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements is true with regard to a greenhouse? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: The shorter wavelength infrared radiations from the Sun can enter into the greenhouse while longer wavelength infrared radiations from the ground and the plants inside the greenhouse cannot pass back out through the glass.
options.b: The shorter wavelength infrared radiations from the Sun can enter into the greenhouse and the longer wavelength infrared radiations from the ground and the plants inside the greenhouse cannot pass back out through the glass.
options.c: The shorter wavelength infrared radiations from the Sun cannot enter into the greenhouse while longer wavelength infrared radiations from the ground and the plants inside the greenhouse can pass back out through the glass.
options.d: No infrared radiation can pass through the glass of the greenhouse.

solution:
(A) The shorter wavelength infrared radiations from the Sun can enter into the greenhouse while longer wavelength infrared radiations from the ground and the plants inside the greenhouse cannot pass back out through the glass.
For short wavelength radiations, greenhouse acts as transparent therefore allowing them to enter while for the long wavelength radiations, greenhouse acts as opaque.
Earth receives insolation in the form of short wave radiations and gives back heat into space by long wave terrestrial radiations which heat up the atmosphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is the most important factor for the formulation of smog? [CDS I 2022]

options.c: Presence of many air pollutant resources.
options.d: Rapid fall in temperature with increasing height above the sea level.

solution:
(B) Formation of 'inversion lid'
Smog (smoke + fog) is a type of air pollution and is formed when pollutants react with sunlight and atmospheric conditions. Formation of inversion lid is crucial for smog. An inversion lid is formed when hot air traps the cooler air below it, preventing vertical air movement and dispersion of pollutants.
Haze is the suspension of dry particles in the air which obstructs visibility while dew is the water droplets which condense on the surface when air cools down below the dew point.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements about inversion of temperature is/are correct?
1. Temperature increases with increasing altitude.
2. A long winter night with clear skies is an ideal situation.
3. It is a short term phenomenon and is common all over the globe except at the poles. [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(A) 1 and 2 only
Temperature inversion - air temperature rises with altitude.
Favourable conditions - long winter night, still air, clear cloudless sky
At poles, temperature inversion is common because of continuous loss of heat from the surface and minimal absorption of solar radiation by the surface.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists:
List I (Cloud Form)
a. Cirrus
b. Cumulus
c. Nimbostratus
d. Stratus

List II (Characteristic)
1. Storm clouds
2. High fog "Low Ceiling"
3. Fair-weather clouds
4. Entirely of ice particles [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(C) a-4, b-3, c-1, d-2
Cirrus clouds - entirely made of ice particles, Cumulus clouds - fair weather clouds, Nimbostratus clouds - storm clouds, Stratus clouds - high fog "low ceiling".
Halo - ring of light seen around Sun or moon when light is reflected or refracted by the ice particles. Cirrostratus clouds form halos.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following characteristics of a cloud type:
1. They are born through convection.
2. Only cloud type that can produce hail, thunder and lightning.
3. They are large cauliflower-shaped towers, often 'anvil tops'.
Identify the type of cloud on the basis of the given characteristics. [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(B) Cumulonimbus
Cumulonimbus clouds (thunder clouds) can produce hail, thunder, lightning. They are born through convection and have extreme vertical development. They are also called as 'anvil tops'.
Do you know that Cumulonimbus Incus is also called as Anvil cloud and is formed when the Cumulonimbus cloud reaches its maturity?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements about clouds:
1. Two major forms of clouds are stratiform and cumuliform.
2. According to the altitude, clouds are classified as high clouds, middle clouds and low clouds.
3. Stratus, nimbostratus and stratocumulus are types of high clouds.
4. Clouds having nimbo attached to their name produce precipitation.
Which of the statements given above are correct? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(B) 1, 2 and 4
Two major forms of clouds are stratiform and cumuliform. Clouds are of three types according to height - high clouds, middle clouds and low clouds. Clouds with 'nimbo' in their name produce precipitation. Stratus, nimbostratus and stratocumulus are types of low clouds not high clouds.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements on 'Fog':
1. Fog is simply a cloud that forms close to the ground.
2. Radiation fog is associated with radiation cooling of the land at night.
3. Advection fog forms when moisture is blown over a cold surface and is chilled by contact.
Which of the statements given above are correct? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Fog - Tiny water droplets or ice crystals suspended near the surface. It is simply a cloud that forms close to the ground.
Radiation fog - Radiation fog forms when the ground cools at night causing the air near the surface to cool as well, reaching its dew point and forming fog.
Advection fog - Advection fog forms when warm, moist air moves horizontally over a colder surface, causing the air to cool and condense into fog.
Dew is the water vapour that condenses on cold surfaces while fog is a cloud formed near the ground consisting of water droplets suspended in the air.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following is the correct order of clouds in ascending order (lowest to highest) of height from surface?
1. Cirrocumulus
2. Nimbostratus
3. Cirrus
4. Altostratus [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(D) 2, 4, 1, 3
Nimbostratus - Altostratus - Cirrocumulus - Cirrus

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is related to global dimming? [CDS I 2024]

options.a: Fall of atmospheric pressure due to increased particulates.
options.b: Raise of atmospheric pressure due to increased particulates.
options.c: Raise of temperature due to increased particulates.
options.d: Fall of temperature due to increased particulates.

solution:
(D) Fall of temperature due to increased particulates
Global dimming means the decrease in amount of sunlight reaching the Earth's surface. It can lead to cooling effect on the planet which acts in opposition to global warming.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Identify the type of cloud on the basis of the given characteristics:
1. High, thin, puffy white clouds of ice crystals that look like ripples.
2. They appear between 20000 feet and 40000 feet above the Earth's surface.
3. One of its types is called a 'mackerel sky' because the clouds resemble large fish scales, especially when they are coloured pink at sunset. [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(D) Cirrocumulus
Cirrocumulus clouds are high (20,000-40,000 feet), thin, puffy white clouds of ice crystals that look like ripples.
Mackerel sky is the sky formed with rows of small, white or grey clouds, often resembling the scales of mackerel fish.
Do you know that cloud cover is measured in Oktas?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding insolation is/are correct?
1. Insolation is predominantly short-wave radiation, with wavelengths in the range of 0.39 micrometre to 0.76 micrometre.
2. Insolation is evenly distributed across the Earth because of the Earth's curved surface. [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Insolation or incoming solar radiation is predominantly shortwave radiation having wavelength in the range of 0.39-0.76 µm. Insolation is not evenly distributed across the Earth. The equatorial regions receive more solar radiation than the polar regions.
Insolation is less at equator due to presence of clouds while it is high at tropics due to absence of clouds.
Heat budget - when the Earth's surface maintains its normal temperature, neither cools nor heats up.
Albedo - amount of insolation reflected back by a surface. Venus has the highest albedo among all the planets. Earth reflected 2/3rd of what it receives.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: How can one determine the relative humidity on a particular day at a given place? [CDS I 2024]

options.c: If both temperature and absolute humidity are given
options.d: If percentage of absolute humidity is given

solution:
(C) If both temperature and absolute humidity are given.
Humidity refers to the amount of water vapour present in atmosphere. Absolute humidity - actual amount of water vapour in the atmosphere. Relative humidity - % of moisture present in atmosphere compared to its full capacity.
Relative humidity = Absolute humidity/Max water vapour air can hold at a given temperature × 100

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which greenhouse gas has the maximum average residence time in atmosphere? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) N_2O
Nitrous oxide has the highest average residence time in the atmosphere, staying for about 121 years.
Do you know that in terms of trapping ability, Sulphur Hexafluoride (SF_6) is the most potent greenhouse gas?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements is correct with reference to normal lapse rate? [CAPF 2022]

options.a: Temperature is highest at ground level and decreases with increasing altitude.
options.b: Temperature is lowest at ground level and increases with increasing altitude.
options.c: Temperature remains stable with increasing altitude.
options.d: Temperature first increases with increasing altitude and gradually starts decreasing.

solution:
(A) Temperature is highest at ground level and decreases with increasing altitude.
Lapse rate - Rate of change of temperature with altitude. It is -ve when temperature increases with altitude, zero when temperature remains constant with altitude and +ve when temperature decreases with altitude.
Normal Lapse Rate - temperature decreases with height (1°C for every 165m or 6.5°C for every 1km).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one is the most abundant of all the elements on Earth? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(D) Oxygen
Most abundant elements of Earth's crust - Oxygen (46.6%) > Silicon (27.7%) > Aluminium (8.1%) > Iron (5%)
Aluminium is the most abundant metal in the Earth's crust.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The depletion in atmospheric ozone layer in last century was due to: [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(A) Chlorofluorocarbon
CFCs (Chlorofluorocarbons) are man-made compounds containing chlorine, fluorine and carbon. They are used as refrigerants, solvents and aerosol propellants. They are responsible for ozone layer depletion.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements regarding the temperature of the Earth's surface:
1. The temperature of a surface is determined by net radiation.
2. Net radiation produces a radiant energy flow that can heat or cool a surface.
3. Net radiation balance is usually positive during the daytime and negative during night time.
4. Soil surface loses energy as temperature increases during the daytime.
Which of the statements given above are correct? [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(C) 1, 2 and 3 only
The temperature of a surface is determined by net radiation (incoming radiation - outgoing radiation). Net radiation is positive during day as incoming radiation is more than outgoing radiation and vice-versa during night. Net radiation produces a radiant energy flow that can heat (if net radiation is +ve) or cool (if net radiation is -ve) a surface. Soil surface gains energy during daytime as temperature is more.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statement with reference to hailstones is NOT correct? [CAPF 2024]

options.a: Their formation usually occurs during the monsoon season.
options.b: They are associated with dense cumulonimbus clouds.
options.c: Rain that forms in the warmer, upper layer solidifies into tiny ice pellets or hailstones in the lower, subfreezing layer.
options.d: Hailstones have multiple, concentric layers of ice.

solution:
(A) Their formation usually occurs during the monsoon season.
Hailstones are associated with cumulonimbus clouds. They can form during different seasons (not specifically in monsoon). They have multiple, concentric layers of ice. Rain solidifies as it passes through subfreezing layers of atmosphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following is not among the conditions ideal for inversion of temperature? [CBI 2023]

options.d: Low air pressure near the ground surface

solution:
(D) Low air pressure near the ground surface
Temperature inversion - air temperature rises with altitude.
Favourable conditions - long winter night, still air, clear cloudless sky.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not a factor that causes variations in 'Insolation'? [CBI 2023]

options.a: Rotation of the Earth on its axis
options.b: Angle of inclination of the Sun's rays
options.d: Vegetation cover on the Earth's surface

solution:
(D) Vegetation cover on the Earth's surface
Factors affecting insolation - Transparency of atmosphere, Length of the day, Tilt of Earth, Rotation, Position of Earth, Latitude, Altitude.
During Aphelion, insolation is less. During perihelion, insolation is high.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The main constituents of atmospheric air are Oxygen (O_2) and Nitrogen (N_2). The composition of O_2 and N_2 is approximately: [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(C) 21% and 78% respectively
Composition of gases in Earth's atmosphere :- N_2 (78%) > O_2 (21%) > Ar (0.9%) > CO_2 (0.036%) > Neon > Helium > Methane > Krypton > Hydrogen.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following factors does not affect the distribution of groundwater? [NDA II 2021]

options.c: Ability of the ground surface to allow water to infiltrate into the groundwater system

solution:
(D) Distance from sea
Factors affecting distribution of groundwater - precipitation, evaporation, ability of surface to absorb water etc.
Distance from sea doesn't directly affect the distribution of groundwater.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not a form of condensation? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(D) Sleet
Sleet is liquid water that freezes before hitting the ground. Hence, it isn't a form of condensation.
Different forms of condensation - Dew - moisture that forms as a result of condensation
Fog - no solid surface needed, water vapour (big particles)
Mist - condenses around hygroscopic particles (small particles)
Frost - deposition of white crystals

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following clouds is a rain-bearing cloud? [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(C) Nimbus cloud
Nimbus are rain bearing clouds.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The composition of gases in exosphere is [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(A) Helium and Hydrogen
In exosphere, the main gases present are hydrogen and helium.
Do you know that Oxygen is almost negligible beyond the height of 120 km. Similarly, CO_2 and water vapour are found only upto 90 km from the Earth's surface?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of layers as we move from the Earth's surface upwards? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(B) Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere, Thermosphere
Layers of atmosphere - Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere, Thermosphere, Exosphere

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The Earth's atmosphere is mainly heated by which one of the following? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(C) Long wave terrestrial radiation
Earth receives solar radiation (short wave) and then reflects it back in the form of long wave radiation which are captured by atmosphere and reflected back, therefore increasing the Earth's temperature.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
According to the Indian Meteorological Department, which of the following is/are the feature/features of cloudburst?
1. Heavy precipitation in short period of time in a limited geographical area.
2. It occurs generally during monsoon period and triggers flash flood and landslides.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Cloudburst - Heavy precipitation during a short period of time over a limited area. According to Indian Meteorological Department (IMD), cloudburst is characterised by rainfall exceeding 100mm/h in a area of 20-30 km^2 area.
Headquarters of IMD - Mausam Bhawan (New Delhi).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Snow, sleet and hail are the forms of: [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(A) Precipitation
Precipitation - any type of water falling from atmosphere to the Earth's surface. Eg. rainfall, snowfall, sleet, hail etc.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not a main greenhouse gas? [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(B) Oxygen
Oxygen is not a greenhouse gas.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is the usual unit of measurement for 'Air Pressure' used in India? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(B) Millibar
Millibar is the standard unit of measurement of air pressure.
1 atm = 760 mm of Hg.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In which one of the following layers of the atmosphere all weather phenomenon occur? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(B) Troposphere
All weather phenomenon occur in troposphere which is the lowest layer of atmosphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding air temperature is/are correct?
1. Air temperature is measured at a standard height of 1-2 m (4.0 feet) above the ground surface.
2. The average rate of temperature decrease with height is termed as the environmental temperature lapse rate.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Air temperature is measured at a standard height of 1-2 m to avoid the influence of ground heat. Temperature decreases with altitude which is called Lapse Rate (1°C every 165 m).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding 'aurora' is/are correct?
1. The solar wind upon reaching the Earth's atmosphere is directed towards two magnetic poles and a colourful display of lights is seen in the night sky.
2. The particles interact with the different gases of the atmosphere and each gas glows with a particular colour.
3. In April 2023, aurora was captured in the night sky in India by the Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Auroras are colourful display of lights seen in night sky at high latitudes when solar wind is directed towards the magnetic poles (aurora borealis - northern hemisphere, aurora australis - southern hemisphere). Each gas of atmosphere produces different colour. In April 2023, aurora was captured in the night sky in India by Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh.
Do you know that Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh is the highest observatory in India?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding humidity is/are correct?
1. It is a ratio between the actual amount of water vapour present in the atmosphere and the maximum amount it can hold at a given temperature.
2. When the relative humidity is high, more water evaporates from the skin.
3. Higher the air temperature lower the relative humidity of the air.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(C) 1 and 3 only
Relative humidity - percentage of moisture present in atmosphere compared to its full capacity.
Relative humidity = Absolute humidity/Max water vapour air can hold at a given temperature × 100.
High relative humidity means air already contains high amount of water and hence can't absorb more water from sources like sweat. Water evaporates more from the skin when the relative humidity is less.
Relative humidity is inversely related to the temperature as hot air can absorb more water vapour.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements regarding 'fronts':
1. The movement of a front causes a slow change in weather in the area over which it moves.
2. Cold fronts are associated with thunderstorms.
3. Warm front is the boundary between an advancing mass of warm air where it is overriding and rising above a mass of colder air.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Front is a 3-D boundary zone between two converging air masses with different physical properties (temperature, humidity, density).
Process of formation of front - Frontogenesis
Process of dissipation of front - Frontolysis
TYPES OF FRONT:
Stationary Front - When two air masses are unable to push against each other.
Cold Front - When cold air mass replaces a warm air mass by pushing into it. It is associated with thunderstorms.
Warm Front - When warm air mass moves over cold air mass. It is associated with gradual change in weather and associated precipitation.
Occluded Front - When cold air mass overtakes a warm air mass and goes underneath it.
Do you know that Temperate cyclones are produced by Frontogenesis?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding clouds is/are correct?
1. Clouds are classified on the basis of altitude and their form.
2. According to altitude they are classified as High, Middle and Low clouds.
3. Stratus, Nimbostratus and Stratocumulus are high clouds.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(B) 1 and 2 only
Clouds are classified on the basis of altitude and form. According to altitude, they are classified as high, middle and low clouds and according to form they are classified as stratiform and cumuliform. Stratus, Nimbostratus and Stratocumulus are low clouds.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following important greenhouse gases is not considered to be largely changed in its amount due to human activities? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(A) Water vapour
Water vapour is the most abundant greenhouse gas but its amount isn't directly changed due to human activities.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The incident solar radiation is trapped by constituents of the atmosphere and reflects it partly towards the Earth as: [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(B) Thermal infrared rays
Earth receives insolation in the form of shortwave radiation and gives back heat into space in the form of long wave or infrared radiation.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The ultraviolet solar radiations are largely absorbed by: [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(C) Oxygen and Ozone
Oxygen and Ozone absorb the UV radiation coming from Sun.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The most significant variable gases in the atmosphere are: [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(C) Water vapour and Carbon dioxide
Variable gases are those gases which are present in small amounts and variable amounts. These include CO_2, CH_4, water vapour, ozone etc.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not correct in the context of the 'greenhouse effect'? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.a: Nitrogen and oxygen are poor absorbers of shortwave solar radiation.
options.b: Clouds are poor absorbers of the Earth radiation.
options.c: The most Earth radiation that escapes to space is in a narrow band from 8-13 micrometres.
options.d: More than 70% of the atmospheric radiant energy is directed back to the surface.

solution:
(B) Clouds are poor absorbers of the Earth radiation
Nitrogen and oxygen are considered poor absorbers of shortwave radiation.
Clouds are poor absorbers of incoming short wave radiation but excellent absorbers of outgoing long wave radiations (Earth radiation).
The most Earth radiation that escapes to space is in a narrow band from 8-13 µm. It is called Atmospheric window.
More than 70% of the atmospheric radiant energy is directed back to the surface by atmosphere which is called Greenhouse effect.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not correct in the context of daytime heating experienced on the Earth? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.a: The highest daytime temperatures usually occur several hours after the time of maximum solar input.
options.b: Equilibrium between incoming and outgoing radiation occurs about an hour and a half after sunset.
options.c: The extent of daily lag in maximum temperature varies.
options.d: Presence of vegetation cover reduces the maximum temperature.

solution:
(B) Equilibrium between incoming and outgoing radiation occurs about an hour and a half after sunset.
The highest daytime temperatures usually occur several hours after the time of maximum solar input as Earth takes some time to absorb and reradiate heat.
There is no fix time for equilibrium between incoming and outgoing radiation and it depends on various factors.
The extent of daily lag in temperature varies depending on factors like solar radiation, heat absorption, atmospheric conditions etc.
Presence of vegetation cover reduces the maximum temperature.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Dew point temperature represents that: [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.a: Thin layer of air near the ground surface has become cold enough to condensate.
options.b: There is thorough mixing of air.

solution:
(A) Thin layer of air near the ground surface has become cold enough to condensate.
Dew point - it is the temperature at which air becomes saturated and can hold no more water.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following greenhouse gases is man-made? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(B) Chlorofluorocarbon
CFCs (Chlorofluorocarbons) are man-made compounds containing chlorine, fluorine and carbon. They are used as refrigerants, solvents, and aerosol propellants. They are responsible for ozone layer depletion.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: As compared to natural greenhouse effect, the enhanced greenhouse effect refers to increase in average surface temperature of the Earth due to: [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(D) Anthropogenic activities
Enhanced greenhouse effect means amplification of greenhouse effect due to increase in greenhouse gases by human activities.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The uplift mechanism that takes place when lighter warm moist air mass rises after encountering a colder and denser air mass causes: [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(B) Frontal precipitation
When lighter warm moist air mass rises after encountering a colder and denser air mass, it is called warm front and is related to precipitation known as Frontal precipitation.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In which sphere of the atmosphere is harmful ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the Sun absorbed? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(D) Stratosphere
Ozone layer is present in stratosphere which absorbs the harmful UV radiation.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following combinations of lapse rate in relation to atmospheric region:
1. Troposphere: Negative lapse rate
2. Stratosphere: Negative lapse rate
3. Mesosphere: Negative lapse rate
4. Thermosphere: Positive lapse rate
How many of the combinations given above is/are correct? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(A) 1
Lapse Rate - It is the rate of change in temperature with altitude. Lapse rate is +ve when temperature decreases with height, zero when temperature remains constant and -ve when temperature increases with height.
Troposphere - +ve lapse rate, Stratosphere - -ve lapse rate, Mesosphere - +ve lapse rate, Thermosphere - -ve lapse rate.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
With reference to the water on the planet Earth, consider the following statements:
1. The amount of water in the rivers and lakes is more than the amount of groundwater.
2. The amount of water in polar ice caps and glaciers is more than the amount of groundwater.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(B) 2 only
97.2% water on Earth is saline and 2.8% is fresh water. This 2.8% is comprised of ice caps/glaciers (2%), ground water (0.68%), lakes (0.4%), atmosphere, rivers. Fresh water as a whole is composed of ice caps/glaciers (68.7%), groundwater (30.1%).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. High clouds primarily reflect solar radiation and cool the surface of the Earth.
2. Low clouds have a high absorption of infrared radiation emanating from the Earth's surface and thus cause a warming effect.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(D) Neither 1 nor 2
High clouds are thin (albedo - 25-30%) so they let through most of the incoming solar radiation and block and reflect the outgoing long wave radiation. Low clouds are thick clouds (albedo - 70-80%) and hence they reflect more insolation and thus have net cooling effect.
Albedo is the percentage of light reflected by a surface. Highest albedo - fresh ice.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: With reference to the Earth's atmosphere, which one of the following statements is correct? [IAS 2023]

options.a: The total amount of insolation received at the equator is roughly about 10 times of that received at the poles.
options.b: Infrared rays constitute roughly two-thirds of insolation.
options.c: Infrared waves are largely absorbed by water vapour that is concentrated in the lower atmosphere.
options.d: Infrared waves are a part of visible spectrum of electromagnetic waves of solar radiation.

solution:
(C) Infrared waves are largely absorbed by water vapour that is concentrated in the lower atmosphere.
The total amount of insolation received at the equator is not about 10 times of that received at poles.
Infrared rays don't constitute about 2/3rd of insolation. Albedo of Earth is about 2/3rd of insolation.
Infrared waves are absorbed by water vapour that acts as a greenhouse gas in the lower atmosphere.
Infrared waves aren't a part of visible spectrum because they have higher wavelength than the visible spectrum.
Descending order of wavelength of electromagnetic spectrum - Radio waves > Microwave > Infrared > Visible > UV > X-rays > Gamma rays {TRICK - Raging Martians Invaded Venus Using X-ray Guns}

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
With reference to "water vapour", which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is a gas, the amount of which decreases with altitude.
2. Its percentage is maximum at the poles.
Select the answer using the code given below: [IAS 2024]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Water vapour is a gas and its concentration decreases with altitude.
The percentage of water vapour is not maximum at the poles; it is generally higher in tropical and equatorial regions.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: Rainfall is one of the reasons for weathering of rocks.
