Statement-II: Rain water contains carbon dioxide in solution.
Statement-III: Rain water contains atmospheric oxygen.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? [IAS 2024]

options.a: Both Statement-II and Statement-III are correct and both of them explain Statement-I.
options.b: Both Statement-II and Statement-III are correct, but only one of them explains Statement-I.
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement-I.
options.d: Neither Statement-II nor Statement-III is correct

solution:
(A) Both statement-II and statement-III are correct and both of them explain statement-I.
Rainfall is one of the reasons for weathering of rocks (chemical weathering). Rain water has pH value of 5.6 due to the dissolution of CO_2, which forms weak carbonic acid. During rain, oxygen concentrations in rain drops tend to rise as rain drops saturate with oxygen as they fall.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: Thickness of the troposphere at the equator is much greater as compared to poles.
Statement-II: At the equator, heat is transported to great heights by strong convectional currents.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? [IAS 2024]

options.a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement-I.
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I.
options.c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect.
options.d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct.

solution:
(A) Both statement-I and statement-II are correct and statement-II explains statement-I.
Troposphere (height - poles 8km, equator 18km, average 13km) is the lowest layer of atmosphere.
The height of troposphere is more at equator because of the heated air that rises to greater heights.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: The atmosphere is heated more by incoming solar radiation than by terrestrial radiation.
Statement-II: Carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases in the atmosphere are good absorbers of long wave radiation.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? [IAS 2024]

options.a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement-I.
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I.
options.c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect.
options.d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct.

solution:
(D) Statement-I is incorrect but statement-II is correct.
The atmosphere is primarily heated by outgoing long wave radiation. Carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases in the atmosphere are good absorbers of long wave radiation.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following is/are correct inference/inferences from isothermal maps in the month of January?
1. The isotherms deviate to the north over the ocean and to the south over the continent.
2. The presence of cold ocean currents, Gulf Stream and North Atlantic Drift make the North Atlantic Ocean colder and the isotherms bend towards the north.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [IAS 2024]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Isotherms - lines joining places with same temperature
Isotherms deviate to the north over the oceans and to the south over the continent in the month of January as the temperature over the oceans in more compared to continents. So for same temperature, we have to move slightly polewards over the oceans and equator wards over the continents. The presence of Gulf stream and North Atlantic drift, which are warm ocean currents, make the North Atlantic ocean warmer and the isotherms bend towards the north.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Identify the cloud type based on the given characteristics:
1. Looks like cotton wool.
2. Exists in patches with a flat base.
3. Forms at 4-7 km height.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(B) Cumulus
CLOUDS
Low Level Clouds - Nimbus - rain bearing, opaque to sunlight (black, dark grey coloured),
Stratus - layered clouds
High Level Clouds - Cumulus - looks like cotton wool, flat base, 4-7 km height, doesn't rain
Cirrus - high altitude, feathery appearance, doesn't rain

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which statements about Surface inversion of temperature are correct?
1. It causes instability in the lower atmosphere.
2. It commonly lasts until sunrise.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(B) 2 only
Temperature Inversion - it is a phenomena where temperature increases with height.
Conditions favourable for temperature inversion : Long winter nights, Cloudless and clear sky, Dry air near the ground, Slow movement of air, Snow covered ground surface.
It leads to stable atmospheric conditions. It lasts till sunrise and dissipates when the Sun begins to heat the ground.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements with reference to Sleet is/are correct?
1. Sleet is frozen raindrops and refrozen melted snow-water.
2. It occurs when a layer of air with the temperature below freezing point overlies a warm layer near the ground surface.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Sleet is a form of precipitation composed of small ice pellets. It forms when raindrops or partially melted snowflakes travel from relatively warm air above to freezing air near the ground.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The day-to-day condition of the atmosphere at a place with respect to the temperature, humidity, rainfall, wind speed, etc. is called _______ at that place. [SSC MTS 16/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Weather
Weather means the short term state of atmosphere at a specific place and time including elements like temperature, humidity, wind, and precipitation.
Climate refers to the average weather conditions of a particular location over a periods of 30 years.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The weather pattern formed on the basis of weather data collected for a place over a long period of time, say 25 years, is called _______ of that place. [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Climate
Climate is the average of weather conditions of a location over long time (typically 30 years).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: What type of climate is found in North Western Europe, West Coast of North America, North of California, Southern Chile, South-eastern Australia, and New Zealand? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Marine west coast climate
Marine west coast climate is located polewards from the Mediterranean climate on the west coast of continents. Due to marine influence, the temperature is moderate and in winter, it is warmer for its latitude. Precipitation occurs throughout the year.
Koeppen climatic classification (1884) - empirical climatic classification. 5 major climatic groups :-
A - Tropical (average temperature of the coldest month is 18°C or higher).
B - Dry climate (S - steppe, W - desert) (potential evaporation exceeds precipitation).
C - Warm Temperate (average temperature of the coldest month is higher than -3°C but below 18°C).
D - Cold snow forest (average temperature of the coldest month is -3°C or below).
E - Polar type/tundra (average temperature for all months is below 10°C).
w - winter dry, s - summer dry, m - monsoon, f - full baarish.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements is correct regarding the climate of the Indian subcontinent? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
I. The climate of North India remains similar throughout the year.
II. The Himalayas protect the subcontinent from extremely cold winds from central Asia.

options.b: Both Statements A and B incorrect
options.d: Both Statements A and B correct

solution:
(C) Only statement B is correct
The climate of India is defined as tropical monsoon climate with distinct seasons and significant rainfall during the summers. The Himalayas protect the subcontinent from extremely cold winds from central Asia. The climate of North India doesn't remain similar and changes considerably due to continentality.
Continentality - when a region is located in the interior of the continent, the moderating influence of sea decreases and hence such region experiences extreme weather conditions. For eg. Mumbai being located near the coast experiences less extreme climate than Delhi which is located in the interior of the continent.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: How many controls are there globally that influence the climate of a particular zone? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) 6
There are 6 factors that affect the climate of a particular zone. They are :- Latitude, Altitude, Ocean currents, Pressure and wind system, Distance from sea and Relief features.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following statements about climate is INCORRECT? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]

options.a: 'Monsoon' type of climate is found mainly in south and southeast Asia.
options.b: India's climate has characteristics of tropical as well as subtropical climates.
options.c: Coastal areas of India experience less contrasts in temperature conditions as compared to the interior of the country.
options.d: India experiences comparatively stronger winters as compared to central Asia.

solution:
(D) India experiences comparatively stronger winters as compared to central Asia.
South and Southeast Asia has 'monsoon' type of climate. India has characteristics of both tropical and subtropical climates. Coastal areas of India experience less contrasts in temperature conditions as compared to the interior of the country because of moderating influence of sea. India experiences less stronger winters as compared to central Asia.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: What type of climate do the two island regions of India, Lakshadweep and Andaman and Nicobar Islands experience? [SSC CHSL 06/06/2022]
solution:
(C) Tropical warm climate
Lakshadweep and Andaman and Nicobar experience tropical warm climate, characterised by warm humid conditions year round with distinct wet and dry seasons influenced by monsoon.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following factors does NOT control the climate of a place? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Distance between two cities
The climate of a place is affected by 6 factors - Latitude, Altitude, Ocean currents, Pressure and wind system, Distance from sea and Relief features.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following is incorrectly matched? [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]

options.a: Montane climate - Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Jharkhand
options.b: Tropical monsoon climate - The Western Ghats, the Malabar Coast And Southern Assam
options.c: Tropical semi-arid (steppe) climate - Karnataka, central Maharashtra, some parts of Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh
options.d: Hot deserts, arid climate - Rajasthan and some parts of Gujarat

solution:
(A) Montane climate - Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Jharkhand
Montane forest - seen in mountainous region. With increase in height, there is a succession of natural vegetation.
Foothills of Himalayas : Deciduous forests, 1000-2000 m : wet temperate forests (oaks, chestnuts), 1500-3000 m : coniferous trees (pine, deodar, silver fir, spruce, cedar), above 3600 m : alpine vegetation (silver fir, junipers, pines, rhododendrons, birches).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The centre of a cyclone is a calm area. It is called the _______ of the storm. [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Eye
Cyclone is any large system of airmass circulating around a centre of low pressure. Direction of cyclone - Northern Hemisphere: anticlockwise, Southern Hemisphere - clockwise. At equator, coriolis force is zero and hence no cyclone is formed at equator. Centre (eye) - calm area with no clouds.
Conditions favourable for cyclone - Large sea surface temperature (more than 27°C), Coriolis force, Small variation in vertical wind speed, Pre-existing low pressure area.
Do you know that cyclone at high latitudes is caused due to frontogenesis and are called Extratropical/Temperate cyclones?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which winds blow across the Asian continent at latitudes north of the Himalayas roughly parallel to the Tibetan highlands? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Jet streams
Jet streams blow across the Asian continent at latitudes north of Himalayas roughly parallel to the Tibetan highlands. Jet streams are fast moving winds in upper atmosphere (near tropopause) moving from west to east. Jet streams brings Mediterranean disturbances in India aiding in rainfall in Northern India.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In coastal areas, during the night, wind blows from land to sea because: [SSC MTS 18/07/2022]

options.a: Low pressure is created on land
options.d: High pressure is created at sea

solution:
(C) Water cools slower than land
Sea Breeze - During day, land gets heated faster than water and hence low pressure is created on land so wind blows from sea to land called Sea breeze.
Land Breeze - During night, land gets cold faster than water and hence high pressure is created on land so wind blows from land to sea called Land breeze.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In the context of local storms of hot weather season, Nor'Westers are: [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]

options.a: The pre-monsoon showers that are a common phenomenon in Kerala and coastal areas of Karnataka.
options.b: A nearby wind that blows coffee flowers in Kerala.
options.c: The dreaded evening thunderstorms in Bengal and Assam.
options.d: The hot, dry and oppressing winds blowing in the Northern plains from Punjab to Bihar with higher intensity between Delhi and Patna.

solution:
(C) The dreaded evening thunderstorms in Bengal and Assam
Nor' Westers (Kalbaisakhi/Bardoli Cheerha) - localised rainfall and thunderstorm event occurring in the pre - monsoon season in Bengal and Assam. It is helpful in the growth of jute and tea.
The hot, dry and oppressing winds blowing in the Northern plains from Punjab to Bihar with higher intensity between Delhi and Patna is called 'Loo'.
Other pre-monsoon showers - Blossom showers - Kerala/Karnataka. Helps in growth of coffee.
Mango showers - Kerala. Helps in early ripening of mango.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following is a term for the common local wind that blows in north and north western parts of India during summers? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Loo
Loo - it is the hot, dry and oppressing winds blowing over north India during summer season.
Chinook - warm winds in North America. It is also called Snow Eater. It is a katabatic wind.
Foehn - warm winds in Alps mountains
Bora - cold winds in Europe
Other local winds - Santa Ana (North America) - warm, Blizzard (North America) - cold, Mistral (Europe) - cold, Sirocco (Africa) - warm, causing blood rain; Khamsin (Africa) - warm; Harmattan (Africa) - also known as Doctor wind, warm; Pamparo (Argentina); Brickfielder (Australia).
Anabatic wind - wind rising up a slope, Katabatic wind - wind coming down a slope.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following statements is correct about 'Loo' winds in the context of the Indian climate? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]

options.a: They blow in the winter season.
options.b: They blow in the rainy season.
options.c: They blow in the summer season.
options.d: They blow in the winter and rainy season.

solution:
(C) They blow in the summer season
Loo - it is the hot, dry and oppressing winds blowing over north India during summer season. It is an outcome of advection process (horizontal transfer of heat).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In which type of rainfall, winds move out from a high-pressure area with wind direction clockwise in the northern hemisphere and anti-clockwise in the southern hemisphere? [SSC CPO 05/10/2020]
solution:
(C) Anticyclone
Anticyclone - An area of high pressure with winds blowing out from the centre resulting in calm, clear weather.
Direction - clockwise in Northern Hemisphere and anticlockwise in Southern Hemisphere.
Do you know that Tropical cyclone dissipates on reaching land yet it is more destructive?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In which of the following states of India the 'Loo' wind does NOT blow during the summer season? [SSC MTS 01/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Goa
Loo doesn't blow in Goa as loo blows mainly in northern India.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following is NOT one of the factors that contributed towards localisation of the cotton textile industry in India? [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Dry climate
Cotton grows in warm, humid climate with long frost free days (210 days). Hence dry climate doesn't contribute to the localisation of cotton textile industry in India.
Accessible port facilities, availability of labor and availability of raw cotton are favourable conditions that contribute towards localisation of cotton textile industry.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In how many agro-climatic zones has Tamil Nadu been classified? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(C) 7
Agro climatic zones are regions which are divided based on their climate, rainfall, soil type and temperature, which affect farming. Tamil Nadu has been divided into 7 agro-climatic zones based on its climate, soil type and rainfall pattern. These are North Eastern zone, North Western zone, Western zone, Cauvery delta zone, Southern zone, High rainfall zone, Hilly zone.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which winds are also known by the name of 'Roaring Forties'? [SSC MTS 11/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Westerly winds
The westerlies are the winds blowing from sub-tropical high-pressure belts towards the sub-polar low-pressure belts. Direction - from southwest to northeast in Northern Hemisphere and from northwest to southeast in Southern Hemisphere. The westerlies of the southern hemisphere are stronger and persistent due to the vast expanse of water and are called Roaring Forties, Furious Fifties, Shrieking Sixties.
Three types of cells - Hadley cell - air rises near the equator and descends in subtropics, Ferrel cell - air rises in mid latitudes and descends in subtropics, Polar cell - air rising from mid latitudes and descending at poles.
Do you know that ITCZ (Inter Tropical Convergence Zone) shifts to north and south with the apparent shift of Sun?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which among the following wind is associated with extreme cold and dryness? [SSC MTS 03/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Polar Easterlies
Polar easterlies are dry, cold winds blowing from high-pressure polar areas to the sub-polar lows. Direction - from northeast to southwest in Northern Hemisphere and from southeast to northwest in Southern Hemisphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the main reasons for the excessive cold in north India during the cold weather season and identify which of the statements are correct. [SSC CGL 07/03/2023]
1. States like Punjab, Haryana and Rajasthan, being far away from the moderating influence of sea, experience continental climate.
2. The snowfall in the nearby Himalayan ranges creates a cold wave situation.
3. Around February, the cold winds coming from the Caspian Sea and Turkmenistan bring a cold wave along with frost and fog over the northwestern parts of India.
solution:
(C) 1, 2 and 3
States like Punjab, Haryana and Rajasthan, being far away from the moderating influence of sea, experience continental climate.
The snowfall in the nearby Himalayan ranges creates a cold wave situation.
Around February, the cold winds coming from the Caspian sea and Turkmenistan bring a cold wave along with frost and fog over the northwestern parts of India.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: _______ zone is generally defined as an environment with moderate rainfall throughout the year or parts of the year, with sporadic dry, mild to hot summers and cool to cold winters. [SSC CHSL 01/06/2022]
solution:
(A) Temperate
Temperate zone - Region between 23.5°N and 66.5°N and between 23.5°S and 66.5°S is called temperate zone.
This zone experiences moderate climate with temperature neither too hot nor too cold and moderate rainfall throughout the year.
Tropical zone - Region between 23.5°N and 23.5°S is called Tropical zone. It is characterised by warm, humid climate and abundant rainfall.
Frigid zone - Region beyond 66.5°N and 66.5°S is called Frigid zone. It is extremely cold and receive slanting rays of Sun.
Do you know that the variation of climate on Earth is caused due to the tilt and revolution of the Earth?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following statements is correct about 'Loo' winds in the context of the Indian climate? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]

options.a: They blow in the winter season.
options.b: They blow in the rainy season.
options.c: They blow in the summer season.
options.d: They blow in the winter and rainy season.

solution:
(C) They blow in the summer season
Loo is a hot, dry and oppressing wind blowing over north India during summer season.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Choose the correct statement(s) related to 'continentality'. [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
I. The sea exerts a moderating influence on climate.
II. As the distance from the sea increases, the people experience extreme weather conditions.
III. As one goes from the surface of the earth to higher altitudes, the atmosphere becomes less dense and temperature decreases.
solution:
(D) I and II
When a region is located away from the sea then the moderating influence of sea decreases and the region experiences extreme weather conditions. This phenomenon is called continentality.
As one goes from the surface of the Earth to higher altitudes, the atmosphere becomes less dense and temperature decreases. This statement is correct but it's not related to continentality. It is called Lapse rate.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following statements regarding ocean currents is correct? [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]

options.a: The Labrador Ocean current is warm current.
options.b: The areas where the warm and cold currents meet provide the best fishing grounds.
options.c: The cold ocean currents move towards the poles.
options.d: The warm ocean currents move towards the equator.

solution:
(B) The areas where the warm and cold currents meet provide the best fishing grounds.
Ocean currents are the continuous, predictable, directional movement of seawater. Types - surface (10%) and deep sea (90%).
Causes of ocean current - Heating by Sun, Wind, Density difference, Coriolis force, Coastline of continents.
Cold water moves from poles to equator and warm water moves from equator to poles.
Cold ocean currents - Benguela, Peru, Greenland, Oyashio, Labrador, Falkland, West Australian Drift, Canary, California. (TRICK - Bengaluru Played GOLF World Cup in California)
Areas where warm and cold ocean currents meet provide best fishing ground but creates foggy conditions which is worst for harbouring. Eg. Newfoundland (Gulf stream + Labrador)
Important meet of ocean currents - Labrador+Gulf stream, Oyashio+Kurashio, Okhotsk+Tshusima.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following is a warm current? [SSC MTS 12/07/2022]
solution:
(D) Gulf stream
Warm ocean currents - Gulf stream, Alaska, Brazil, Norwegian, Agulhas, Mozambique, Kuroshio, East Australian Drift.
Trick to remember cold ocean currents - Bengaluru Played GOLF World Cup in California (Benguela, Peru, Greenland, Oyashio, Labrador, Falkland, West Australian Drift, Canary, California).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following is an example of a terrestrial ecosystem? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Grasslands
Ecosystem is a community of living beings and their surroundings that interact with each other.
Types of ecosystem :- Terrestrial ecosystem (desert, grassland, forest, tundra) and Aquatic ecosystem (pond, river, ocean, lagoons).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Tropical grasslands of Brazil are known as _______. [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Campos
Tropical grasslands :- tall, non-nutritious elephant grass. Eg- Savannah (Africa), Campos (Brazil), Llanos (Venezuela)
Temperate grasslands :- Short and nutritious. Eg- Prairies (North America), Steppes (Asia/Europe), Velds (South Africa), Downs (Australia), Pustaz (Hungary), Canterbury (New Zealand), Pampas (Argentina).
Wheat granaries of world - Prairies. Alpha alpha grass are present in Pampas.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following statements is correct regarding air pressure? [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]

options.a: High pressure is associated with cloudy skies and wet weather.
options.b: Low pressure is associated with clear and sunny skies.
options.c: The air always moves from high pressure areas to low pressure areas.
options.d: The air pressure is lowest at sea level and increases with height.

solution:
(C) The air always moves from high pressure areas to low pressure areas.
High pressure (anticyclone) is associated with clear and sunny skies while low pressure (cyclone) is associated with cloudy skies and wet weather. The air pressure is highest near the surface and decreases with altitude. Air always moves from the regions of high pressure to region of low pressure.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Low annual range of temperature and high annual rainfall are characteristics of a _______. [SSC CGL 07/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Tropical humid climate
Tropical Humid climate - Found between Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn. Low annual range of temperature (because Sun is overhead round the year) and high annual rainfall.
Mediterranean climate - Located around the Mediterranean sea, along the west coast of continents in subtropical latitudes. It is characterised by hot, dry summer and mild, rainy winter.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In early November, the low Pressure conditions shift over the Bay of Bengal. This transfer is related to cyclonic low pressure, which originates over _______. [SSC MTS 13/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Andaman Sea
The low pressure shifts over Bay of Bengal in November due to cyclonic low pressure which originates over Andaman Sea.
Tropical Cyclone - only in sea, more destructible, not frequent, flows East to West. Cyclones - Indian Ocean
Hurricanes - Atlantic Ocean, Typhoons - South China Sea, Willy-willies - Western Australia.
Temperate Cyclone - in land/sea, less destructible, more frequent, flows from West to East.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following regions has more seasonal contrast than other regions of India? [SSC CPO 09/11/2022]
solution:
(A) Interiors of India
Interiors of India experience more seasonal contrast than other regions of India due to continentality.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The southern part of India lies in the _______ belt. [SSC MTS 19/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Warm
The southern part of India lies between Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn and hence lies in warm belt.
Torrid zone - between Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn.
Temperate zone - between Tropic of Cancer and Arctic circle and between Tropic of Capricorn and Antarctic circle.
Frigid zone - between Arctic circle and North pole and between Antarctic circle and South pole.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following groups of places in India has little differences in day and night temperatures? [SSC CHSL 01/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Chennai and Thiruvananthapuram
Chennai and Thiruvananthapuram have little differences in day and night temperatures as they are located on coast and so climate is moderated by moderating influence of sea.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the main reasons for the excessive cold in north India during the cold weather season and identify which of the statements are correct. [SSC CGL 07/03/2023]
1. States like Punjab, Haryana and Rajasthan, being far away from the moderating influence of sea, experience continental climate.
2. The snowfall in the nearby Himalayan ranges creates a cold wave situation.
3. Around February, the cold winds coming from the Caspian Sea and Turkmenistan bring a cold wave along with frost and fog over the northwestern parts of India.
solution:
(C) 1, 2 and 3
States like Punjab, Haryana and Rajasthan, being far away from the moderating influence of sea, experience continental climate.
The snowfall in the nearby Himalayan ranges creates a cold wave situation.
Around February, the cold winds coming from the Caspian sea and Turkmenistan bring a cold wave along with frost and fog over the northwestern parts of India.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Select the correct statement from the given options. [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]

options.a: There is very little temperature difference between day and night in the coastal areas of Kerala.
options.b: There is very little temperature difference between day and night in Rajasthan.
options.c: There is extreme cold in southern India during winter season.
options.d: There is plenty of rain observed in central India throughout the year.

solution:
(A) There is very little temperature difference between day and night in the coastal areas of Kerala.
Coastal areas of Kerala experience moderating influence of sea so temperature difference between day and night is very little while Rajasthan being in the interior experiences large temperature difference between day and night. Southern India being located in torrid zone doesn't experience extreme cold even during winter season. Central India observes rainfall during rainy season not throughout the year.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: In which of the following states is there hardly any difference in day and night temperatures? [SSC MTS 01/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Kerala
Kerala has hardly any difference in day and night temperatures due to its located at coast.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which of the following places of India can have maximum temperature difference between the day and night? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Jaisalmer
Jaisalmer has the maximum temperature difference between day and night from the given options due to continentality.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Coriolis force is not correct? [CDS I 2021]

options.a: It is maximum at the Poles.
options.b: It is absent at the Equator.
options.c: It deflects the wind to the right direction in the southern hemisphere.
options.d: It deflects the wind to the right direction in the northern hemisphere.

solution:
(C) It deflects the wind to the right direction in the southern hemisphere.
Coriolis force is the apparent force caused by the Earth's rotation. It deflects the wind to the right in the Northern Hemisphere and to the left in the Southern Hemisphere. It is absent at equator and is maximum at poles. Coriolis force increases with increase in wind velocity and vice-versa.
Cyclone - low pressure centre, direction - anticlockwise in Northern Hemisphere.
Anticyclone - high pressure centre, direction - clockwise in Northern Hemisphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Isohalines are lines joining equal: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(D) Salinity
Isohaline - imaginary line on map connecting places of equal salinity.
Isotherm - Imaginary line on map connecting places of equal temperature.
Isobar - imaginary line on map connecting places of equal pressure.
Isohyets - Imaginary line on map connecting places of equal rainfall.
Isobath - Imaginary line on map connecting places with same depth.
Isohel - imaginary line on map connecting places with same sunshine.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following land surfaces has the highest albedo? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(A) A snow covered mountain
Albedo is the proportion of sunlight that a surface can reflect back into the space. Snow covered areas have the highest albedo and can reflect upto 70-90% of insolation.
Albedo of other surfaces - Fresh snow (80-90%), water (5-80%), desert (30-45%), grass (20-25%), forest (10-20%), asphalt (5%).
Venus has the highest albedo among all the planets while Mercury has the lowest albedo. Earth's albedo - 0.3 (30% of insolation is reflected).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following do not influence the ocean currents? [CDS II 2022]

options.c: Gravitational pull by Sun and Moon

solution:
(C) Gravitational pull by Sun and Moon.
Ocean currents are the continuous, predictable, directional movement of seawater.
Causes of ocean current - Heating by Sun, Wind, Density difference, Coriolis force, Coastline of continents.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The speed of the wind is measured by [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(D) Anemometer
Anemometer - an instrument used to measure wind speed.
Barometer - an instrument used to measure atmospheric pressure
Wind vane/ weather vane/ weather cock - an instrument used to measure wind direction.
Wind rose - a circular chart that displays frequency and direction of winds at a particular location.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Trough and ridge are [CAPF 2022]

options.a: Elongated area of low pressure and of high pressure respectively.
options.d: Elongated area of high pressure and of low pressure respectively.

solution:
(A) Elongated area of low pressure and of high pressure respectively.
Trough is elongated area of low pressure and ridge is elongated area of high pressure.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not correct? [CAPF 2022]

options.a: The Gulf Stream is a warm ocean current.
options.b: The North Atlantic Drift is a warm ocean current.
options.c: The Labrador Current is a cold ocean current.
options.d: The California Current is a warm ocean current.

solution:
(D) The California Current is a warm ocean current.
Warm ocean currents - Gulf stream, Alaska, Brazil, Norwegian, Agulhas, Mozambique, Kuroshio, East Australian Drift.
Cold ocean currents - Benguela, Peru, Greenland, Oyashio, Labrador, Falkland, West Australian Drift, Canary, California. (TRICK - Bengaluru Played GOLF World Cup in California).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists:

a. Cyclones
b. Hurricanes
c. Typhoons
d. Willy-willies

List II:
1. Western Australia

Hurricanes - Atlantic Ocean

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:

b. Agulhas Current

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following characteristics of a type of wind:
1. They occur at upper levels in the atmosphere.
2. They are subjected to two forces, a pressure gradient force and the Coriolis force.
3. When the forces balance, air moves at right angles to the pressure gradient, parallel to the isobars.
Identify the type of wind on the basis of the given characteristics. [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(B) Geostrophic Wind
Geostrophic winds are the winds that blow parallel to isobars when the pressure gradient force and coriolis force are in balance. They occur at the upper atmosphere.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not the characteristic feature of 'El Nino' phenomenon impacting the Indian monsoon? [CBI 2023]

options.a: It replaces the cold Peruvian current.
options.b: Appearance of cold current in East Pacific region.
options.d: Results in drought or weak monsoon in India.

solution:
(B) Appearance of cold current in East Pacific region
El-Nino is the occasional development of warm ocean surface waters along Eastern Pacific ocean.
Effects of El-Nino - Heavy rainfall on west coast of North and South America and severe droughts in Australia, Indonesia, India and southern Africa.
La-Nina is the accumulation of cold water in the central and eastern Pacific ocean.
Effects of La-Nina - Abnormally heavy monsoon in India, cold and wet winters in south-eastern Africa, wet weather in eastern Australia, cold winter in western Canada and north-western United States, winter droughts in the southern United States.
El Nino Southern Oscillation - formation of El Nino is linked with the pacific ocean circulation pattern known as Southern Oscillation (low pressure over Eastern Pacific and high pressure over Western Pacific). El Nino and Southern Oscillation coincide most of the time hence called ENSO (El Nino Southern Oscillation).
Do you know that The Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD) is a climate phenomenon characterised by fluctuations in sea surface temperatures and atmospheric conditions in the Indian Ocean. It consists of a see-saw pattern of sea surface temperature anomalies between the western and eastern parts of the Indian Ocean?

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following relating to the formation of tropical cyclone is/are correct?
1. Large sea surface with temperature above 27°C.
2. Presence of Coriolis force.
3. Upper convergence over the sea level system.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CBI 2023]
solution:
(A) 1 and 2
Conditions favourable for the formation of cyclones are :- Large sea surface temperature (27°C), Presence of Coriolis force, A pre existing low-pressure area, Low wind shear, Upper-level divergence. Third statement talks about upper level convergence while for formation of cyclone upper level divergence is required not upper level convergence. So it is incorrect.
Wind shear is the difference between wind speeds at different altitudes.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not a 'cold ocean current'? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(D) Kuroshio Current
See solution of question 67 (level II)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Consider the following statements about anticyclones.
1. Anticyclones are high pressure systems.
2. Air in the centre of the system must be subsiding.
3. Anticyclones are characterized by converging winds.
How many of the above statements is/are correct? [NDA II 2022]
solution:
(B) 2
Anticyclones are high pressure systems. Air in the centre of the system must be subsiding. In anticyclones, air moves outwards (diverging) from the high pressure centre.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following is not a cold current? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(B) Eastern Australian Current
See solution of question 67 (level II)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding 'cyclones and anticyclones' is/are correct?
1. In the Northern Hemisphere, cyclones rotate counter clockwise and anticyclones rotate clockwise.
2. Cyclones are often associated with cloudy or rainy weather, whereas anticyclones are often associated with fair weather.
3. In the Southern Hemisphere, the cyclonic spiral will be clockwise because the Coriolis force acts to the left.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Direction of cyclone - anticlockwise in northern hemisphere and clockwise in southern hemisphere. (iska ulta karke anticyclone ki direction yaad ho jaayegi).
Coriolis force acts to the right in the northern hemisphere and to the left in the southern hemisphere.
Recall the characteristics of cyclones and anticyclones.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding the World Climate Types and their relation with Inter Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) is/are correct?
1. Tropical Wet Climate is one of the world's rainiest due to the dominance of the ITCZ over it.
2. Tropical Savanna Climate experiences a distinctive alternation of wet and dry seasons, caused chiefly by the seasonal shift in latitude of the subtropical highs and ITCZ.
3. The Tropical Monsoonal Climate experiences heavy rainfall as a consequence of the nearness of the ITCZ for much of the year.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Tropical Wet climate is one of the world's rainiest due to the dominance of the ITCZ over it.
Tropical Savanna climate experiences a distinctive alternation of wet and dry seasons caused chiefly by the seasonal shift in latitude of the subtropical highs and ITCZ.
The Tropical Monsoonal climate experiences heavy rainfall as a consequence of the nearness of the ITCZ for much of the year.
Recall about Savannah grasslands.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding tropical cyclones is/are correct?
1. These storms develop during the summer and autumn in every tropical ocean except the South Atlantic and eastern South Pacific Oceans.
2. Tropical cyclones that occur in the North Atlantic and eastern North Pacific Oceans are known as typhoons.
3. Tropical cyclones that occur in the Indian and South Pacific Oceans are called cyclones.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(C) 1 and 3 only
Tropical cyclones typically form over warm ocean waters. These develop during the summer and autumn in every tropical ocean when the sea surface temperature is highest. However, they are rare in South Atlantic ocean and eastern south Pacific ocean due to unfavourable conditions such as cool water.
Tropical cyclones are called hurricanes in the North Atlantic and eastern North Pacific oceans and cyclone in Indian ocean.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Where is the Coriolis Effect the largest? [NDA II 2024]

options.b: At the Tropic of Capricorn and Tropic of Cancer
options.d: At 45 degree latitude in each hemisphere

solution:
(C) At the North Pole
Coriolis force is the apparent force caused by the Earth's rotation. It deflects the wind to the right in the Northern Hemisphere and to the left in the Southern Hemisphere. It is absent at equator and is maximum at poles. Coriolis force increases with increase in wind velocity and vice-versa.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one among the following local winds is NOT related to Mediterranean Sea? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(A) Harmattan
Harmattan (doctor wind) isn't related to Mediterranean sea.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one among the following statements with reference to Benguela current is NOT correct? [NDA II 2024]

options.a: It influences the climate of the West coast of North Africa.
options.c: Its direction is from South to North.
options.d: It flows in the Atlantic Ocean.

solution:
(A) It influences the climate of the West coast of North Africa.
Benguela current is cold ocean current in Atlantic ocean. It influences the climate of West coast of South Africa. Its direction is from south to north.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
With reference to "Coriolis force", which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It increases with increase in wind velocity.
2. It is maximum at the poles and is absent at the equator.
Select the answer using the code given below: [IAS 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Coriolis force is the apparent force caused by the Earth's rotation. It deflects the wind to the right in the Northern Hemisphere and to the left in the Southern Hemisphere. It is absent at equator and is maximum at poles. Coriolis force increases with increase in wind velocity and vice-versa.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which one of the following results due to the 'edge effect' between cold and warm ocean waters? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.a: Development of dense phyto-plantons rich in diatoms.
options.b: Decrease in fish population in the area.
options.c: Decrease in mussel population in the area.
options.d: Decrease in primary productivity of the area.

solution:
(A) Development of dense phyto-plantons rich in diatoms.
When the warm and cold ocean waters meet, it leads to the formation of dense phyto-planktons rich in diatoms and creation of foggy conditions leading to difficulty in harbouring.

question_text:

Ascending order of salts in seawater - Calcium Carbonate < Calcium Sulphate < Magnesium Chloride.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:

Cold Ocean Currents - Benguela, Peru, Greenland, Oyashio, Labrador, Falkland, West Australian Drift, Canary, California (TRICK - Bengaluru Played GOLF World Cup in California)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text:
Which of the following statements with reference to Coriolis force is/are correct?
1. Coriolis force acts perpendicular to the pressure gradient force.
2. At the equator, the Coriolis force is zero and the wind blows perpendicular to the isobars.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Coriolis force is an imaginary force that deflects moving objects. It deflects winds rightwards in the Northern Hemisphere and leftwards in the Southern Hemisphere. It acts perpendicular to the pressure gradient force. It is maximum at poles and zero at equator (hence wind blows parallel to isobars).

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The Arabian Sea is connected to the Red Sea, through the Strait of Bab-el-Mandeb and the Gulf of Aden, and the Persian Gulf via the _______. [SSC JE 06/06/2024]

Amazon is the world's largest drainage basin. Nile is the longest river in the world. It is formed from the confluence of Blue Nile and White Nile (origin - Lake Victoria of Tanzania) and empties into Mediterranean sea. Congo river cuts the equator twice.

subject: World Core & Climate

Majuli is the world's largest inhabited riverine island. It is also the first island district of India. It is located on Brahmaputra river in Assam.

Regions with topmost citrus fruit production in the world are Mediterranean regions, Brazil, South Africa, China etc.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Copacabana beach is situated in _______. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]

subject: World Core & Climate

question_text: What is the name of the geographic feature that serves as a boundary between Europe and Africa? [SSC MTS 06/09/2023]

question_text: Which of the following oceans is surrounded by the continent of North America and South America to the east? [SSC MTS 08/09/2023]

Ocean in order of decreasing size - Pacific Ocean > Atlantic Ocean > Indian Ocean > Southern Ocean > Antarctic Ocean (TRICK - PAISA)

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Mid-latitude deserts (BWk) are found between 35° and _______° N and S, which are poleward of subtropical high-pressure areas. [SSC CHSL 02/08/2023]

question_text: Which of the following combinations of "animal" and "area" they are found" is INCORRECT? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The Sahara Desert is located in which of the following continents? [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]

question_text: In 'Andes Mountain' of South America, the animals used for transportation are _______. [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]

Mid-oceanic ridge - world's longest mountain chain.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: The world's longest river, the Nile, flows through which of the following continents? [SSC CGL 09/12/2022]
solution:

Nile is the world's longest river. It flows through the continent of Africa. It is formed from the confluence of Blue Nile and White Nile (origin - Lake Victoria of Tanzania) and empties into Mediterranean sea.

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: _______ is the only continent through which the Tropic of Cancer, the Equator, and the Tropic of Capricorn pass. [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]

subject: World Core & Climate
question_text: Which water body is adjacent to the Atlantic Ocean, located in the southeast of the Gulf of Mexico? [SSC CHSL 31/05/2022]

question_text: Which one among the following states was NOT impacted by the Land Boundary Agreement Treaty between India and Bangladesh (2015)? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(D) Arunachal Pradesh
The Land Boundary Agreement Treaty between India and Bangladesh demarcated boundary in three states - West Bengal, Assam and Tripura. So Arunachal Pradesh wasn't impacted by this treaty.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Himalayan Mountain range was formed by the collision of [SSC MTS 18/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Indian Plate and Eurasian Plate
The Himalayas are young fold mountains which were formed by the collision of Indian plate and Eurasian plate. Its core is made up of granitic rocks.
Other young fold mountains - Andes Mt. range (South America, highest peak - Mt. Aconcagua), Alps Mt. range (Europe, highest peak - Mt. Blanc), Rockies Mt. range (North America, highest peak - Mt. Mckinley/ Mt. Denali).
Old fold mountains - formed more than 250 million years ago. Gentle slopes and low altitudes due to denudation. Eg. Ural Mt. range, Appalachians, Aravalli.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which among the following glaciers is not located in Jammu & Kashmir? [SSC CHSL 13/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Milam
Milam glacier is located in Uttarakhand while Bhillan, Thajiwas, Prui are located in Jammu & Kashmir.
A glacier is a large, perennial mass of ice that moves slowly under its own weight.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following hills are NOT part of the Purvanchal hills? [SSC CHSL 15/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Patli hills
Purvanchal hills are a range of hills in the northeastern India in the states of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh and Nagaland. The important mountain ranges of Purvanchal hills are: Patkai Bum (Dihing river), Naga hills (Doyang and Dhansiri rivers), Manipur hills, Mizo/Lushai hills (Muhuri river), Barail range (Barak river).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which among the following is the largest of all the duns of the Shiwaliks mountain range in India? [SSC CHSL 15/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Dehra Dun
Duns are the longitudinal valleys lying between Shiwaliks and lesser Himalayas. Dehra Dun is the largest dun.
Do you know that Duars are alluvial floodplains located south of Shiwaliks. These are good for tea cultivation?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following mountain peaks is located in the Karakoram Range? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(D) K₂
Trans Himalayas - Karakoram range - highest peak - K₂/Godwin Austin (8,611m, world's second highest peak) Ladakh range - high slope Zanskar range
Glaciers of Karakoram range - Siachen (2nd longest non polar glacier of the world, highest battlefield, operation Meghdoot 1984), Baltoro (Tajikistan), Hisper, Diafo.
Shyok river flows between Karakoram and Ladakh range. Indus flows between Ladakh and Zanskar.
Highest peaks of the world - Mt. Everest (8848 m) > K₂ (8611 m) > Kangchenjunga (8598 m) > Lhotse (8516 m).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Himalayas have uplifted from which of the following geosynclines? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Tethys geosynclines
The Himalayas have uplifted from Tethys geosyncline. Tethys sea was an ancient sea between Gondwanaland and Laurasia before the uplift of Himalayas.
India was a part of Gondwanaland and holds 2.4% of the world's geographical area.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the name of the physical feature that is the longitudinal valley lying between the Lesser Himalayas and the Siwalik in India? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Duns
Duns are the longitudinal valleys lying between Shiwaliks and lesser Himalayas. Dehra Dun is the largest dun.
Doab - land between two rivers
Bhabhar - It is a narrow belt (8-16 km) lying south of Shiwaliks. Rivers deposit big stones and pebbles here and hence disappear. It is not fit for agriculture.
Terai - Area south of Bhabhar, without properly demarcated channel, streams of river reappear here, marshy area, lush green vegetation, fit for agriculture.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The _______ is famous for 'Karewa' formations. [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Kashmir Himalayas
Kashmir Himalayas - lies between Great and Lesser Himalayas. Karewa formation (glacial deposits, lacustrine deposits), Zafraan (local variety of saffron). Lakes - Dal lake (freshwater), Wular lake (freshwater), Pangong Tso (saltwater), Tso Moriri (saltwater). Passes - Banihal (Pir Panjal), Photi La (Zanskar), Khardung La (Ladakh range).
Do you know that Saffron has acquired GI tag in 2020?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Rama experiences a drop in temperature as she is getting higher and higher in the mountains while trekking. What could be the cause of this? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Lower air pressure at altitude
As the height increases, the air pressure decreases and the less dense air holds less heat so temperature also decreases.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which sub-division of Himalayas are famous for Karewa formations? [SSC CHSL 01/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Kashmir Himalayas
See solution of question 9 (level 1)
Uttarakhand/Himachal Himalayas - between Kali and Ravi rivers. Bhotia tribe, summer grassland - Bugyal, important hill stations - Dharamshala, Mussoorie, Shimla.
Arunachal Himalayas - jhuming cultivation, important peaks - Kangtu and Namcha Barwa, important rivers - Kameng, Subansiri, Dibang, tribes - Monpa, Abor, Mishmi, Nyishi, Naga.
Sikkim/Darjeeling Himalayas - Mt. Kanchenjunga, tribe - Lepcha, Bhutia, absence of Shiwaliks - Duars.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Himalayas' outermost range is called _______. [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Shiwaliks
Shiwaliks (height - 1,000 m, width 10-15 km) are the outermost range of Himalayas. In the eastern Himalayas gets replaced by Duars (soft unconsolidated deposits) - good for tea cultivation.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following hills is located in the easternmost part of India? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Patkai Bum Hills
Patkai Bum hills are located in the easternmost part of India along the Indo-Myanmar border.
Order of Purvanchal hills from North to South - Patkai Bum, Naga hills, Manipur hills, Mizo/Lushai hills.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Lushai Hills are also known as _______. [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Mizo hills
Lushai hills are also known as Mizo hills.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Siachen in Himalayas is: [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(A) A glacier
Siachen is a glacier in the Karakoram range of Himalayas.
Regional divisions of Himalayas :
Punjab Himalayas - between Indus and Sutlej
Kumaon Himalayas - between Sutlej and Kali
Nepal Himalayas - between Kali and Teesta
Assam Himalayas - between Dihang and Teesta

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The holy mountain 'Kailash' is located in which neighbouring country of India? [SSC MTS 01/09/2023]
solution:
(D) China
Mount Kailash is located in China.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following hills are located in Purvachal? [SSC MTS 12/09/2023]
solution:
(A) Mizo hills and Naga hills
Purvanchal hills are a range of hills in the northeastern India in the states of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh and Nagaland. The important mountain ranges of Purvanchal hills are :- Patkai Bum, Naga hills, Manipur hills, Mizo/Lushai hills.
Shevroy hills and Javadi hills - part of Eastern Ghats, Khasi and Jaintia hills - part of Meghalaya plateau, Anai Mudi and Doda Betta - part of Western Ghats.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which mountain range from the following is associated with Rajasthan state? [SSC MTS 12/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Aravalli Range
Aravalli range is old fold mountains spreading across Gujarat, Rajasthan, Delhi, Haryana. Highest peak - Guru Shikhar (Mt. Abu). Dilwara Jain temple is situated in Mt. Abu.
Other old fold mountains - Ural Mt. range (separates Asia and Europe), Appalachians (North America).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Identify the southernmost range of the Himalaya from the following. [SSC MTS 14/09/2023]
solution:
(A) Shiwaliks Range
Shiwaliks are the outermost or southernmost range of Himalayas.
Division of Himalayas :
Great Himalayas/Himadri/Inner Himalayas - westernmost point - Nanga Parwat, easternmost point - Namcha Barwa, average height - 6,000 m
Highest peaks - Mt. Everest (8,848 m), K₂/Godwin Austin (8611m), Mt. Kanchenjunga (8,598 m, highest in India), Mt. Lhotse (8,516 m), Dhaulagiri (8,167 m), Mt. Makalu (8485 m), Annapurna (8091 m), Nanda Devi (7816 m, highest in Uttarakhand), Mt. Kamet (Uttarakhand).
Lesser/Middle/Himachal Himalayas - average height 4,000 m, width 60-80 km, local names - Pir Panjal (J & K) Dhauladhar (Himachal Pradesh), Nagtibha (Uttarakhand), Mahabharat (Nepal)
Shiwaliks - outermost range of Himalayas

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Mount Jopuno is situated in which of the following states? [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which part of the Indian Himalayas does its highest mountain peak, Kanchenjunga, lie? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
