Kanchenjunga lies in the Darjeeling and Sikkim Himalayas.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a type of a fold mountain? [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(B) The Rhine Valley
Rhine valley is a block mountain, not fold mountain, located in Germany.
Block mountains are created because of faulting on a large scale. Eg. Rhine valley, Vosges mountain (France), Caucasus mountains, Vindhya, Satpura, Harz mountain (Germany).
Horst - uplifted part, Graben - lowered blocks
Aravali, Appalachians, Ural mountains are examples of old fold mountains.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The approximate length of the Great Himalayan range, also known as the central axial range, is _______ km from east to west. [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]
solution:
(B) 2500
The approximate length of Great Himalayan range, also known as the central axial range is 2,500 km from east to west. The Himalayas consist of - Great Himalayas, Middle Himalayas, Shiwaliks.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the average height of inner Himalayas? [SSC CHSL 21/03/2023]
solution:
(A) 6000 metres
The average height of Inner / Great Himalayas / Himadri is 6,000 m. The average height of Middle Himalayas is 4,000 m and that of Shiwaliks is 1,000 m. Westernmost point - Nanga Parwat
Easternmost point - Namcha Barwa (syntaxial banding)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Asia is separated from Europe by _______ mountains. [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Ural
Ural mountains (old fold mountain) separates Asia from Europe.
Andes mountains - located along the entire west coast of South America. Highest peak - Mt. Aconcagua.
Alps mountains - most extensive mountains of Europe. Highest peak - Mont Blanc (4806 m).
Andes mountains - along the west coast of North America. Highest peak - Mt. Elbert.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following have mountain range young fold mountains? [SSC CGL 13/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Alps mountain range
Alps are young fold mountains while Appalachians, Aravali and Ural are old fold mountains.
Other young fold mountains - Andes Mt. range (South America, highest peak - Mt. Aconcagua), Alps Mt. range (Europe, highest peak - Mt. Blanc), Rockies Mt. range (North America, highest peak - Mt. Mckinley/ Mt. Denali).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The uplifted blocks of block mountains are called _______. [SSC MTS 14/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Horsts
The uplifted blocks of block mountain are called Horsts while the lowered blocks are called Graben.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following mountains is formed as a result of the compression of tectonic plates, leading to the formation of large fold-like structures on the Earth's crust? [SSC CHSL 25/05/2022]
solution:
(A) Ural mountain in Russia
Ural mountains are example of old fold mountains which were formed as a result of the compression of tectonic plates. Vosges and Harz mountains are examples of block mountains. Kilimanjaro in Africa is an example of volcanic mountain.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following mountains were formed when molten rock from the depths of the earth rose from the crust and piled up on its own? [SSC CHSL 31/05/2022]
solution:
(A) Mount Kilimanjaro in Africa
Mt. Kilimanjaro is the highest peak of Africa and is an example of volcanic mountain which was formed due to eruption of molten rocks.
Other volcanic mountains - Mt. Stromboli (lighthouse of Mediterranean), Mt. Fujiyama (Japan), Mt. Ojas del Salado (Chile-Argentina border), Mt. Cotopaxi (Equador), Barren & Narcondam (Andaman & Nicobar).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Correctly match the following peaks of the Himalayas to their heights. [SSC CHSL 06/06/2022]
Peak - Height in meters
1. Mt. Everest - a. 8168
2. Dhaulagiri - b. 8848
3. Kamet - c. 8078
4. Annapurna - d. 7756
solution:
(C) 1-b, 2-a, 3-d, 4-c
Mt. Everest - 8848 m, Dhaulagiri - 8167 m, Kamet - 7756 m, Annapurna - 8078 m

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Fotu La, the Great Mountain Pass, at an altitude of 4108 m above sea level, is the highest point of which Indian state/UT? [SSC CHSL 09/06/2022]
solution:
(A) Ladakh
Fotu La is a pass in Ladakh.
Other passes - Karakoram (highest pass), Burzil (Kashmir to Gilgit), Zoji La (Kashmir to Leh), Banihal, Pir Panjal, Umling La (highest motorable road).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is also known as the 'White Mountain'? [SSC CGL 11/04/2022]
solution:
(C) Dhaulagiri
Dhaulagiri name comes from Sanskrit word 'dhawal' meaning white/beautiful and 'giri' meaning mountain. Thus Dhaulagiri is also called 'White mountain'.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct regarding mountains? [SSC CGL 11/04/2022]
I. Mountains may be arranged in a line known as range.
II. The Aravali range in India is one of the oldest fold mountain systems in the world.
III. Mt. Kilimanjaro in South America is an example of Volcanic Mountain.
solution:
(A) Only I and II
Mountain range is a series or chain of mountains. Aravali range in India is one of the oldest fold mountains in the world. Mt. Kilimanjaro in Africa (not South America) is an example of volcanic mountains.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following Indian states is the Gorichen peak located? [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]
solution:
(B) Arunachal Pradesh
Gorichen peak is located in the Eastern Himalayas in the state of Arunachal Pradesh.
Highest peaks - Arunachal Pradesh - Kangtu (7090 m), Nagaland - Saramati peak, Manipur - Mt. Esii (Tenipu), Mizoram - Phawngpui (blue mountain), MP - Dhupgarh. Odisha - Deomali.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Mountaineers refer to altitudes above about _______ meters as the 'death zone'. [SSC CGL 19/04/2022]
solution:
(B) 8,000
Death zone in mountaineering is the height above which the pressure of oxygen is insufficient to sustain human life. This point is generally agreed as 8,000 m

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following mountain ranges is home to the second highest peak of the world, K2? [SSC MTS 02/11/2021]
solution:
(A) Karakoram Range
K2/Godwin Austin is the world's second highest peak and is located in Karakoram range.
General orientation of Himalayas - NW Himalayas - NW to SE, Arunachal - West to East, Purvanchal hills - North to South. Average width of Himalayas decreases from West to East.
Easternmost point of Himalayas - Namcha Barwa, Westernmost point of Himalayas - Nanga Parvat.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: _______ is created by the collision of convergent plate boundaries. [SSC CGL 16/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Mountain building
Convergent plate boundaries can create mountains, volcanoes, subduction zones. Mid-oceanic ridge is associated with divergent plate boundaries.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following Himalayan peaks is NOT located in Nepal? [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Kamet
Mt. Kamet (7756 m) is located in Uttarakhand.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: 'Pidurutalagala' is the highest mountain peak in _______. [SSC CHSL 15/04/2021]
solution:
(A) Sri Lanka
Pidurutalagala (Mt. Pedro) is the highest mountain peak in Sri Lanka.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Kumaon Himalayas lie between: [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(B) Satluj River and Kali River
Regional divisions of Himalayas :
Punjab Himalayas - between Indus and Sutlej.
Kumaon Himalayas - between Sutlej and Kali.
Nepal Himalayas - between Kali and Teesta.
Assam Himalayas - between Teesta and Brahmaputra.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statements about river valleys is/are correct?
1. Kullu Valley is an example of strike valley.
2. Kangra Valley is a transverse valley.
3. Narmada flows in a rift valley.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(D) 3 only
Strike valley is the valley that develops parallel to the strike of the underlying rock strata. Transverse valley is the valley that cuts across a ridge or mountain range, generally running at right angle to the main mountain chain. Rift valley is a lowland region that forms when the tectonic plates move apart. Kullu valley is a transverse valley which is formed when the Beas river cuts through the Himalayas. Kangra valley is a strike valley. Narmada flows in a rift valley between Vindhya and Satpura ranges.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following pairs:
Peak - Mountains
1. Namcha Barwa - Garhwal Himalaya
2. Nanda Devi - Kumaon Himalaya
3. Nokrek - Sikkim Himalaya
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(B) 2 only
Namcha Barwa is in Arunachal Himalaya. Nanda Devi is in Garhwal Himalayas. Nokrek (highest peak of Garo hills) is in Sikkim Himalaya.
Nokrek is also a biosphere reserve in Meghalaya. Total biosphere reserves in India - 18. Categorised by UNESCO under Man and Biosphere (MAB) programme.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Amarkantak Hills are at the confluence of Vindhya and Sahyadri Ranges.
2. Biligirirangan Hills constitute the easternmost part of Satpura Range.
3. Seshachalam Hills constitute the southernmost part of Western Ghats.
How many of the statements given above are correct? [IAS 2023]
solution:
(D) None
Amarkantak hills are at the confluence of Vindhya and Satpura ranges. It is the origin of Narmada, Mahanadi and Son. Biligirirangan hills (BR hills) aren't the part of Satpura range but are a part of Eastern Ghats. Seshachalam hills (Tirumala hills) are part of Eastern ghats.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following information: [IAS 2024]
Region - Name of the mountain range - Type of mountain
1. Central Asia - Vosges - Fold mountain
2. Europe - Alps - Block mountain
3. North America - Appalachians - Fold mountain
4. South America - Andes - Fold mountain
In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
solution:
(B) Only two
France - Vosges - Block mountain
Europe - Alps - Fold mountain (young)
North America - Appalachians - Fold mountain (old)
South America - Andes - Fold mountain (young)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Malwa Plateau is located in which of the following states? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Madhya Pradesh
Malwa plateau is located in the states of Gujarat, Rajasthan and MP. It is made up of lava (basaltic rock). Rivers flowing through Malwa plateau - Chambal, Betwa, Sindh, Ken. Malwa plateau is covered by Black soil (good for cotton cultivation) and it is wider in the east and narrower in the west.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Palamu plateau is located in which of the following states? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Jharkhand
Palamu plateau is located in Jharkhand.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following regions of India is the Mundari language spoken? [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Chota-Nagpur plateau
Mundari language is spoken in the region of Chota-Nagpur plateau which spreads across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha and Bihar. This region is home to Munda tribe which speaks Mundari language.
Chota Nagpur plateau (Ruhr state) - famous for minerals. 3 important plateaus - Ranchi plateau, Hazaribagh plateau and Koderma plateau. Highest peak - Parsavnath (1365 m). Jadugoda mines - famous for Uranium. River flowing through rift valley - Damodar river (eastern side).
Do you know that Kosi river is known as 'sorrow of Bengal'?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which direction is the general elevation of the peninsular plateau of India? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]
solution:
(C) From West to East
Peninsular plateau is a table land composed of old, crystalline, igneous and metamorphic rocks. Its general elevation is from west to east as indicated by the flow of majority of rivers. Average height of peninsular plateau - 600-900 m. Largest plateau of India - Deccan plateau.
Do you know that it is difficult to setup ports on the Eastern coastal plains as large number of rivers form delta there?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The peninsular plateau of India was a part of which continent earlier? [SSC CHSL 01/01/2024]
solution:
(A) Africa
The peninsular plateau was a part of Gondwanaland which included regions of Africa, South America, Australia, Antarctica and India.
The peninsular plateau can be divided into three broad groups - The Deccan Plateau, The Central Highlands, The Northeastern Plateau.
Central Highlands - north of Narmada river, covered by Vindhya, Satpura to the south and Aravali to the NW.
Main plateaus - Malwa plateau (western side), Chota nagpur plateau (eastern side).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Deccan Plateau is a _______ landmass that lies to the south of the river Narmada. [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Triangular
Deccan plateau is a triangular landmass lying south of Narmada. Northern border - Satpura, Eastern border - Mahadev, Kaimur, Maikal range; Northeast boundary - Rajmahal hills. Extension of deccan plateau is in Northeast - Meghalaya plateau (Garo, Khasi, Jaintia hills), Karbi Anglong plateau and North Cachar hills (Assam).
Malda fault/Malda gap separated entire part of Deccan plateau from Karbi Anglong plateau.
Do you know that Cherrapunji, Mawsynram (highest rainfall in India), Shillong are located in the Khasi hills?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The western Ghats and the Eastern Ghats meet at which of the following places in southern India? [SSC CHSL 02/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Nilgiri Hills
Western ghats and Eastern ghats meet at Nilgiri hills.
Western Ghats - continuous, higher than eastern ghats, stretch from Tapi to south of Nilgiri hills, highest peak - Anaimudi (Anaimalai hills, Kerala, 2695 m), 2nd highest peak - Doddabetta (2637 m, on Nilgiri hills, Ooty is here), southernmost hills - Cardamom hills.
Eastern Ghats - discontinuous, irregular, stretched from Mahanadi valley to Nilgiri, highest peak - Mahendragiri (1501 m)/ Jindagada (1690 m), Shevroy and Javadi hills are located to the southeast of it.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Shevaroy Hills are located in which state of India? [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Tamil Nadu
Shevaroy hills are located in Tamil Nadu. They are part of Eastern ghats.
Nagari hills - Andhra Pradesh. Nilachal hills (Kamakhya temple) - Guwahati.
Other hills of Eastern Ghats - Ramgarh hills, Odisha hills, Northern Circars, Nallamala hills, Venukonda hills, Palkonda hills, Nagari hills, Javadi hills, Shevaroy hills, Panchaimalai hills, Sirumali hills, Palani hills, Varushnad hills.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which among the following is the highest peak of West Bengal? [SSC MTS 12/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Sandakphu
Sandakphu peak is the highest peak of West Bengal, located in the district of Darjeeling.
Highest peak of MP - Dhupgarh (located on Mahadeo hills)
Highest peak of Aravali - Guru Shikhar (1722m, situated in Mt. Abu hills)
Do you know that Panchmarhi hills are also known as 'Queen of Satpura'?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The rock shelters of Bhimbetka are in the foothills of which mountains? [SSC MTS 16/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Vindhyan
The rock shelters of Bhimbetka are in the foothills of Vindhyan range.
Vindhya - elevation 400-600 m, highest peak - Sadbhavna Shikhar/ Kalumar peak/ peak of goodwill (752 m). Panna (MP) - famous for diamonds.
Panna is one of the 18 biosphere reserves in India. It is also recognised under Man and Biosphere programme (MAB) of UNESCO.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which among the following peaks is the highest peak of Jharkhand? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Parasnath
The highest peak of Jharkhand is Parasnath 1365 m (23rd Jain tirthankar).
Parasnath is a part of chota nagpur plateau.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]

options.a: Mahendragiri is the highest peak in the Eastern Ghats.
options.b: The height of the Western Ghats progressively decreases from north to south.
options.c: The Western Ghats cause orographic rain.
options.d: The Eastern Ghats stretch from the Mahanadi Valley to the Nilgiris in the south.

solution:
(B) The height of the Western Ghats progressively decreases from north to south.
Mahendragiri is the highest peak of Eastern Ghats.
The height of Western Ghats increases from north to south.
Western Ghats cause orographic rainfall.
Eastern Ghats stretch from the Mahanadi valley to the Nilgiri in the South.
Passes - Bhorghat - Mumbai to Pune, Thalghat - Mumbai to Nasik, Pal ghat/Palakkad gap - Annamalai to Nilgiri.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Dhinodhar Hills is located in which Indian state/Union Territory? [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
solution:
(C) Gujarat
Dhinodhar hills are located in Gujarat. Shatrunjay hills are also located in Gujarat.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Kalsubai peak is located in which of the following Indian states? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Maharashtra
Kalsubai peak is located in Maharashtra and is the highest peak of Maharashtra.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following Indian states will you find the Netarhat Mountains, also called 'Queen of Chotanagpur'? [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]
solution:
(B) Jharkhand
Netarhat mountains also known as 'Queen of Chotanagpur' is a hill station in Jharkhand.
Do you know that Leh is the highest hill station?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is a mountain peak that rises above the surrounding peaks at the mountainous border of the Indian state of Nagaland and the Sagaing region of Myanmar? [SSC MTS 26/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Mount Saramati
Mount Saramati rises above the surrounding peaks at the border of Nagaland and sagaing region of Myanmar.
Do you know that Andaman & Nicobar islands are extension of Arankanyoma hills of Myanmar?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following hills is situated in Assam? [SSC CHSL 15/04/2021]
solution:
(B) Karbi Anglong Hills
Karbi Anglong hills are situated in Assam. North Cachar hills - extension of Deccan plateau.
Aravali Hills - NW extension of Central Highlands, old fold mountains, residual mountains, spread across- Gujarat, Rajasthan, Delhi (Raisina hills), Haryana. Highest peak - Gurushikhar (1722 m, in Mt. Abu hills).
Do you know that Dilwara Jain temple is located in Mt. Abu hills?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following states is the Shatrunjaya Hills located? [SSC CHSL 10/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Gujarat
Shatrunjay hills are located in Gujarat. Highest peak of Gujarat is Mt. Girnar (1117 m) located in Junagadh district.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Borra Caves are situated on the East Coast of India in which of the following hills? [SSC CGL 03/03/2020]
solution:
(D) Ananthagiri Hills
Borra caves are located in Ananthagiri hills in Araku valley of Andhra Pradesh.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Choose the correct statements about the Western Ghats. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
I. The height of the Western Ghats progressively increases from north to south.
II. Mahendragiri is the highest peak in the Western Ghats.
III. The Western Ghats are higher than the Eastern Ghats.
IV. The height of the Western Ghats progressively decreases from north to south.
solution:
(A) I and III
Western Ghats - Height increases from north to south. Height is more than the Eastern Ghats.
Mahendragiri is the highest peak of Eastern Ghats not of Western Ghats.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is the longest Highway Tunnel in India (as of 2022)? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Atal Tunnel
Atal tunnel (earlier known as Rohtang tunnel) is the longest (9.02km) highway tunnel of India above 10,000 ft. It connects Manali to Lahaul and Spiti valley.
Dr. Shyama Prasad Mukherjee tunnel (Chenani Nashri tunnel) is the longest (9.28km) highway tunnel in India.
Jawahar tunnel (Banihal pass) connects J&K and Srinagar.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a subdivision of the Meghalaya plateau? [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Dhansiri
Meghalaya plateau is further divided into Garo, Khasi and Jayanatya hills.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following Indian states is NOT covered by the Deccan Plateau? [SSC CHSL 08/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Madhya Pradesh
Deccan plateau spread across the states of Telangana, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, Chattisgarh, Tamil Nadu.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is a mountain pass in the Pir Panjal range at a maximum altitude of 2832 meters that connects the Kashmir Valley in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir with the Outer Himalayas and the plains in the south? [SSC CHSL 26/05/2022]
solution:
(B) Banihal Pass
Banihal pass is a mountain pass in the Pir Panjal range at an altitude of 2832 m that connects Kashmir valley with outer Himalayas and the plains in the south.
Lipu Lekh pass - Uttarakhand, at the tri-junction of India, Nepal and China.
Rohtang pass - connects Manali and Leh.
Mana pass - land route to Kailash and Mansarovar.
Bara-Lacha pass - connects Lahaul and Spiti to Leh.
Zojila pass - connects Kashmir to Leh.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT true about plateaus? [SSC MTS 11/10/2021]

options.a: The Deccan plateau is one of the oldest plateaus in India.
options.b: A plateau is an elevated flat land.
options.c: Plateaus are rich in mineral deposits.
options.d: The Marwar plateau is the highest plateau in the world.

solution:
(D) The Marwar plateau is the highest plateau in the world.
A plateau is a flat, elevated landform that rises sharply above the surrounding area. Plateaus are rich in mineral deposits. Deccan plateau is one of the oldest plateaus in India. Tibetan plateau is the highest plateau in the world.
It plays a major role in bringing rain to India.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following mountains is situated in the state of Uttarakhand? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Mangsha Dhura Pass
Mangsha Dhura pass is located in Uttarakhand and connects Uttarakhand to Tibet.
Nathu La Pass - Sikkim, Baralacha La pass - Himachal Pradesh, Bomdi La pass - Arunachal Pradesh.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Garo, Khasi and Jaintia hills are a subdivision of: [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Meghalaya plateau
Garo, Khasi and Jaintia hills are subdivision of Meghalaya plateau.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following is a part of Deccan Plateau? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(B) Karbi Anglong Hills
Karbi Anglong hills are part of Deccan plateau. It is separated from Chota nagpur plateau by Malda gap.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In India, rigid and stable elevated lands, denuded rocks and series of scarps are the features of which of the following? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(B) Peninsular plateau
Rigid and stable elevated lands, denuded rocks and series of scarps are the features of Peninsular plateau.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following statements about physical features of India:
1. The southern point of the Peninsular Plateau is formed by the Nilgiri Hills where the Eastern and the Western Ghats meet.
2. The Cardamom Hills may be regarded as a continuation of the Eastern Ghats.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Nilgiri hills are the meeting point of Western and Eastern ghats. The Cardamom hills are the extension of Western Ghats. Southernmost hill - Cardamom hills.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the name of the northern part of the western coastal plain between Mumbai and Goa? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Konkan coast
Coastline of India can be divided into Western coastal plain and Eastern coastal plain.
Western coastal plain can be further divided into - Kutch peninsula, Kathiawar peninsula, Konkan coast, Kannad coast (central coast), Malabar coast (Kayals - backwaters).
Eastern coastal plain can be divided into - Baiga plains, Utkal plain, Northern Circars, Coromandel coast.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which is the largest part of the northern plains and is formed of older alluvium? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Bhangar
Bhangar is the largest part of the northern plains and is formed of older alluvium.
Northern plain can be divided into 3 parts - Bhabar, Terai, Alluvial flood plain (Khadar and Bhangar).
Bhabar - narrow belt (8-16 km) lying south of Shiwaliks, not fit for agriculture, streams of river disappear here, big stones and pebbles are seen here.
Terai - 10-20 km, without properly demarcated channel, streams of river reappear, marshy area, lush green vegetation seen here, fit for agriculture.
Khadar - new deposition, renews every year, better fertility.
Bhangar - old deposition, more calcareous concentration (kankad).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The central stretch of the Western Coastal Plains of India is known as the: [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Kannad plains
The central stretch of the Western Coastal plains is called Kannad coast. It stretches from Goa to Mangalore.
Konkan coast lies to the north of Kannad plains while Malabar coast lies to the south of Kannad plains.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Choose the coasts that are running along the Arabian sea.
I. Northern Circars, II. Malabar, III. Konkan, IV. Coromandel
Choose the correct options: [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Only II and III
The coasts that are running along the Arabian sea are the Western coasts (Kutch peninsula, Kathiawar peninsula, Konkan coast, Kannad coast, Malabar coast). Northern Circars and Coromandel coast are part of Eastern coastal plains which run along the Bay of Bengal.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following physical divisions of India is densely populated? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2023]
solution:
(A) The North Indian Plains
The North Indian Plains are densely populated because of fertile soil, abundant water and favourable climate which support agriculture. It spans across area of 7 lakh square km and covers states of Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal, and Assam. They are formed by the alluvial deposits of three major rivers - Indus, Ganga and Brahmaputra.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is true about the Indian Desert? [SSC CHSL 17/08/2023]

options.a: Bounded to the west by the Aravali
options.b: Lies to the southeast of the Aravali
options.c: Lies to the northwest of the Aravali
options.d: Bounded to the south by the Aravali

solution:
(C) Lies to the northwest of the Aravali
Thar desert (marusthali) is a tropical desert located in the NW of Aravali. Rainfall here is less than 150 mm/year.
Relief features - Barchans, Seif, Mushroom rocks, Pedestrial rocks, Oasis, Playas.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following Indian states is biggest in terms of area? [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(A) Rajasthan
Biggest state (area) - Rajasthan, Smallest state (area) - Goa, Biggest state (population) - UP, Smallest state (population) - Sikkim.
Biggest UT (area) - Ladakh, Smallest UT (area) - Lakshadweep, Biggest UT (population) - Delhi, Smallest UT (population) - Lakshadweep.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: India's total length of coastline of the mainland including Andaman and Nicobar and Lakshadweep is _______. [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(C) 7516.6 km
Total coastline of India (including Andaman & Nicobar island is 7516.6 km while coastline of mainland India is 6100 km. Coastline states (9) - Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, West Bengal. Coastal UTs - Andaman & Nicobar, Lakshadweep, Daman & Diu, Puducherry.
Largest coastline - state - Gujarat (1600 km), UT - Andaman & Nicobar (1962 km). Shortest coastline - Goa.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The plains from Ambala to the Sunderban stretch over nearly 1800 km, but the fall in its slope is hardly _______ metres. [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(B) 300
The plains from Ambala to the Sunderban stretch over nearly 1800 km, but the fall in its slope is hardly 300 metres.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Where does the East Coast of India lie? [SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]

options.a: Between Eastern Ghats and the Bay of Bengal
options.d: Between Gulf of Cambay and Cape Comorin

solution:
(A) Between Eastern Ghats and the Bay of Bengal
The Eastern Coast of India lies between the Eastern Ghats and the Bay of Bengal.
Eastern coast - wider continental shelf, emerging, form delta
Western coast - narrow in middle and wider in the ends (dumbbell shaped), submerging, no delta, formation of Kayals (backwater), port development easy.
Do you know that harbouring is difficult in the eastern coast, that is why we see less number of ports on the eastern coast compared to the western coast?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following factors are responsible for railways having not developed much in the north-eastern parts of India?
1. Highly dissected topography, 2. Dense forests, 3. Low population density, 4. Heavy rainfall and frequent flooding
Select the correct code: [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 4
Reasons for railways not developing properly in the NE parts of India - highly dissected topography, dense forests, heavy rainfall and frequent flooding.
Do you know that the first train in India ran in 1853 from Bombay to Thane (34 km). It was run operated by Great Indian Peninsula Railway?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Why do people living on the Konkan coast NOT encounter extremes of temperature and seasonal rhythm? [SSC CHSL 30/05/2022]

options.b: Location on leeward side of Sahyadri

solution:
(C) Coastal location
Konkan coast doesn't experience extremes of temperature and seasonal rhythm because of its coastal location and hence moderating influence of sea plays its role. This phenomena is called Continentality (places located in the interior of continents experience extremes of climate while those located at coast are affected by the moderating influence of sea).
Undulating landform - continuous ups and downs.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: With reference to the major regions of the northern plains, Bhabar is: [SSC CHSL 09/09/2022]

options.a: the older alluvium along the river beds forming terraces higher than the floodplain.
options.b: a narrow belt of about 8 to 16 km that runs in the west-east direction along the foot of the Himalayas from the river Indus to Teesta.
options.c: composed of newer alluvium and forms flood plains along the river banks.
options.d: a broad long zone of northern India and southern Nepal running parallel to the lower ranges of the Himalayas.

solution:
(B) A narrow belt of about 8-16 km that runs in the west-east direction along the foot of the Himalayas from the river Indus to Teesta.
See solution of question 72 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following regions of India is the natural habitat of White-bellied Heron largely distributed? [SSC MTS 05/10/2021]
solution:
(A) North-east
White-bellied Heron has its habitat in Northeast India. It has been listed as Critically Endangered on the IUCN red list.
IUCN (International Union for the Conservation of Nature) established in 1948 maintains a red list which is the world's most comprehensive of the global conservation status of biological species.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following locations has the highest altitude? [SSC CGL 16/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Ranikhet
Among the given options, Ranikhet is located at the highest altitude (1869 m).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Nathu La Pass connects India with which of its neighbouring countries? [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution:
(D) China
Nathu La pass connects Sikkim (India) with the Tibet Autonomous Region (China).
Jelep La pass also connects Sikkim (India) with the Tibet Autonomous Region (China).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which natural port is located along the west coast of India? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Marmagao
Marmagao port (Goa) is located along the west coast of India.
PORTS OF INDIA :
Major - 13 (12 government owned - owned and managed by Central government, 1 privately owned - Ennore).
Minor - 200 (owned by state government).
Ports on west coast - Kandla/Deendayal Port Trust (tidal port, Gujarat); Mumbai port; JNPT (Jawaharlal Nehru Port Trust) - also called Nhava Sheva port, largest container port; Mormugao port - Goa, in Zuari river estuary; Mangaluru - Karnataka; Kochi - queen of Arabian sea, Kerala.
Ports on east coast - Haldia port/ Diamond Harbour (West Bengal); Kolkata port/Shyama Prasad Mukherjee port (riverine port, oldest port 1870s); Paradip port (Odisha); Visakhapatnam port (India's deepest landlocked port); Ennore/Kamarajar port (privately owned port); Chennai port (2nd largest container port in India); Tuticorin port/V.O. Chidambaranar port.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which among the following is a western coast island? [SSC CHSL 26/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Elephanta
Elephanta island are located in Mumbai harbour, east of Mumbai.
Pamban island (Rameshwaram island) is the largest island of Tamil Nadu. It is located between Tamil Nadu and Sri Lanka on Ram Setu archipelago.
Parikud island is a island in Chilika lake (Odisha).
Sriharikota is a barrier island located in Andhra Pradesh.
Do you know that ISRO's Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) is located in Sriharikota?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Choose the correct statements about the islands of India [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
a) Lakshadweep Islands are composed of coral islands.
b) Andaman and Nicobar islands are composed of coral islands.
c) It is believed that Lakshadweep islands are an elevated portion of submarine mountains.
d) It is believed that Andaman and Nicobar islands are an elevated portion of submarine mountains.
solution:
(D) a and d
Lakshadweep - made of coral deposits, 36 islands, largest - Andrott.
Andaman & Nicobar - extension of Arakan Yoma (Myanmar), 572 islands, largest - Great Nicobar.
Tribes in Andaman - Negrito group (North Andamanese, Jarawa, Onge, Senthelese).
Tribes in Nicobar - Shompen, Nicobarese.
Islands renamed - Ross islands - Netaji Subhash Chandra island, Neil island - Shaheed dweep, Havelock island - Swaraj dweep.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which is the waterbody that separates Andaman Islands and Nicobar Islands? [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Ten Degree Channel
10° channel separates Andaman islands (Little Andaman) from Nicobar islands (Car Nicobar).
8° channel - separates Minicoy (Lakshadweep) from Maldives.
9° channel - separates entire group of Lakshadweep from Minicoy.
11° channel - separates Aminidivi from Cannanore.
Duncan passage - separates South Andaman from Little Andaman.
Coco channel - separates Myanmar and A & N.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Match the following ports with their related states correctly. [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]
Port - State
1. Mormugao Port - a. West Bengal
2. Paradip Port - b. Odisha
3. Haldia Port - c. Tamil Nadu
4. Tuticorin Port - d. Goa
solution:
(B) 1-d, 2-b, 3-a, 4-c
Mormugao port - Goa, Paradip port - Odisha, Haldia port - West Bengal, Tuticorin port - Tamil Nadu.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Atal Tunnel connects which two places? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Manali and Lahaul-Spiti valley
Atal tunnel (9.02 km, Rohtang tunnel) connects Manali with Lahaul-Spiti valley. It is the longest highway tunnel in the world.
Do you know that Umling La is the world's highest (5799 m) motorable pass. Earlier this record was held by Uturuncu volcano road, Bolivia (5777 m)?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: _______ is the deepest landlocked and well-protected port. [SSC MTS 15/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Visakhapatnam
Visakhapatnam is the deepest landlocked and hence well protected port. It is located in Andhra Pradesh.
Kandla port (Deendayal Port Trust) - Gujarat, tidal port. Paradip port - Odisha.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Vadhavan Port is located in _______. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Maharashtra
Vadhavan port is located in Maharashtra.
Vizhinjam International Seaport (Kerala) - first transhipment port.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following ancient ports was situated on the Malabar Coast? [SSC CPO 25/11/2020]
solution:
(B) Muziris
Muziris was an ancient port located in Kerala (Malabar coast). It was the port city of Cheras.
Tamralipti port was located in the state of West Bengal.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which ports belong to the western coast of India? [SSC CPO 04/10/2021]

options.a: Kandla port, Mormugao port, Kochi port
options.b: Kandla port, Mormugao port, Chennai port
options.c: Kandla port, Mormugao port, Kolkata port

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The stretch between Mumbai to Goa of western coast of India is known as _______. [SSC MTS 06/09/2023]
solution:

The stretch between Mumbai to Goa of western coast is known as Konkan coast.
See solution of question 71 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Where is Mount Diavolo, an important mountain peak in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, located? [SSC CHSL 05/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Middle Andaman
Mt. Diavolo is located in Middle Andaman. It is the third highest peak of Andaman & Nicobar.
Saddle peak (highest peak of A&N, 732m) - North Andaman, Port Blair - South Andaman
Mt. Thuillier (2nd highest peak) - Great Nicobar

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the geographic location of the world's largest coral reef, known as the Great Barrier Reef? [SSC MTS 05/09/2023]
solution:
(A) Australia
Great Barrier Reef (world's largest coral reef) is located on the NE coast of Australia.
Conditions favourable for coral reefs - Saline water, Sunlight, Clear water, Moderate temperature (30-35°C).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Little Andaman in the south is separated from the Nicobar Islands by the _______. [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
solution:
(B) 10° Channel
See solution of question 91 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following seaports gained significance for handling iron-ore exports to Japan? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(D) Mormugao
Mormugao port in Goa gained significance for handling iron-ore exports to Japan. It is India's premier port for exporting iron-ore.
Recall about all the major ports in India.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Streams and rivers coming from the mountains deposit heavy materials of rocks and boulders in _______. [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(C) Bhabar
Streams and rivers coming from mountains deposit heavy materials of rock and boulders in Bhabar. It isn't fit for agriculture and streams of rivers disappear here.
See solution of question 72 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Mount Thuillier (642 m) is an important mountain peak of Andaman and Nicobar Islands. It is located in: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(D) Great Nicobar
Mt. Thuillier is located in Great Nicobar.
Saddle peak (highest) - North Andaman, Mt. Thuillier (2nd highest) - Great Nicobar, Mt. Diavolo (3rd highest) - Middle Andaman.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Ten Degree Channel is found between: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(C) Little Andaman and Car Nicobar
10° channel separates Andaman islands (Little Andaman) from Nicobar islands (Car Nicobar).
Duncan passage - separates South Andaman and Little Andaman.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statements about the coasts of India is/are correct?
1. The West Coast of India is a high rocky, retreating coast.
2. The West Coast of India is dominated by erosional landforms.
3. The East Coast of India, however, is a low sedimentary coast exhibiting depositional forms.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
The eastern coast of India is dominated by depositional landforms because it is a low lying, sedimentary coast where rivers deposit large amount of sediments while the western coast is dominated by erosional landforms due to strong wave action of Arabian sea, aided by winds, currents, tides and storms.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following is not a mountain peak of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(C) Lhotse Peak
Lhotse peak (8516 m) is a peak between Tibet Autonomous region of China and Nepal.
Peaks of Andaman & Nicobar islands - Saddle peak (North Andaman) > Mt. Thuillier (Great Nicobar) > Mt. Diavolo (Middle Andaman).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Visakhapatnam Port is a landlocked harbour.
2. Deendayal Port is a tidal port.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Visakhapatnam port is a landlocked and the deepest port of India located in Andhra Pradesh.
Deendayal port (Kandla port) is a tidal port in Gujarat.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following is the only riverine major port in India? [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(C) Kolkata
The Kolkata port (Shyama Prasad Mukherjee port) is the only riverine major port located on Hooghly river. It is also the oldest port developed by Britishers in 1870s.
Mormugao port - Goa, Deendayal port (Kandla port) - Gujarat, Paradip port - Odisha.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists: [CAPF 2022]
List-I (Type of plain) - List-II (Characteristic feature)
a. Bhabar - 1. Marsh
b. Bhangar - 2. Silt
c. Khadar - 3. Gravels
d. Delta - 4. Kankar
solution:
(B) a-3, b-4, c-2, d-1
Bhabar - Gravels, Bhangar - Kankar, Khadar - Silt, Delta - Marsh.
See solution of question 72 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following statements about the seaports in India:
1. There are 4 major and 25 minor seaports in India.
2. The major ports are under the administrative control of the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways.
3. The minor ports are under the jurisdiction of respective State Maritime Board/State Government.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(D) 2 and 3 only
There are 13 major and nearly 200 minor ports in India. Major ports are under the administrative control of the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (Central government) while minor ports are under the control of respective state governments.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Great Northern Plains are formed by basins of three distinct river systems-the Indus, the Ganga and the Brahmaputra.
2. They are one of the most densely populated areas on Earth.
3. Between the Yamuna at Delhi and the Bay of Bengal, nearly 1600 km away, there is a drop of only 200 metres in elevation.
Which of the statements given above are correct? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(A) 1, 2 and 3
The Great Northern Plains (7 lakh square km) are formed by basins of three rivers - Indus, Ganga and Brahmaputra.
They are one of the most densely populated areas on Earth because fertile soil and abundant water resources from rivers support large population.
Between Yamuna at Delhi and Bay of Bengal, there is a drop of only 200 metres in elevation.
Ganga and Brahmaputra combine in Bangladesh and the combined stream is known as Meghna.
Yamuna meets Ganga at Prayagraj.
Origins - Indus - Bokhar Chu glacier, Ganga - Gaumukh, Brahmaputra - Chemayungdung glacier/Angsi glacier.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following is the deepest, inland and protected port on the east coast of India? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(D) Visakhapatnam
Visakhapatnam is the deepest, inland and protected port on the east coast of India.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following are called the 'Ports of Call'? [CISF AC 2022]

options.a: These ports have only strategic importance.
options.b: Ports where goods are bought for export from different countries.
options.c: Ports where ships used to anchor for refueling.
options.d: Ports which are exclusively concerned with transportation of passengers.

solution:
(C) Ports where ships used to anchor for refuelling.
Ports of call are the ports where ships stop during their journey from one place to another for refuelling. Eg. Aden, Honolulu, Singapore, and Cozumel.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
With reference to Lakshadweep, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is an archipelago consisting of coral islands and reefs.
2. It is the smallest Union Territory of India in terms of area.
3. The main occupation of people is tourism and allied activities.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(A) 1 and 2 only
Lakshadweep - made of coral deposits, 36 islands, largest - Andrott. It is the smallest UT of India.
The main occupation of people in Lakshadweep is fishing, coconut cultivation and coir twisting.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following statements regarding landscape:
1. Bhabar is a narrow belt ranging between 8 km - 10 km parallel to the Shivalik foothills at the break-up of the slope.
2. Bhabar is a belt consisting of old and new alluvial deposited by rivers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [CISF AC 2024]
solution:
(D) Neither 1 nor 2
Bhabar is a narrow belt ranging between 8-16 km (not 8-10 km) parallel to Shivalik foothills. It contains big stones and pebbles, hence it is not fit for agriculture.
Alluvial flood plain (not Bhabar) is a belt of old and new alluvium deposited by rivers.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists: [NDA II 2021]
List I (Place) - List II (Feature)
a. Mumbai - 1. Queen of the Arabian Sea
b. Visakhapatnam - 2. Biggest port of India
c. Chennai - 3. Land-locked harbour
d. Kochi - 4. Oldest port on the Eastern Coast
solution:
(B) a-2, b-3, c-4, d-1
Mumbai - biggest port of India, Visakhapatnam - land-locked harbour, Chennai - oldest port on the Eastern coast, Kochi - Queen of Arabian sea.
JNPT - largest container port.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Deendayal Port was earlier known as: [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(C) Kandla Port
Deendayal port was earlier known as Kandla port. It is a tidal port located in Gujarat.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following is the narrow belt parallel to the Shiwalik foothills where streams and rivers coming from the mountains deposit heavy materials of rocks and boulders? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(C) Bhabar
Bhabar - narrow belt (8-16 km) lying south of Shiwaliks, not fit for agriculture, streams of river disappear here, big stones and pebbles are seen here.
Terai - 10-20 km, without properly demarcated channel, streams of river reappear, marshy area, lush green vegetation seen here, fit for agriculture. Tropical deciduous forests are present in this region.
Khadar - new deposition, renews every year, better fertility.
Bhangar - old deposition, more calcareous concentration (kankad).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Identify the sea-port in India on the basis of the given characteristics: [NDA I 2024]
1. It is more than a century old port on the west coast of India.
2. It is a natural harbour protected by a breakwater and also by a mole.
3. A deep draft channel of about 14 metres depth permits large vessels to enter the harbour.
Select the correct sea-port from the options given below:
solution:
(A) Mormugao Port
Mormugao port is situated in Goa on the estuary of Zuari river. It is more than a century old port. It is a natural harbour protected by a breakwater and also by a mole. A deep draft channel of about 14 metres depth permits large vessels to enter the harbour.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Little Andaman is separated from Great Andaman by: [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(C) Duncan Passage
Little Andaman is separated from South Andaman (part of Great Andaman) by Duncan passage.
Homfray's strait - separates Baratang island from Middle Andaman.
Austen strait - separates North Andaman from Middle Andaman.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following types of coral reefs are developed along the edges of continental shelves and separated by lagoon? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(C) Barrier reefs
Barrier reefs are developed along the edges of continental shelves and separated by lagoon.
Fringing reefs - grow directly from a shore, located very close to land.
Atolls - roughly circular reef surrounding a large central lagoon.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one among the following biogeographic zones of India covers the largest area? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(C) Semi-arid region
Among the given options, semi-arid region covers the largest area of India (37%).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following pairs: [IAS 2023]
Port - Well known as
1. Kamarajar Port - First major port in India registered as a company
2. Mundra Port - Largest privately owned port in India
3. Visakhapatnam - Largest container port in India
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
solution:
(B) Only two pairs
Kamarajar port (Ennore port) - first major port in India registered as a company.
Mundra port - largest privately owned port in India (owned by Adani group).
Visakhapatnam port - deepest landlocked port in India.
JNPT - largest container port in India.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: An offshore terminal at Vadinar has been developed to reduce pressure from which of the following major ports in India? [NDA I 2025]
solution:
(A) Kandla Port
Major ports in India - 13 (12 government owned, 1 private).
Kandla Port (Deendayal Port Trust) - tidal port, offshore terminal at Vadinar has been developed to reduce pressure from it.
Minor ports in India - 200.
Recall all the major ports in India.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Chambal, Hindon and Tons Rivers are tributaries of the _______ river: [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Yamuna
Chambal, Hindon and Tons are tributaries of Yamuna.
Yamuna - 1370 km, origin - Banderpoonch peak, largest tributary of Ganga.
Tributaries of Yamuna - Chambal (origin - Mhow plateau), Sindh, Betwa, Ken, Tons (largest tributary).
Agra and Delhi are situated on the banks of Yamuna.
Do you know that Chambal is famous for its badland topography formed due to gully erosion?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which water body do several Himalayan tributaries like Shyok, Gilgit, Shigar, Gasting, and Dras meet and finally emerge from the hills near Attock where it receives the Kabul River? [SSC CHSL 01/08/2022]
solution:
(C) Indus river system
Rivers like Shyok, Gilgit, Shigar, Gasting and Dras meet in Indus river system.
Indus River :
Length - 2880 km (1114 km in India)
Origin - Bokhar Chu glacier near lake Mansarovar. Drains into - Arabian sea.
Flow - China - India - Pakistan (enters India through Demchok).
National river of Pakistan.
It is called Singi Khamban (lion's mouth) in Tibet.
Leh is located on the banks of Indus river.
Indus Water Treaty (1960) - between JL Nehru and Ayub Khan, mediator - World Bank, signed in Karachi
Indus, Jhelum, Chenab - 80% can be used by Pakistan and 20% by India.
Ravi, Beas, Satluj - 80% can be used by India and 20% by Pakistan.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which river flows into the Wular lake in the north and then into Baramulla? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Jhelum
Jhelum flows into Wular lake (largest freshwater lake) and then into Baramulla (city in the UT of J & K, called as the 'Gateway of Kashmir valley').
Jhelum (Vitasta) - origin - Verinag. Meanders in its youthful stage. Srinagar is located on its bank.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is a tributary of river Indus? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Shyok
Shyok is a tributary of Indus river. It originates from Rimo glacier.
Lohit and Dibang are tributaries of Brahmaputra while Betwa is a tributary of Yamuna.
Left bank tributaries of Indus - Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas, Satluj.
Right bank tributaries of Indus - Shyok, Gilgit, Kabul, Gomal.
Jhelum + Chenab + Ravi + Beas + Satluj = Panchnad. Panchnad meets Indus at Mithankot (Pakistan).
Indus + Jhelum - Sindh Sagar doab
Jhelum + Chenab - Jech doab
Chenab + Ravi - Rechna doab
Ravi + Beas - Bari doab
Beas + Satluj - Bist doab

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct about River Ganga? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]

options.a: River Ganga and its river basin support lots of wonderful wildlife, particularly river dolphins, otters, freshwater turtles, and gharials.
options.b: River Ganga in India is more than 2500 km long and is the most populated river basin in the world.
options.c: The water level of River Ganga never depends on monsoon and climatic condition of the Himalaya.
options.d: River Ganga flows from the Himalayas all the way to the Bay of Bengal.

solution:
(C) The water level of River Ganga never depends on monsoon and climatic condition of the Himalaya.
Ganga - 2525 km, national river of India (declared in 2008), longest river of India, Bacteriophage virus present (water remains fresh), passes through 5 states - Uttarakhand, UP (longest), Bihar, JH & K (shortest), West Bengal.
Ganga basin is the most populated river basin in the world because of fertile soll and abundant water.
Gangetic dolphin is the state animal of UP.
Do you know that Ganga is called Padma in Bangladesh?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which district of Uttarakhand does the Ramganga river originate from the Doodhatoli ranges? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Pauri Garhwal

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists: [CDS II 2022]
List-I (Drainage Pattern of River) - List-II (Feature)
a. Insequent - 1. Whose direction of flow is controlled by the rock structure
b. Consequent - 2. No apparent reason why it follows the path it takes
c. Subsequent - 3. Flow in the same direction as the consequent stream but at a lower level
d. Resequent - 4. Whose course is determined by the original slope of the land
solution:
(B) a-2, b-4, c-1, d-3
Insequent drainage pattern - doesn't correlate to topology (no apparent reason why it follows the path it takes), usually flow through a highly sloping surface.
Consequent drainage pattern - rivers which follow the general direction of slope. Eg. most of the peninsular rivers.
Subsequent drainage pattern - originates after consequent drainage pattern and follow the underlying geological structures like faults and ridges rather than slope of the land.
Resequent drainage pattern - this river flows in the same direction as that of consequent river but originates at a much later stage.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:

solution:

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which statements about the Indus Water Treaty are correct?

2. The term 'Western Rivers' means the Indus, the Jhelum, and the Chenab taken together.
Code: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Indus Water Treaty (1960) was signed between India (JL Nehru) and Pakistan (Ayub Khan) and was mediated by World Bank. According to it, 80% water of Eastern rivers (Ravi, Beas and Sutlej) was allocated to India and 20% was allocated to Pakistan while 80% water of Western rivers (Indus, Jhelum and Chenab) was allocated to Pakistan and 20% to India.
