Origin - Nag hills (Rajasthan), flows through Rajasthan, Gujarat
Only river that contains saline water and it doesn't reach upto oceans and ends in Rann of Kutchh.
It is an Endorheic river (flows into land basins and salt lakes and doesn't reach sea).
Exorheic river - a river that finally drains into an ocean, forming an open drainage system.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which river has its source in the Western Ghats range of Karnataka state? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Kaveri
Kaveri (800 km)
Origin - Brahmagiri hills (Kodagu district, Karnataka)
Flows through Karnataka, Tamil Nadu.
Only river of South India which flows throughout the year (perennial river). It is called Ganga of South India.
Tributaries - Hemvati, Kabini, Bhavani, Shimsha, Amaravathi, Arkavathi.
Delta - known as Garden of south India.
Banas origin - Aravalli range, Kosi origin - Gosain Dham, Sarayu (Ghaghra) origin - Mapchachungo glacier.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a major river basin in India? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Kalindi
Kalindi is not a major river basin in India. Kalindi is a river in West Bengal. Narmada (1312 km, flows in rift valley), Tapi (724 km), Krishna are major river basins in India. (Yamuna is also called Kalindi. So don't be confused as it is not recognised as a separate river basin).
Do you know that Surat is located on the banks of Tapi river?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which river is known as the sorrow of Bihar? [SSC CHSL 07/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Kosi
Kosi is called 'Sorrow of Bihar' as it causes frequent floods in Bihar. It meets Ganga in Katihar district of Bihar.
Mahanadi is called 'Sorrow of Odisha' while Damodar is called 'Sorrow of Bengal'. 'Sorrow of Gorakhpur' - Rapti
Damodar - origin - Chota Nagpur plateau, flows in rift valley, tributary of Hugli, tributaries - Bokaro, Barakar, Konar

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following pairs of Indian rivers is west flowing? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Narmada and Tapi
Most of the rivers of India are east flowing due to slope eastwards but some rivers are west flowing as they flow in rift valley.
West flowing rivers - Luni, Sabarmati, Mahi, Narmada, Tapi, Mandovi, Zuari, Sharavati, Bharatpuzha, Periyar.
East flowing rivers - Ganga, Yamuna, Godavari, Krishna, Kaveri, Mahanadi, Damodar etc.
East flowing rivers form deltas while west flowing rivers form estuaries.
Do you know that Jog falls/Gersoppa falls/Mahatma Gandhi falls are on the Sharavati river. Kunchikal fall (highest waterfall) - on Varahi river?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following rivers is an east flowing river? [SSC CGL 06/03/2020]
solution:
(D) Brahmani river
Brahmani is a east flowing river flowing primarily through the state of Odisha.
South Koel + Sankha = Brahmani river (joins Baitarni to form delta).
Origin of Baitarani - Gonasika/Guptaganga hills.
APJ Abdul Kalam island (Wheeler island) is near Dhamra river (formed by the confluence of Brahmani and Baitarani). Integrated Test Range of DRDO is located here.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The drainage basin of the Godavari river is present in which of the following states of India? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]

options.a: Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu
options.b: Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Karnataka, and Odisha
options.c: Chhattisgarh, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu
options.d: Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Odisha

solution:
(B) Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Karnataka and Odisha
Godavari (1465 km)
Origin - Trimbakeshwar plateau (Nasik, Maharashtra)
Basin spread across - Chhattisgarh, Odisha, MP, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh.
Largest river of South India (Dakshin Ganga).
Tributaries - Manjira, Sabri, Wainganga, Wardha, Indravati, Painganga, Purna, Pranhita. (TRICK - MSWWIPPP)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Eastern Ghats stretch from the _______ to the Nilgiris in the south. [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Mahanadi Valley
Eastern Ghats - average height - 600m, stretched from Mahanadi valley to Nilgiri hills, highest peak - Mahendragiri (1501 m), Jindagada (1690 m). They are not continuous.
Western Ghats - average height - 1200m, continuous and can be crossed though passes only, highest peak - Anaimudi (2695 m), 2nd highest peak - Doddabetta (2637 m)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which east-flowing river basin covers the states of Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh and Orissa and has a Drainage area of 39,033 km²? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Brahmail
Brahmani river covers the states of Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh and Odisha. Birupa (tributary of Mahanadi) joins Brahmani river. Brahmani river joins Bay of Bengal at Kendrapara where Bhitarkanika NP-WLS and Gahirmatha Marine Sanctuary are located.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the other name of Bharathapuzha, the longest river of Kerala, which flows from the west and falls into the Arabian Sea? [SSC CHSL 02/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Ponnani
Bharathapuzha river is also known as Nila or Ponnani river. It is a west flowing river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Yerla, Warna, and Dindi are tributaries of the _______ river system. [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Krishna
Yerla, Warna and Dindi are tributaries of Krishna river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: With a stretch of about 724 km, which river is one of the most important rivers of peninsular India? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Tapi
Tapi (724 km)
Origin - Satpura ranges/Betul plateau
Tributaries - Aner, Gomai, Girna, Purna, Bori, Girna, Arunawati
Flows through - MP, Maharashtra, Gujarat
Flows through a rift valley.
Surat is located on the banks of Tapi.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which river originates from the southern part of Devarayanadurga hill in Tumkur district, and flows for about 221 km before joining Kaveri? [SSC CHSL 04/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Shimsha river
Shimsha which is a tributary of Kaveri, originates from Deverayanadurga hill in Tumkur district.
Other tributaries of Kaveri - Hemvati, Kabini, Bhavani, Shimsha, Amaravathi, Arkavathi.
Dams on Kaveri - Mettur dam (Tamil Nadu), Shivsamudram (Karnataka).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following rivers originates near Sihawa in Raipur district of Chhattisgarh, flows through Odisha, and eventually empties into the Bay of Bengal? [SSC CHSL 17/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Mahanadi
Mahanadi originates in Sihawa hills of Chhattisgarh.
Mahanadi (850 km)
Sorrow of Odisha
Flows mainly in Chhattisgarh, Odisha
Hirakud dam is built on it. (longest dam in India 25.8 km)
Tributaries - Tel, Jonk, Ong, Hasdeo, Mand.
Distributary of Mahanadi Birupa river flows into Brahmani river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following states is NOT a part of the Tapi Basin? [SSC CPO 14/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Rajasthan
See solution of question 14 (level 1)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which group of rivers is a part of the peninsular drainage system? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Mahanadi and Godavari
Mahanadi and Godavari are a part of peninsular drainage system. Other rivers of peninsular drainage system are - Krishna, Kaveri, Tapi, Narmada, Damodar, Sabarmati etc.
Himalayan drainage system - Indus, Ganga, Brahmaputra.
Himalayan rivers are perennial rivers (water throughout the year) while perennial rivers are ephemeral (seasonal in nature). Himalayan rivers have long courses while perennial rivers have short courses.
Antecedent river - a river that has existed before the uplift of mountain or other geological feature and continues to flow through it. Most of the Himalayan rivers are antecedent rivers.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following rivers does NOT flow in Kerala State? [SSC CGL 26/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Penner
Penner (Pinakini) is an independently flowing river of Andhra Pradesh. It originates in Chikkaballapura (Karnataka).
Rivers of Kerala :- Bharatphuza (Ponnani), Periyar (lifeline of Kerala, longest river of Kerala), Pamba (drains into Vembanad lake).
Do you know that Vaigai is the southernmost river of India?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the name of the branch of river Godavari that joins the Bay of Bengal flowing through the Yanam enclave of the union territory of Puducherry? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Gautami
Gautami is the distributary of Godavari flowing through the Yanam enclave of the UT of Puducherry.
Do you know that Puducherry consists of 4 districts - Puducherry, Karaikal, Yanam, Mahe?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a tributary of the Godavari river? [SSC CGL 21/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Mahi
Tributaries of Godavari - Manjira, Sabri, Wainganga, Wardha, Indravati, Painganga, Purna, Pranhita (TRICK - MSWWIPPP).
Mahi is not a tributary of Godavari. It originates in Vindhya mountains. Flows through - MP, Rajasthan, Gujarat and then drains into Gulf of Khambat.
Do you know that Mahi crosses the Tropic of Cancer twice, Congo crosses equator twice and Limpopo crosses Tropic of Capricorn twice?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which river is the third largest in the peninsula of India and the largest river of Odisha state? [SSC MTS 03/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Mahanadi
Largest rivers of peninsular India - Godavari (1465 km) > Krishna (1400 km) > Mahanadi (900 km) > Kaveri (800 km).
Krishna (1400 km)
Origin - Mahabaleshwar (Sahyadri range)
Flows through - Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh.
Second longest river of South India.
Tributaries - Bhima, Tungabhadra, Ghatprabha, Malprabha, Musi, Koyna, Dhoodganga (TRICK - भीम तू मौसी को घाट दूध की माला)
Do you know that Hyderabad is situated on the banks of Musi river?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which is a major tributary of Bhagirathi river? [SSC MTS 18/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Bhilangna
Bhilangna is a major tributary of Bhagirathi which is a source of Ganga.
Origin of Alaknanda - Satopanth glacier, Origin of Bhagirathi - Gangotri (Goumukh glacier)
Alaknanda and Bhagirathi meet at Devprayag and form Ganga.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Jabalpur is located on the banks of which of the following rivers? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Narmada
Jabalpur is located on the banks of Narmada river. Dhuandhar fall is in Jabalpur.
Kanpur, Varanasi, Patna - banks of Ganga. Gwalior and Kota - banks of Chambal.
Narmada (1312 km)
Origin - Amarkantak plateau
Longest western flowing river. Flows through MP, Maharashtra, Gujarat and drains into Gulf of Khambat.
It flows in rift valley between Vindhya and Satpura.
Tributaries - Banjar, Tawa, Shakkar, Halon.
Dams - Sardar Sarovar (Gujarat), Indira Sagar (MP), Omkareshwar (MP)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is NOT an important river of Malwa plateau? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Mahanadi river
Mahanadi is not an important river of Malwa plateau. Mahanadi (850 m) originates from Sihawa hills (Chhattisgarh). It is called 'Sorrow of Odisha'.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a tributary of river Kaveri? [SSC CHSL 13/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Musi
Tributaries of Kaveri - Hemvati, Kabini, Bhavani, Shimsha.
Musi is a tributary of Krishna.
Hyderabad is situated on the banks of Musi.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of "river - tributary" is correct? [SSC CHSL 15/03/2023]
I. Tapi - Manjra
II. Krishna - Ghatprabha
solution:
(B) Only II
Manjra is a tributary of Godavari (not Tapi) while Ghatprabha is a tributary of Krishna.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which among the following is a river that originates in the Western Ghats? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Mandovi
Mandovi originates from Western Ghats. It is called 'lifeline of Goa'.
Panaji is located on its bank.
Do you know that Mormugao port is located on the estuary of Zuari river?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Tapi river basin extends over the state of _______. [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Maharashtra
See solution of question 14 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The decommissioned Havelock Bridge has been built over which of the following rivers? [SSC CPO 09/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Godavari
The Havelock bridge is built over Godavari river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which river basin covers an area of 65,145 km², of which about 80% lies in Maharashtra? [SSC CHSL 27/05/2022]
solution:
(C) Godavari
Godavari river basin covers an area of 65,145 km of which about 80% lies in Maharashtra.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In ancient times, which river was also known as "Pooni" in Tamil, which is the fourth largest river flowing in the southeast direction through the states of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu? [SSC CHSL 02/06/2022]
solution:
(A) Kaveri River
Kaveri was known as 'Pooni' in Tamil, which is the fourth largest river flowing in the southeast direction through the states of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. The Grand Anicut dam was built across Kaveri river by the Chola king Karalkala chola.
Ancient name of Satluj - Shutudri. Ancient name of Ravi - Parushni.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following rivers flows into the Bay of Bengal? [SSC MTS 07/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Mahanadi
Mahanadi flows into Bay of Bengal while Narmada, Tapi and Indus flow into Arabian sea.
Recall all the west and east flowing rivers.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: About 50% of the _______ Basin lies in Maharashtra. [SSC MTS 07/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Godavari
About 50% of Godavari river lies in Maharashtra.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which Indian state is the source of the rivers Brahmani, Kharkai, and Subarnarekha? [SSC MTS 26/10/2021]
solution:
(C) Jharkhand
Rivers like Brahmani, Kharkai and Subarnarekha have their origin in Jharkhand.
Subarnarekha river - gold particles are seen in it.
Kharkal is the largest tributary of Subarnarekha.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following states has the largest catchment area of Godavari Basin? [SSC MTS 20/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Maharashtra
Maharashtra has the largest catchment area of Godavari.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the name of the river formed by the confluence of Sankh River and South Koel River at Vedvyas in Odisha? [SSC CGL 16/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Brahmani
Brahmani is formed by the confluence of Sankh and South Koel rivers.
Rihand and North Koel are the major tributaries of Son river which itself is a tributary of Ganga.
Sabarmati - origin - Aravalli mountains (Udaipur, Rajasthan). Flows through - Rajasthan, Gujarat.
Gandhinagar and Ahmedabad are located on the banks of Sabarmati river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following is/are east flowing rivers in India?
1. Palar
2. Periyar
3. Pennar
4. Kalinadi
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(B) 1 and 3
Palar and Pennar are east flowing rivers while Periyar and Kalinadi are west flowing rivers.
Periyar/Mullaperiyar/Idukki dam - across Periyar river (lifeline of Kerala)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following tributaries is/are correctly matched with the Deccan Rivers? [NDA II 2024]

| Tributary | River |
|---|---|
| 1. Bhavani | Krishna |
| 2. Kabini | Kaveri |
| 3. Manjra | Godavari |
| 4. Bhima | Mahanadi |

Select the answer using the code given below:
solution:
(B) 2 and 3

| Tributary | River | Tributary | River |
|---|---|---|---|
| Bhavani | Kaveri | Kabini | Kaveri |
| Manjra | Godavari | Bhima | Krishna |

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following is a tributary of Krishna River? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(D) Munneru
Munneru is a tributary of Krishna.
Tributaries - Bhima, Tungabhadra, Ghatprabha, Malprabha, Musi, Koyna, Dhoodganga (TRICK - भीम तू मौसी को घाट दूध की माला)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following rivers:
1. Brahmani
2. Nagavali
3. Subarnarekha
4. Vamsadhara
Which of the above rise from the Eastern Ghats? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(B) 2 and 4
Nagavali and Vamsadhara rise from the Eastern Ghats.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Gandikota canyon of South India was created by which one of the following rivers? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(C) Pennar
Gandikota canyon (the grand canyon of India) was created by Pennar river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is the multi-purpose rock and earth-fill embankment dam on the Bhagirathi River? [SSC CGL 01/08/2022]
solution:
(D) Tehri Dam
Tehri dam is the multi-purpose rock and earth-fill embankment dam on the Bhagirathi river in Uttarakhand. It is the highest dam of India (261 m).
Sunderlal Bahuguna led the Chipko movement (anti-tehri movement) against deforestation.
Koyna dam - on Koyna river (tributary of Krishna), largest dam of Maharashtra. Gandhisagar dam - on Chambal river. Thein dam (Ranjit Sagar dam) - on Ravi river, in Punjab.
Dams in Punjab - Shahpur Kandi dam (Beas), Harike dam (confluence of Sutlej and Beas, Indira Gandhi canal from this dam).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: On which of the following rivers is Pakistan constructing a dam named 'Diamer Basha' dam? [SSC MTS 12/10/2021]
solution:
(A) Indus
Pakistan is constructing Diamer Basha dam on Indus river.
Indus flows between Ladakh and Zanskar range. Indus enters India through Umling La pass (highest motorable pass) and skirts along the Nanga Parbat massif before entering Pakistan.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which type of dam is a temporary dam constructed to exclude water from the specific area? [SSC CHSL 19/04/2021]
solution:
(A) Coffer
Coffer Dam - It is a temporary dam constructed to exclude water from a specific area.
Impounding Dam - It is a barrier built across a river or stream to hold back water, creating a reservoir or impoundment for various purposes like water storage, power generation, flood control, and irrigation.
Detention Dam - It is a dam that controls the flow of water in a channel or stream to reduce flooding.
Debris Dam - It is a dam designed to collect and trap sediments, debris and other materials that could otherwise flow downstream and can cause blockages.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Hirakud Dam is built near the city of _______ in Odisha. [SSC CGL 20/04/2022]
solution:
(C) Sambalpur
Hirakud dam is built near Sambalpur in Odisha. It is the longest dam of the world/India (4.8 km/25.8 km) built on Mahanadi river.
Tallest dam - Tehri dam (260.5m), Highest gravity dam - Bhakra Nangal dam (height - 225m), Oldest dam - Grand Anicut (Kallanai) dam (built by Karalkal Chola).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Kadana Dam of Gujarat is built over which of the following rivers? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Mahi
Dams in Gujarat
Kandana dam - Mahi river, Ukai dam - Tapi river, Kakrapar dam - Tapi river, Sardar Sarovar dam - Narmada river.
Do you know that Mahi river cuts Tropic of Cancer twice?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statements is correct regarding large dams? [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
I. They have social problems because they displace a large number of peasants and tribals without adequate compensation or rehabilitation.
II. They have environmental problems because they contribute enormously to deforestation and the loss of biological diversity.
solution:
(B) Both I and II
Drawbacks of large dams:
They displace large number of peasants and tribals.
They contribute to deforestation and biodiversity loss.
Loss of ecosystem.
Disturbance to ground water table.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Almatti Dam of Karnataka is built over which of the following rivers? [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Krishna
Dams in Karnataka :
Almatti dam - Krishna river, Jog/Mahatma Gandhi dam - Sharavati river, Linganamakki dam - Sharavati river, Shivsamudram dam - Kaveri river, Krishnaraja Sagar dam - Kaveri river.
Bhakra Nangal dam - built on Satluj, Bhakra : Himachal Pradesh and Nangal : Punjab.
Do you know that Jog falls (2nd highest waterfall in India) is located on the Sharavati river in Karnataka?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following districts is Baira Siul Dam located? [SSC CPO 18/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Chamba
Dams of Himachal Pradesh :
Baira Siul dam (Chamba district) - Ravi river, Pong dam (Maharana Pratap Sagar) - Beas river, Chamera dam - Ravi river, Nathpa Jhakri dam - Satluj river, Bassi dam - Ravi river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: On which river in India was the Somasila Dam built? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Pennar river
Dams of Andhra Pradesh :
Somasila dam (Nellore district) - Pennar river, Srisailam dam - Krishna river, Nagarjuna Sagar dam - Krishna river.
Dams of Telangana :
Pochampad/Sriram Sagar/ Kaleshwaram life irrigation project - on Godavari river, Nizam Sagar dam - Majira river, Nagarjuna Sagar dam - Krishna river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which state of India had the highest number of large dams as of January 2021? [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Maharashtra
Maharashtra (2354) has the largest number of dams in India, followed by MP (906).
Dams in Maharashtra - Jayakwadi dam (Godavari), Dhom dam (Krishna), Isapur dam (Penganga), Ujjaini dam (Krishna), Koyna dam (Koyna).
Koyna is a tributary of Krishna river.
Origin : Godavari - Trimbakeshwar plateau (Nashik), Krishna - Mahabaleshwar

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Identify the dam that is located in South India. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Mettur dam
Mettur dam - on Kaveri river in Tamil Nadu
Gandhi Sagar dam - on Chambal river in MP
Tilaiya dam - on Damodar river in Jharkhand
Rihand dam - on Rihand river (tributary of Son) in UP {other dams of UP - Matatila dam, Lakshmibai dam; both on Betwa river}
Do you know that Govind Ballabh Pant Sagar reservoir is the largest artificial lake of India and it is formed due to construction of Rihand dam?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: _______ are NOT considered surface water. [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Aquifers
Aquifer is an underground layer of rock that holds groundwater. Hence it isn't considered surface water.
Karst aquifers - underground water system that develops within soluble rocks like limestone or dolomite.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which state is Gobind Sagar (Bhakra Nangal Project) located? [SSC MTS 04/09/2023]
solution:
(A) Himachal Pradesh
Bhakra Nangal dam :
It is located in the Bilaspur district of Himachal Pradesh. Constructed during 1st Five Year Plan on Satluj river. It is the highest gravity dam (bears forces on its own). It is also the largest dam of India in terms of area. Consists of two dams : Bhakra - in Himachal Pradesh (Gobind Sagar lake from Bhakra Nangal dam) and Nangal - Punjab.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following lakes is the longest lake in India? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Vembanad lake
Lakes of Kerala :
Vembanad lake - longest lake of India, Pamba river falls in it.
Sasthamcotta lake, Ashtamudi lake
Dams in Kerala - Periyar / Mullaperiyar / Idukki dam on Periyar river (lifeline of Kerala).
Do you know that Mullaperiyar dam is located in Kerala but is managed by Tamil Nadu under the 1886 agreement by the British Raj?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which state of India is Tawa dam located? [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Madhya Pradesh
Dams in MP :
Tawa dam - Tawa river (tributary of Narmada), Ban Sagar dam - Son river (origin - Amarkantak plateau), Omkareshwar dam - Narmada river, Indira Sagar dam - Narmada river (origin - Amarkantak plateau), Gandhi Sagar dam - Chambal river.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Where is the Baglihar Dam located? [SSC CHSL 26/10/2020]
solution:
(C) Jammu & Kashmir
Dams in J & K :
Baglihar dam - Chenab river (largest tributary of Indus), Dulhasti hydroelectric project - Chenab river, Salal hydroelectric project - Chenab river, Kishanganga dam - Jhelum river, Tulbul dam - Jhelum river, Uri dam - Jhelum river.
Do you know that Chenab rail bridge (highest railway bridge, 359 m high) is constructed on Chenab river near Salal hydroelectric project?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following barrages / dams is closest to the India-Bangladesh border? [SSC CPO 23/11/2020]
solution:
(B) Farakka

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Epilimnetic, metalimnetic, and hypolimnetic water strata are example of: [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]

options.d: Typical thermal stratification of a lake

solution:
(D) Typical thermal stratification of a lake
Epilimnetic, metalimnetic and hypolimnetic water strata are examples of thermal stratification of lake where these layers are formed due to variation in temperature differences and density variation in lake.
Epilimnetic layer - upper, less dense, warmer layer.
Metalimnetic layer - middle layer, temperature begins to decline.
Hypolimnetic layer - lower layer, denser layer, low light level (prevents the growth of aquatic plants)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following soil formation process takes place in Phumdis (floating fields) in Loktak Lake of Manipur? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(C) Gleization
Gleization - it is the soil formation process that occurs in waterlogged or poorly drained environments.
Laterization - it is a process by which soil gets depleted of soluble substances (such as silica, alkaline components) and becomes rich in insoluble substances (such as iron and aluminium). It takes place in humid tropical climates.
Podzolization - in cold humid regions, soil is formed through the process of accumulation of dissolved organic matter, iron and aluminium ions in the lower horizons of soil.
Calcification - process in which calcium carbonate accumulates in the lower horizons of soil.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following states of India receives initial rainfall by the Arabian Sea branch of monsoon? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Kerala
Monsoon - seasonal reversal of winds. Winds come from southwest direction (high pressure region) to the north (near Tibetan plateau - low pressure region). The Southwest monsoon reaches India in two branches :- Arabian sea branch and Bay of Bengal branch.
Kerala receives initial rainfall by the Arabian sea branch of monsoon in 1st week of June.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The north-west region of India receives rainfall in winter due to: [SSC PHASE XII 21/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Western disturbances
The north-west region of India receives rainfall in winter due to Western disturbances.
Western disturbances (Upper atmospheric circulation) - cyclonic disturbances rising in Mediterranean sea bringing winter rainfall in India (beneficial for Rabi crops). These are brought in India by Jetstreams and cause rainfall in Punjab, Haryana, Western UP, J&K, Himachal Pradesh. These are known as Mahavat in Rajasthan.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: According to Koeppen's Scheme of classification of climate, which of the following characteristics is associated with 'Group E'? [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]

options.a: The average temperature for all months is below 10°C.
options.b: The average temperature of the coldest month is 18°C or higher.
options.c: The average temperature of the coldest month is -3°C or below.
options.d: The average temperature of the coldest month is higher than -3°C but below 18°C.

solution:
(A) The average temperature for all months is below 10°C
Koppen Climatic Classification (1918)
Empirical climatic classification, 5 major climatic groups:
A (Tropical) - average temperature of the coldest month is 18°C or higher.
B (Dry climate) - two types - S (Steppe) and W (Desert), only classification not based on temperature, on the basis of precipitation, potential evaporation exceeds precipitation.
C (Warm temperate) - average temperature of the coldest month is higher than -3°C but below 18°C.
D (Cold snow forest) - average temperature of the coldest month is -3°C or below.
E (Polar type/Tundra) - average temperature for all months is below 10°C.
W - winter dry, s - summer dry, m - monsoon, f - no dry season (full baarish).

3 types under Tropical Humid Climate : Tropical Wet - Af (no dry season), Tropical Monsoon - Am (monsoonal, short dry season), Tropical Wet and Dry - Aw (winter dry season).
Aw is a climate mainly found in Peninsular India.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following codes signify the Cold Climate with dry Winters according to Köppen? [SSC CPO 09/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Dw
Other types under the D (cold snow forest climates) are Df (humid continental) and Dw (subarctic).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: According to the Köppen climate classification system, 'Am' is the code for which type of climate? [SSC PHASE XII 20/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Tropical monsoon climate
See solution of question 9 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Mango shower is a term used for _______. [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Pre-monsoon showers
Pre-monsoon Showers
Mango showers — seen in Kerala, helps in early ripening of mangoes.
Nor-westers — Kal Baisakhi - calamity in the month of Baisakh in West Bengal, helpful for growth of jute, tea.
Bardoli Chheerha - in Assam
Blossom showers — seen in Kerala/Karnataka, help in growth/cultivation of coffee.
Do you know that Karnataka is the coffee bowl of India?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which months are known for the season of retreating monsoon in India? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(D) October and November
Retreating monsoon (North-East monsoon) - During the months of October-November, the winds return from Indian peninsula due to creation of low pressure in Indian ocean in response to the southward shift of Sun. The retreating monsoon is marked by clear skies and rise in temperature (october heat). It causes rainfall along the coromandel coast (Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: It is hot outside. It is cloudy and raining most of the time in the month of June. Which season is it? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Monsoon
June marks the beginning of monsoon in India characterised by rains most of the time.
Do you know that Kharif crops which requires substantial amount of water are sown during July (onset of monsoon) and harvested in September-October?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which is the first state to receive rain from the Monsoon in India? [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Kerala
Kerala is the first state to receive rain from monsoon in 1st week of June.
Windward side of mountain receives rainfall while leeward side remains dry. Hence western ghats bring rainfall to the western coast of India (Mumbai, Mahabaleshwar etc.).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Western disturbances are responsible for: [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]

options.a: Extremely chilled weather of north-east India
options.b: Dusty storms in eastern coastal area of India
options.c: Hot winds during summers in northern India
options.d: Rainfall during winters in north-western part of India

solution:
(D) Rainfall during winters in north-western part of India
Western disturbances are cyclonic disturbances originating over Mediterranean sea and are responsible for winter rainfall in north-western part of India (beneficial for Rabi crops).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following phenomena is NOT associated with monsoons in India? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Western disturbance
Western disturbances are not associated with monsoon. It is a winter phenomena. Arabian sea branch, cloudburst and break in rainfall are associated with monsoon.
Cloudburst - sudden and very heavy rainfall in a short period of time.
Break in rainfall - sudden decrease in rainfall after the onset of monsoon.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: El Nino is a climate pattern that describes the unusual warming of surface waters in which ocean? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Pacific Ocean
El Nino - Above average sea temperature in east-central equatorial Pacific ocean (warm phase of ENSO).
Trade winds weaken, allowing warm winds to spread eastwards. It can cause droughts in Asia and Australia and above average rainfall in western coast of North America. Fishing industries will not flourish.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the name of the cold phase of the ENSO climate pattern that describes the unusual cooling of the surface waters of the region? [SSC PHASE XII 25/06/2024]
solution:
(B) La Nina
La Nina - Below average sea temperature in east-central Pacific ocean (cold phase of ENSO).
Trade winds strengthen, pushing warm water westward.
It can cause intense rainfall in Australia and India. Drier conditions in South America. Fishing industries will flourish.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following cities in India faces monsoon rain first? [SSC CHSL 10/01/2024]
solution:
(C) Thiruvananthapuram
Thiruvananthapuram in Kerala faces the monsoon rain first (1st week of June).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Mawsynram receives the highest average annual rainfall in the world because the Monsoon Winds of the Bay of Bengal strike the _______ hill. [SSC CHSL 02/11/2023]
solution:
(B) Khasi
Mawsynram receives the highest average annual rainfall (caused by Bay of Bengal branch) in the world because the moisture laden winds strike the Khasi hills in Meghalaya causing orographic rainfall. It is the wettest place on the Earth.
Windward side (onshore side) - Side of mountain on which winds strike, receives rainfall.
Leeward side (offshore side) - Side of mountain on which winds don't strike, doesn't receive rainfall.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is Kalbaisakhi? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Pre-monsoon showers and thunderstorms
See solution of question 13 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: When was the Indian Meteorological Department, which collects data on temperature, rainfall, wind, etc. and makes weather forecasts, established by the Government of India? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) 1875
The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) was established by the Government of India in 1875 with its headquarters at Mausam Bhawan (New Delhi).
Do you know that the HPC (High Performance Computing) tailored for weather and climate research Arka and Arunika are located in Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology in Pune and the National Center for Medium Range Weather Forecast in Noida?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Choose the INCORRECT statement related to monsoon. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]

options.a: The intensity and position of the high-pressure area, east of Madagascar affects the Indian Monsoon.
options.b: The Southwest Monsoon winds blow over the warm oceans, gather moisture, and bring widespread rainfall over the mainland of India.
options.c: The difference in pressure over Tahiti (Pacific Ocean) and Darwin in northern Australia (Indian Ocean) is computed to predict the intensity of the monsoons.
options.d: The western cyclonic disturbances occur due to withdrawal or the retreat of the monsoon.

solution:
(D) The western cyclonic disturbances occur due to withdrawal or the retreat of the monsoon.
Western cyclonic disturbances are extratropical cyclonic disturbances bringing winter rainfall in India and don't occur due to withdrawal or retreat of monsoon.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT true for Indian monsoons? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]

options.a: Monsoonal rainfall is the lifeline of Indian agriculture.
options.b: Monsoonal rainfall is not affected by the relief features of the country.
options.c: Himalayas play an important role in monsoonal rainfall.
options.d: Monsoonal rainfall is affected by jet streams.

solution:
(B) Monsoonal rainfall is not affected by the relief features of the country.
Monsoonal rainfall is affected by the relief features of the country. Mountains like Western Ghats and Khasi hills cause orographic rainfall while mountains parallel to the monsoonal winds don't cause any rainfall.
Jet streams are fast flowing, narrow air currents in upper atmosphere, flowing from west to east.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Choose the INCORRECT statement out of the following. [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]

options.a: There is an increase in rainfall generally from east to west in the Northern Plains.
options.b: Most parts of the country receive rainfall from June to September.
options.c: The annual precipitation in Meghalaya is over 400 cm.
options.d: Some parts like the Tamil Nadu's coast gets a large portion of its rains during October and November.

solution:
(A) There is an increase in rainfall generally from east to west in the Northern Plains.
On moving from east to west, rainfall decreases as moisture laden winds lose moisture as they move inland.
The annual precipitation in Meghalaya is about 1150 cm.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following locations experiences the lowest amount of rainfall during the monsoon season? [SSC MTS 01/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Leh
Leh experiences the lowest rainfall during monsoon as it is located in arid and high altitude region.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is 'Mahawat' in India? [SSC MTS 06/09/2023]

options.a: Cold waves during the winter season
options.b: Cloud burst during the monsoon season
options.c: Cyclonic rainfall during the winter season
options.d: Hot and dry winds during the summer season

solution:
(C) Cyclonic rainfall during the winter season
See solution of question 3 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the phenomenon called when rain fails to occur for one or more weeks during the south-west monsoon period? [SSC CHSL 14/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Break in the monsoon
Break in the monsoon refers to the sudden decrease in rainfall after the onset of monsoon.
Monsoon depression - it is a low pressure area that forms over Bay of Bengal or Arabian sea during monsoon season and brings heavy rainfall in India when it moves over land.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Where does the Bay of Bengal Monsoon Branch and Arabian Sea Branch merge? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]

options.c: North Western Part of Ganga Plains

solution:
(C) North Western Part of Ganga plains
The Bay of Bengal branch and Arabian sea branch merge at Punjab (Northwestern India).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What would happen if the difference in pressure in Tahiti was negative? [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Below average and late monsoon
Negative pressure difference in Tahiti means high pressure in Darwin and low pressure in Tahiti. It leads to below average and late monsoon in India.
Negative pressure difference between Tahiti and Darwin - El Nino conditions.
Positive pressure difference between Tahiti and Darwin - La Nina conditions.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: ENSO is the phenomenon related to pressure conditions. What is the full form of ENSO? [SSC MTS 12/05/2023]
solution:
(B) El Nino Southern Oscillation
El Nino Southern Oscillation - formation of El Nino is linked with the Pacific ocean circulation pattern known as Southern Oscillation (low pressure over Eastern Pacific (South American coast or Peru coast) and high pressure over Western Pacific (Southern coast of Australia). El Nino and Southern Oscillation are interconnected events and hence called ENSO (El Nino Southern Oscillation).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Seasonal reversal in the wind direction during a year refers to _______. [SSC MTS 17/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Monsoon
Monsoon - derived from the Arabic word 'Mausim' which means seasonal reversal of winds.
Breeze - it refers to a gentle wind.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The summer months experience _______ in the northern part of India. [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Falling air pressure
In summers, air heats up leading to decreased air pressure.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Identify the option that contains the correct sequence of Indian states in terms of decreasing average annual rainfall. [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Assam > Bihar > Rajasthan
Assam - 200 cm, Bihar - 120 cm, Rajasthan - 67.8 cm

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What is the usual term used to describe the climate of India? [SSC MTS 04/09/2023]
solution:
(B) Tropical monsoon
Indian climate is called Tropical monsoon climate as India experiences distinct monsoon season and it is located in the tropical zone.
Weather - temporary conditions of atmosphere, including temperature, atmospheric pressure, wind, humidity, precipitation and cloud cover.
Climate - average weather in a given area over a longer period of time (30 years).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The climate of Assam can be described as: [SSC CPO 25/11/2020]
solution:
(D) Tropical monsoon rainforest climate
Assam experiences a tropical monsoon rainforest climate characterised by high humidity, heavy rainfall, warm summers, and mild winters.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following States receives rainfall from both the Arabian Sea branch and the Bay of Bengal branch of Monsoon? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) Punjab
Punjab receives rainfall from both the Arabian sea branch and the Bay of Bengal branch as both the branches meet here.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following climatic types (Köppen's Scheme) is best suited to describe the climate of the western fringe of Rajasthan? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(C) BWhw
According to Koppen's classification, the climate of western fringe of Rajasthan is named BWhw.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which one of the following is not a resultant of the El Nino effect? [CDS I 2022]

Effects of El Nino :-
1. Distortion of equatorial atmospheric circulation.
2. Flow of the South-East Trade Winds towards the Indian Ocean.
3. Irregularities in the evaporation of seawater.
4. Reduction in the amount of planktons which reduces the number of fish in the sea.
solution:
(B) Flow of the South-East Trade Winds towards the Indian Ocean.
El Nino - Above average sea temperature in east-central equatorial Pacific ocean (warm phase of ENSO).

Effects of El Nino :-
Distortion of equatorial atmospheric circulation.
Flow of South East trade winds eastwards.
Irregularities in the evaporation of seawater.
Reduction in the amount of planktons which reduces the number of fish in the sea.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What does BW stand for as per the Koeppen climate classification? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(B) Desert climate
BW stands for desert climate characterised by arid conditions and very low rainfall.
BS (Steppe climate) - semi arid, low precipitation, vegetation dominated by grasslands and shrubs.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The jet streams are high altitude easterly winds.
2. El Nino is a warm ocean current.
3. El Nino appears along the Peru coast.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(B) 2 and 3 only
The jet streams are high altitude westerly winds (moving from west to east).
El Nino is a warm ocean current. El Nino appears along the Peru coast. It appears with a gap of 4-5 years.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following crops is generally benefited by the rain caused by western disturbances in India? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(B) Wheat
Western disturbances are beneficial for Rabi crops (wheat, barley, mustard, peas, gram etc.)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: According to Koppen's climatic classifications, the Great Northern Plains of India have which one of the following climates? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(B) Cwg climate
According to Koppen's classification, the Great Northern plains of India have Cwg climate (Warm temperate, winter dry, Ganges type).
g- Ganges type of climate.
Recall about other Koppen classification.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following climatic regions of the world have a typical characteristic of seasonal reversal of wind? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(D) Monsoon Climate
Monsoon climate is characterised by seasonal reversal of winds.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which statement is NOT correct with reference to Andaman and Nicobar Islands? [NDA II 2024]

options.a: Climate is humid, tropical coastal climate.
options.b: Maximum rainfall occurs between March and April.
options.c: Andaman and Nicobar islands are separated by '10 degree Channel' which is 150 Km wide.
options.d: Islands are inhabited by Negrito tribes.

solution:
(B) Maximum rainfall occurs between March and April
Maximum rainfall in Andaman & Nicobar islands occurs between July and August. Rainfall is caused by Bay of Bengal branch.
Islands are inhabited by Negrito tribes (Great Andamanese, Onge, Jarawa, and Sentinelese).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists: [CBI 2023]
List-I (Koppen's type of climate) - List-II (Area of occurrence)
a. Amw - 1. Coromandel Coast
b. Cwg - 2. West Coast of India
c. As - 3. West Rajasthan
d. BWhw - 4. Ganga Plain
solution:
(C) a-2, b-4, c-1, d-3

| Koppen's type of climate | Area of occurrence |
|---|---|
| Amw | West Coast of India |
| Cwg | Ganga Plain |
| As | Coromandel Coast |
| BWhw | West Rajasthan |

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one among the following is not an area of Marine West Coast Climate? [CISF AC 2021]
solution:
(D) North-West Africa
Marine West Coast Climate is located polewards from the Mediterranean climate on the west coast of continents. The main areas are : North-western Europe, west coast of North America, north of California, southern Chile, South-eastern Australia and New Zealand.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statements about breaks in the Southwest Monsoon is/are correct?
1. In Northern India, rains are likely to fail if the rain-bearing storms are not very frequent along the monsoon trough.
2. In the West Coast, the dry spells occur when winds blow parallel to the coast.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [GEOSCIENTIST 2021]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
In Northern India, rains are likely to fail if the rain-bearing storms are not very frequent along the monsoon trough.
In the West Coast, the dry spells occur when winds blow parallel to the coast rather than perpendicular to it.
Monsoon winds move parallel to Aravallis and hence no rainfall takes place there.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following description:
1. Annual and daily range of temperatures is low.
2. Precipitation occurs throughout the year.
3. Precipitation varies between 50 cm - 250 cm.
What is this type of climate? [IAS 2020]
solution:
(D) Marine West coast climate
Characteristics of Marine West coast climate -
Low annual and daily range of temperatures.
Precipitation throughout the year.
Precipitation varies between 50 cm - 250 cm.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Identify the climate type based on the given characteristics:
1. High temperature year-round.
2. Variable rainfall with a short wet season and longer dry season.
3. Example: Sudan and Paraguay. [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(D) Tropical wet and dry climate
The given characteristics are true about Tropical wet and dry climate, also known as Tropical Savannah climate.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Humid Subtropical Climate is NOT experienced in which among the following regions? [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(D) South Argentina
Humid Subtropical climate is characterised by hot and humid summers, and mild to cool winters. It is experienced in regions like Coastal South Africa, East coast of Australia, South Japan etc.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following regions is the tropical evergreen forest found in India? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Western Ghats
Tropical Evergreen Forests
Layered forests, appear green all year because trees in these forests don't shed their leaves at the same time.
Seen in places of high temperature (> 22°C) and high rainfall (200cm). In India seen in Western Ghats, Andaman & Nicobar islands, NE India, Tamil Nadu coast.
Highest biodiversity is seen here. Vegetation - Ebony (epiphytes), Mahogany, Rosewood, Rubber, Cinchona etc.
Do you know that soil of tropical evergreen forests is deficient in nutrients as all the nutrients get washed away by heavy rainfall. So this region is also called 'desert covered by trees'?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following parts of India are thorn forests and scrub forests found? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(D) North-western India
Thorn Forests
Found in region with rainfall less than 70 cm. Leaves modify to form thorns.
Vegetation - Acacias, Palms, Euphorbias, Cacti.
Seen in NW region (Gujarat, Rajasthan, MP, Chhattisgarh, UP, Haryana).
Special type of grass seen here - Tussocky grass (height upto 2m)

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Sundari trees are found in which type of forests in India? [SSC CGL 04/06/2019]
solution:
(D) Mangrove forests
Mangrove Forests
Also known as Littoral/Swamp forests. Trees are viviparous (seeds germinate and grow while being attached to their parents) and their roots are called Pneumatophores (living roots).
Seen in coastal areas that are highly influenced by tides. Eg. deltas of Ganga, Mahanadi, Krishna, Godavari, Kaveri.
More in Sundarban delta (West Bengal) – Sundari trees are found here.
Do you know that the name 'sundarban' came up due to presence of 'sundari' trees?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following states has mangrove forests? [SSC CHSL 05/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Andhra Pradesh
Mangrove covers 0.15% of India's geographical area.
States having mangrove forests - West Bengal (42.45%) > Gujarat > Andaman & Nicobar islands > Andhra Pradesh > Maharashtra > Odisha.
Forests that can survive in saline water - Halophytes.
MISHTI (Mangrove Initiative for Shoreline Habitats and Tangible Incomes) - to increase mangrove cover in India, launched in 2023, till 2028.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Mangrove forests of West Bengal are also called: [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Sunderbans
Mangroves mainly grow in tropical and subtropical latitudes. The mangrove forests of West Bengal are called Sunderbans due to Sundari trees which are found there. Trees growing in mangrove ecosystem - Garan, Byne, Ganwea.
Sholas - tropical montane forests found in Western Ghats.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is the most dominant species of the Tropical Deciduous Forests? [SSC CHSL 21/10/2020]
solution:
(A) Teak
Teak is the most dominant species of the Tropical Deciduous forests.
Deciduous Forests
Also called Monsoon forests. Found in areas having rainfall between 70cm and 200cm. Most widespread forests of India.
Two types - Moist deciduous (100-200 cm) - Sal, Amla, Shisham, Mahua, Sandalwood, Khair, Mulberry, Kusum Dry deciduous (70-100 cm) - Tendu, Palas, Amaltas, Kair, Bel, Axelwood

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT true about the moist deciduous forests of India? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]

options.a: They grow in regions with rainfall of 100-200 cm.
options.b: They are found in the foothills of Himalayas.
options.c: They are found in the plains of Bihar and Uttar Pradesh.
options.d: Sal, amla, shisham and mahua are some of the main species of these forests.

solution:
(C) They are found in the plains of Bihar and Uttar Pradesh
Moist Deciduous Forests
Found in areas with rainfall 100-200 cm. Found in the foothills of Himalayas.
Eg. Sal, Amla, Shisham, Mahua, Sandalwood, Khair, Mulberry, Kusum, Arjun etc.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Under which strata do trees fall? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Top vertical
In forest ecosystem, trees occupy the top vertical, shrubs occupy the middle vertical and herbs and bushes occupy the bottom vertical.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The natural vegetation of India is broadly divided into _______ groups. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(D) 5
The natural vegetation of India can be broadly divided into 5 types - Tropical evergreen, Tropical deciduous, Thorn forests, Mangroves and Montane forests.
Indian State of Forest Report (1st - 1987, 18th - 2023) - forest cover - 21.76%, tree cover - 3.41%, forest+tree - 25.17%.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which type of forests are wild donkeys and camels found in India? [SSC MTS 14/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Thorny forests and bushes
Wild donkeys and camels are found in thorny forests and bushes type of forests.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Sal, acacia, and bamboo, which are types of Dry Tropical Forest, receive what amount of annual rainfall? [SSC CHSL 02/08/2023]
solution:
(A) From 51 cm to 151 cm
Dry tropical forest (dry deciduous forest) is found in the rainier parts of Peninsular plateau and plains of Bihar and UP. Rainfall is between 51 - 151 cm. Eg. Teak, Sal, Peepal, Neem, Tendu, Palas, Amaltas, Bel, Khair, Axlewood etc.
Do you know that Tendu leaves are used for making bidis?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following forest types is found on the foothills of the Himalayas? [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Deciduous
Deciduous forests (monsoon forests) are found in the foothills of Himalayas. Rainfall is between 70 cm and 200 cm. These are the most widespread forests of India. Two types - Moist deciduous (100-200cm) and Dry deciduous (70-100cm). These forests shed their leaves for about 6-8 weeks in dry summer.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is the most widespread forest type in India? [SSC CHSL 17/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Tropical Deciduous Forests
See solution of question 12 (level 1).
Decreasing order of area of forests – Tropical moist deciduous > Tropical dry deciduous > Tropical wet evergreen > Subtropical moist hill > Tropical semi evergreen.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What type of vegetation is found in the plateau of Meghalaya, Sahyadri, and the central and southern islands of the Nicobar group? [SSC CGL 20/07/2023]
solution:
(D) Moist Tropical Semi-Evergreen Vegetation
Meghalaya plateau, Sahyadri and the central and southern islands of the Nicobar group have moist tropical semi-evergreen vegetation.
Mawsynram (wettest place on Earth) is on the Khasi hills of Karbi Anglong plateau.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Plant varieties like bamboo, epiphytes, aini, semul, gutel and mundane are found in which type of Indian forest? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Moist tropical forest
Plant varieties like bamboo, epiphytes, aini, semul, gutel and mundane are found in moist tropical forest which is characterised by high rainfall and high humidity.
Alpine forest is a part of Montane forest.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following states is associated with the tropical thorn forests? [SSC CGL 30/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Madhya Pradesh
See solution of question 2 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following characteristics is INCORRECT about the tropical evergreen forests of India? [SSC CHSL 27/06/2024]

options.b: In these forests, trees reach great heights of up to 60 m or above.
options.c: They are found in warm and humid areas.
options.d: These forests receive annual precipitation of below 150 cm.

solution:
(D) These forests receive annual precipitation of below 150 cm.
See solution of question 1 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Mosses and lichens are found in which kind of forest? [SSC MTS 03/05/2023]
solution:
(D) Tundra Forest
Mosses and Lichens are found in Tundra forest which is found in polar regions. Tundra forest is located on the apex of montane forest.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Major plantations are found in the _______ region of the world. [SSC MTS 10/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Tropical
Major plantations are found in tropical regions of the world where high temperature and high rainfall supports the growth of biodiversity.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is an example of a terrestrial ecosystem? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Grasslands
Ecosystem is a community where living things interact with non-living things in a particular area.
Tropical Grasslands - tall, non-nutritious, elephant grass. Eg. Savannah, Campos, Llanos etc.
Temperate Grasslands - short and nutritious grass. Eg. Prairies, Steppes, Velds, Downs etc.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In the higher latitudes (50° - 70°) of the Northern Hemisphere, the spectacular Coniferous forests are found. These are also called _______. [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Taiga
Coniferous forests / Taiga
Seen in areas of high snowfall. In India, seen in upper Himalayas (3000 - 4000 m). It is followed by wet temperate and above it has tundra forest.
Trees - Chir, Pine, Cedar, Deodar, Spruce.
Difference between Tundra and Taiga - Tundra is treeless (only mosses and lichens present) and is found in polar regions. Taiga on the other hand refers to coniferous forests.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In India, palm, coconut, keora, agar are the common trees of which of the following forests? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Mangrove Forests
In India, palm, coconut, keora, agar are the types of mangrove forests which are found along the coastal areas of river delta.
Evergreen Forests -dense, multi layered, found in areas with high rainfall. Eg. Teak, Rosewood, Jamun.
Semi-evergreen Forests - transitional, found in areas with less rainfall, a mix of evergreen and deciduous trees. Eg. Ebony, Jackfruit, Cullenia.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In India, trees of which forest type shed their leaves for about six to eight weeks in dry summer? [SSC CHSL 10/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Tropical Deciduous Forests
See solution of question 12 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The wet temperate type of forests are found between a height of _______ metres. [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(C) 1000 and 2000
Succession of Montane forests with altitude
Foothills of Himalayas - Deciduous forests
1000-2000 m - Wet temperate forests, evergreen broad leaf tress (oaks, chestnuts)
1500-3000 m - Temperate forests containing coniferous trees (pine, deodar, silver fir, spruce, cedar)
Above 3600 m – Temperate forests and grasslands (alpine vegetation consisting of silver fir, junipers, pines, rhododendrons, birches)
At higher altitudes - mosses and lichens (tundra vegetation)
Do you know that montane forests are extensively used for grazing by nomadic tribes like Gujjars and Bakarwals?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: With which neighbouring country does India share the Sunderbans forest? [SSC MTS 15/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Bangladesh
India shares the Sunderbans forest with Bangladesh. Sunderbans are the largest mangrove forest of the world.
Sunderbans delta - largest delta in the world, formed by Ganga and Brahmaputra.
Recall all about mangrove forests.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Thorny bushes are found in _______ region. [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Dry Desert
Thorny bushes are found in dry desert region with rainfall less than 50 cm.
Koppen classification of Mediterranean - Cs
Recall every other Koppen classification.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The type of forests located in mid latitudinal coastal region is _______. [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]
solution:
(D) Temperate Evergreen Forest
Temperate evergreen forests are located in the mid latituding coastal region which is characterised by warm summers and cool winters. These are located along the eastern coast of continents.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The birds like pheasant and monals are found in which type of forest? [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Temperate Deciduous Forest
The birds like pheasant and monals are found in temperate deciduous forest.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is a tree found in Mountain vegetation? [SSC CGL 07/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Chir
Vegetation in montane forests - Oaks, Chestnuts, Pine, Deodar, Silver fir, Spruce, Cedar, Chir, Junipers etc.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which biome is found in the northern parts of Asia, Europe, and North America where 300 to 900 millimeters (12 to 35 in) of rain can be expected per year? [SSC CGL 02/08/2022]
solution:
(C) Coniferous Forest
Coniferous forest (taiga/boreal forest) is found in the northern parts of Asia, Europe and North America where 300-900 mm of rain takes place per year.
Biome - large geographical area on Earth with similar climate, plants and animals. Eg. Desert biome, Grassland biome, Tundra biome, Forest biome.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Dry deciduous forests are found in areas having rainfall between _______ [SSC MTS 22/07/2022]
solution:
(B) 100 cm and 70 cm
Dry deciduous forests are found in areas having rainfall between 70 cm and 100 cm.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: What type of forests are found north and south of the equator between 40° and 60° where rainfall is high, between 500-1500 mm in a year? [SSC CHSL 25/05/2022]
solution:
(D) Temperate Deciduous Forests
Temperate deciduous forests are found north and south of the equator between 40° and 60° where rainfall is high, between 500-1500 mm per year. Trees - Oak, Maple, Beech, Hickory and Chestnut etc.
Taiga forests - Seen in areas of high snowfall. In India, seen in upper Himalayas. Trees - Chir, Pine, Cedar, Deodar etc.
Mediterranean forests - also known as 'Orchards of the World' due to abundance of fruit-producing trees. Trees - oaks, pines, rosemary, lavendar etc.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Sandalwood is found in which type of forests in India? [SSC CHSL 30/05/2022]
solution:
(B) Tropical Deciduous Forests
Sandalwood is found in tropical deciduous forests while rosewood is found in tropical evergreen forests.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following statements about forests is INCORRECT? [SSC MTS 06/10/2021]

options.a: In a forest, trees form the uppermost layer of vegetation.
options.b: Forests protect the soil from erosion.
options.c: In a forest, shrubs form the lowest layer of vegetation.
options.d: Forests influence climate, water cycle, and air quality.

solution:
(C) In a forest, shrubs form the lowest layer of vegetation.
In a forest, trees form the uppermost layer, shrubs form the middle layer and bushes and herbs form the lowest layer of vegetation. Forests protect the soil form erosion as the roots tightly hold the soil. Forests also influence climate, water cycle and air quality. Recall about the strata classification of forests.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following is an example of a terrestrial ecosystem? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Grasslands
See solution of question 20 (level 1).
Terai grasslands - Gangetic and Brahmaputra floodplains, Bugyal grasslands - Uttarakhand, Shola grasslands - Western Ghats, Banni grasslands - Gujarat.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of countries - name of grassland is correct? [SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]
I. Argentina - Pampas
II. Brazil - Steppe
solution:
(A) Only 1
Tropical Grassland
Savannah (Africa), Campos (Brazil), Llanos (Venezuela)
Temperate Grassland
Prairies (North America), Steppes (Asia/Europe), Velds (South Africa), Downs (Australia), Pustaz (Hungary), Canterbury (New Zealand), Pampas (Argentina, alpha alpha grass).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The climatic conditions of thorn forests and scrubs are the most ideal for which of the following plant species? [SSC MTS 05/07/2022]
solution:
(D) Euphorbia
Thorn Forests
Found in region with rainfall less than 70 cm. Leaves modify to form leaves.
Vegetation - Acacias, Palms, Euphorbias, Cacti.
Seen in NW region (Gujarat, Rajasthan, MP, Chhattisgarh, UP, Haryana).
Special type of grass seen here - Tussocky grass (height upto 2m)
Do you know that Prairies are known as the 'wheat granaries of the world'?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Tropical grasslands of Brazil are known as _______ [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(B) Campos
See solution of question 36 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Temperate grasslands are ideal for _______ cultivation. [SSC MTS 11/07/2022]
solution:
(D) Wheat
Temperate grasslands are ideal for wheat cultivation. Prairies - wheat granaries of the world.
Recall about the different names of grasslands.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which types of vegetation will you find on the way as you travel from Bikaner to Konark by road by shortest distance? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: Tropical thorny, Desert, Moist deciduous, Mangrove
options.b: Desert, Tropical thorny, Moist deciduous, Mangrove
options.c: Moist deciduous, Tropical thorny, Mangrove, Desert
options.d: Desert, Tropical thorny, Mangrove, Moist deciduous

solution:
(B) Desert, Tropical thorny, Moist deciduous, Mangrove
Bikaner lies in western Rajasthan and hence supports desert vegetation. Eastern Rajasthan has thorny vegetation while MP and Chhattisgarh have moist deciduous forests. Konark lies on the eastern coast of Odisha and has mangrove forests.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which one of the following highlands of India can you find temperate forests called 'Sholas'? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(A) Nilgiris
Sholas are the temperate grasslands/forests in Nilgiris (Western Ghats), Anaimalai and Palani hills.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which one of the following set of trees does not belong to the Temperate Floral Zone of Himalaya? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(A) Cashewnut and Magnolia
Cashewnuts are native to tropical regions, not temperate regions. Magnolia is found in temperate regions but is not exclusive to Himalayas.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statements about tropical rain forests are correct?
1. The soils of tropical rain forests are quite infertile.
2. The vegetation is evergreen, enabling photosynthesis to take place year around.
3. They have been described as 'deserts covered by trees'.
4. They are most productive land-based ecosystem.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(D) 1, 2, 3 and 4
The soils of tropical rainforests are infertile as the heavy rainfall causes leaching (washing away) of minerals.
The vegetation of tropical rainforests is evergreen, enabling photosynthesis to take place year round.
Tropical rainforests are called the 'desert covered by trees'. They are the most productive land-based ecosystem.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The greatest diversity of animal and plant species is found in: [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(D) Tropical moist forests
The highest diversity of animal and plant species is found in tropical moist forest.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists: [CAPF 2023]
List-I (Temperate Grassland) - List-II (Region)
a. Prairies - 1. Eurasia
b. Steppes - 2. South Africa
c. Pampas - 3. North America
d. Veldt - 4. South America
solution:
(C) a-3, b-1, c-4, d-2
See solution of question 36 (level 1).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The natural vegetation which covers the maximum geographical areas of India is: [NDA I 2021]
solution:
(A) Tropical deciduous forests
Tropical deciduous forests cover the maximum geographical area of India.
Indian State of Forest Report (ISFR)
1st published in 1987, 18th published in 2023. Biennial report
Forest cover 21.76%, Tree cover - 3.41%, Forest + Tree cover - 25.17%
Forest cover (area wise) - MP > Arunachal Pradesh > Chhattisgarh
Forest cover (% wise) - Lakshadweep > Mizoram > Andaman & Nicobar islands
Maximum increase (forest + tree cover) - Chhattisgarh > UP
Maximum increase (forest cover) - Mizoram > Gujarat
Maximum decrease - MP > Karnataka
Indian Forest Policy 1952 - 33% of geographical area should be under forest or tree cover.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: A person had visited a region in India and found trees, such as Khair, Neem, Khejri and Palas. Which one of the following regions is she/he expected to have visited? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(D) Desert Region
Trees such as Khair, Neem, Khejri, Babool and Palas are found in desert regions.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following tree species is/are found on Himalayas?
1. Oak
2. Rhododendron
3. Rosewood
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2023]
solution:
(D) 1 and 2
Oak and Rhododendron are found in Himalayas (temperate forests) while Rosewood is found in tropical evergreen forests.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Identify the land biome on the basis of the given characteristics:
1. Their climates are characterised by high rainfall and temperatures that vary from cold to mild.
2. These forests contain primarily deciduous trees – including maple, oak, hickory and beechwood.
3. Raccoons, opossums, bats and squirrels are found in the trees.
Select the correct land biome from the options given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(B) Temperate forest
The given characteristics are of temperate forest.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following characteristics of a grassland:
1. An extensive area of mid latitude grasslands are devoid of trees and shrubs.
2. The climate is characterised by hot summers, cold winters and relatively low rainfall occurring mainly in spring and summer.
3. In humid parts, grasses grow to a meter or more in height.
On the basis of the above characteristics, select the correct grassland from the options given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(A) Steppes
Steppes Grasslands
Temperate grasslands of Eurasia.
An extensive area of mid latitude grasslands devoid of trees and shrubs.
Climate is characterised by hot summers, cold winters and relatively low rainfall occurring mainly in spring and summer.
In humid parts, these grasses grow at least a meter or more in height.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Mangroves represent: [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(B) Salt-tolerant vegetation
Mangrove Forests :
Also known as Littoral/Swamp forests. Trees are viviparous and their roots are called Pneumatophores (living roots).
Seen in coastal areas that are highly influenced by tides. Eg. deltas of Ganga, Mahanadi, Krishna, Godavari, Kaveri.
More in Sundarban delta (West Bengal) – Sundari trees are found here.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following pairs of Forest Research Centres and their locations are correctly matched?
1. Forest Research Centre for Eco-Rehabilitation : Prayagraj
2. Forest Research Centre for Livelihood Extension : Shillong
3. Forest Research Centre for Skill Development : Bhopal
4. Forest Research Centre for Coastal Ecosystems : Visakhapatnam
Select the answer using the code given below; [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(B) 1 and 4
Forest Research Centre for Eco-Rehabilitation : Prayagraj
Forest Research Centre for Livelihood Extension : Agartala
Forest Research Centre for Skill Development : Chhindwara
Forest Research Centre for Coastal Ecosystems : Visakhapatnam

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding Tropical Deciduous forests in India is/are correct?
1. They are the most widespread forests in India.
2. They spread over regions which receive annual rainfall between 70 - 200 cm.
Select the answer using the code given below : [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Deciduous Forests :
Also called Monsoon forests. Found in areas having rainfall between 70cm and 200cm. Most widespread forests of India. Two types - Moist deciduous (100-200cm) and Dry deciduous (70-100cm).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I : The soil in tropical rain forests is rich in nutrients.
Statement-II : The high temperature and moisture of tropical rain forests cause dead organic matter in the soil to decompose quickly.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? [IAS 2023]

options.a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I.
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I.
options.c: Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect.
options.d: Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct.

solution:
(D) Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct.
The soil in tropical rainforests is poor in nutrients due to leaching caused by heavy rainfall. The rate of decomposition is high in tropical rainforests due to high temperature and moisture.
Recall the facts about tropical evergreen forests and semi-evergreen forests.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Moringa (drumstick tree) is a leguminous evergreen tree.
2. Tamarind tree is endemic to South Asia.
3. In India, most of the tamarind is collected as minor forest produce.
4. India exports tamarind and seeds of moringa.
5. Seeds of moringa and tamarind can be used in the production of biofuels.
Which of the statements given above are correct? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(B) 3, 4 and 5
Moringa is a deciduous, non-leguminous plant.
Tamarind tree is endemic to Africa.
Tamarind is collected as minor forest produce in India.
India exports tamarind and seeds of moringa.
Seeds of moringa and tamarind can be used in the production of biofuels.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
The vegetation of savannah consists of grassland with scattered small trees, but extensive areas have no trees. The forest development in such areas is generally kept in check by one or more or a combination of some conditions. Which of the following are such conditions?
1. Burrowing animals and termites
2. Fire
3. Grazing herbivores
4. Seasonal rainfall
5. Soil properties
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [IAS 2021]
solution:
(C) 2, 3 and 4
The forest development in Savannah is kept in check by fire, grazing herbivores and seasonal rainfall.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: "Leaf litter decomposes faster than in any other biome and as a result the soil surface is often almost bare. Apart from trees, the vegetation is largely composed of plant forms that reach up into the canopy vicariously, by climbing the trees or growing as epiphytes, rooted on the upper branches of trees." This is the most likely description of [IAS 2021]
solution:
(D) Tropical rainforest
In tropical rainforests, leaf decomposed quickly due to high temperature and high humidity. Vegetation consists of large trees, plants, shrubs, climbers etc.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text:
Consider the following trees:
1. Jackfruit (Artocarpus heterophyllus)
2. Mahua (Madhuca indica)
3. Teak (Tectona grandis)
How many of the above are deciduous trees? [IAS 2023]
solution:
(B) Only two
Mahua and Teak are deciduous trees while jackfruit is an evergreen tree.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following physiographic divisions of India has extensive alluvial soil? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(C) The North Indian Plain
The North Indian plains (area - 7 lakh 2 km) are covered by alluvial soil deposited by rivers like Ganga, Indus and Brahmaputra.
Alluvial soil covers about 40% area of India. It is rich in potash and poor in phosphorus.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: The Arid soils are developed in the western part of which state from the following? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Rajasthan
Arid Soil
Red to brown in colour. Sandy in texture and saline in nature.
Found in western parts of Rajasthan and Gujarat.
Kankar layer (calcium carbonate) formation in bottom horizon restrict the infiltration of water.
Do you know that the saline soil is treated with Gypsum while the acidic soil is treated with Lime?

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which of the following are NOT contents of soil? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Steroids
Steroids are not found in soil. Contents of soil include - minerals, organic matter, water, air, grains of stones etc.
Three main nutrients that plants obtain from soil are Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P) and Potassium (K).

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: Which type of soil is found on about 40% of the total area of India? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Alluvial soil
Alluvial Soil
Found in Northern Plains (UP, Bihar, West Bengal, Assam), deltas of peninsular rivers, in eastern coastal plains.
Rich in potash and poor in phosphorus (agar phosphorus mein rich hoti to sab log matchsticks bna lete so poor in it).
Types :- Old alluvial (Bhangar) - calcareous concentration, poor in humus and nitrogen (less fertile)
New alluvial (Khadar) - more fertile.

subject: Indian Geography & Resources
question_text: In which of the following processes nutrients or contaminants are carried away by water or moved into the lower layer of the soil? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Leaching
